candidate_fixture: Accepted first-wave fixture: tiny public Lake project with four compiled declarations, invalid-proof negative case, Mathlib/Aesop/Batteries forbidden-import rejection, manifest proof-body rejection, private-source-ref rejection, source hashes, body_redacted=true, and authority_ceiling=bounded_public_lean_lake_witness_only.
failure_mode_prevented: A public formal-math flagship remains a prose claim or leaks proof bodies/private sources; a Lean demo implies Mathlib/provider/benchmark/release authority instead of proving a bounded local toy witness with negative-case receipts.
notes: Status promoted from absent to present after observing accepted current-authority public-root organ formal_math_lean_proof_witness. The original operator note remains directionally right; the public-safe realization is a tiny redacted Lean/Lake witness rather than an Erdos257 private proof import.
one_line: The public Microcosm root has an accepted bounded Lean/Lake proof-witness organ: it copies a tiny public Lake project into a temporary workspace, runs lake build, records declaration/source metadata, rejects broken proofs and forbidden imports, and keeps proof bodies out of JSON receipts.
title: Lean self-contained formal-math organ
why_load_bearing: This is still the most attention-grabbing public formal-math move: it turns the ledger from a proposed Lean story into an executable, locally verified witness while preserving the public authority ceiling. The receipt proves only declared toy witnesses under local Lean/Lake, not Mathlib-dependent theorem proving, provider success, benchmark performance, release readiness, or private-root equivalence.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic failure-mode JSONL across 3 toy leaves; an extractor that pulls failure axes; a proposed DAG patch; a re-run over the sample; an adoption-on-probation marker.
notes: Voice memo 2026-05-17: 'CP1, CP2 is not self-refreshing prompts, it's a self-updating DAG with ledgers of local failure modes.'
one_line: The system mints a local failure ledger per leaf, cross-compares failure axes, proposes DAG patches against a sample, validates improvement, and adopts on probation — the 'CP1/CP2 self-updating DAG' frame the operator named.
title: Recursive self-improvement operating loop
why_load_bearing: Without this, the substrate is a frozen demo; with it, the substrate gets *better* under its own gates each pass. This is the load-bearing difference between a 'cool repo' and a 'cognition organ.'

candidate_fixture: Three synthetic pressure events; a candidate queue; a classifier that splits concept/mechanism/principle/axiom; a probation row; an adoption row.
notes: Paired with `recursive_self_improvement_operating_loop` but distinct: this is the *intake* lane; that one is the *gate-and-revise* lane.
one_line: Pressure (raw seed / failure mode / operator correction) becomes a candidate concept/mechanism/principle/axiom, which gets routed through a population queue with classified lanes and only adopted when its scope and proof are explicit.
title: Doctrine population loop
why_load_bearing: Solves the hardest part of any agent system: how does the system change its own rules without rule-drift collapse. The lanes make adoption auditable.

candidate_fixture: Mini-`std_paper_module.json`; two toy paper modules; the builder that emits an index + validation report; an intentionally-broken module that the validator catches.
notes: This is the *meta-doctrine layer*. Putting it in the microcosm would let cold readers see 'doctrine is also code.'
one_line: `std_paper_module_v8` is a self-describing standard that says what a paper module must contain, has a builder that projects an index over 208 authored modules, and emits a validation report — the doctrine layer is itself standards-governed.
title: Paper-module authoring runtime
why_load_bearing: Without this, paper modules drift into prose; with it, the doctrine layer is a typed surface that can be queried, audited, and projected.

candidate_fixture: Mini-`std_widget.json` declaring `owned_bands`; ten toy widget rows; the CLI emitting cluster_flag → flag → card → atom; a deliberate omission receipt.
notes: The current `atlas_navigation_bands` leaf gestures at this but does not show the *standard-owned band* generative mechanism that makes it work at scale.
one_line: The kernel's `--option-surface <kind> --band cluster_flag|flag|card|atom` projection is governed by a standard that names which bands a kind owns, with cluster-first navigation for high-cardinality kinds and an omission receipt for transparency.
title: Standard-owned option surface with bands
why_load_bearing: Architecturally the most novel routing primitive in the macro. Replaces 'grep + open file' with 'cluster-first projection at a standard-declared rung.' This is what makes 208 paper modules tractable.

candidate_fixture: Mini entry packet emitting a `route_lease`; a `banned_routes` list with replacements; `allowed_drilldowns` rules; a ceremony budget tier; a small route-replay receipt.
notes: `self_comprehension_navigator` shows the cold-agent surface but not the hologram beneath it.
one_line: All navigation surfaces (entry packet, kind atlas, option surface, drilldowns, banned routes, allowed drilldowns) compose into one route plane with first-contact rules, banned-route catalog, and ceremony budget.
title: Unified navigation route plane (hologram)
why_load_bearing: Stops agent navigation from being prompt soup; routes are first-class typed objects with provenance and cost.

candidate_fixture: Two synthetic concurrent claims on the same path, one unrelated dirty tree, one staged-index contaminant, one scoped commit through the private index, one WorkItem landing attempt with finalizer, and one replan receipt for the conflicting path.
failure_mode_prevented: Parallel Type A agents treat ambient dirty state as either a total freeze or a license to broad-stage; scoped work lands without claim/lease evidence; same-path collisions escalate instead of replanning; finalizer receipts stay chat-only.
notes: The current `concurrency_mission_control` leaf has the board shape but not the transactional finalizer semantics that distinguish this from a TODO list. Bind this row to mission transaction, Work Ledger, scoped commit, and WorkItem landing evidence before projecting any public concurrency organ.
one_line: An atomic landing transaction spans claim -> lease -> mutation -> validation -> scoped commit or checkpoint lane -> finalizer, with same-path conflict detection and replan-instead-of-escalate semantics.
title: Mission transaction landing with claim/lease/finalizer
why_load_bearing: The thing that makes concurrent agents actually safe to run in parallel is not optimistic locking, eventual consistency, or a TODO board. It is a typed landing transaction that binds claims, leases, dirty-tree classification, validation, scoped commit authority, and finalizer receipts.

candidate_fixture: A synthetic detached job with a continuation packet; a yield; a wake from disk; a controller heartbeat ledger; a finalize.
notes: 11 paper modules in `subdomain_bridge_runtime`; cold-agent jaw-drop potential is high if shown as a working specimen.
one_line: Long-running or detached work yields a continuation packet to disk and re-enters from a durable wake surface, with controller-heartbeat liveness and a transport-adapter boundary.
title: Bridge runtime dispatch-yield-resume
why_load_bearing: Makes 'agent that survives context loss' real instead of metaphorical — durable continuation as a typed contract, not an unreliable retry loop.

candidate_fixture: 5-row registry (compress, classify, route, distill, validate); a tiny invocation receipt for each; an audit of cross-operator composition.
notes: Pairs naturally with `compression_profile_governed_option_surface` — operators ARE the bands' actions.
one_line: A registry of named cognitive primitives (compress, classify, route, project, distill, validate, retire, supersede, …) each typed by a standard and usable as the lingua franca of agent reasoning.
title: Typed cognitive-operator registry
why_load_bearing: Stops every doctrine artifact from inventing its own verbs; cognitive moves become first-class typed operations with provenance.

candidate_fixture: 3 toy external patterns; the classifier routing each into one of the 8 lanes; one rejection receipt with reason; one anti-corruption-adapter row.
notes: 14 paper modules in `subdomain_external_reference_transfer`. Whole organ family currently absent from the public microcosm.
one_line: External patterns enter through an annex import lane with classified routing (pattern_transfer / substrate_wedge / hybrid_wrap / upgrade_ours / anti_corruption_adapter / defer / reject / archive) — foreign semantics never silently colonize the substrate.
title: Annex distillation with anti-corruption boundary
why_load_bearing: This is how the substrate stays coherent while still learning from outside — adopt patterns, reject doctrine drift.

candidate_fixture: SYNTHETIC operator-voice paragraph (not real raw seed); atomization into 4 shards; enrichment tags; one type-discovery row; one routed candidate.
notes: HIGH PRIVATE-STATE RISK: real raw_seed.md must never ship. The public projection needs a fully synthetic operator-voice fixture. 23 skills in `skills/raw_seed/`.
one_line: A two-phase chain that turns operator-voice raw seed into atomized shards, enriches them, routes them to type-discovery campaigns, and feeds the doctrine population loop — voice becomes typed substrate.
title: Raw-seed metabolism: operator voice to doctrine
why_load_bearing: Operator intent is the only true source authority for this system; without an explicit metabolism, voice either gets lost or pollutes doctrine. This is the pipe that solves both.

candidate_fixture: 3 toy session traces; a histogram of tool verbs vs typed routes; a navigation-expectation comparison; one steering hint emission.
notes: `meta_diagnostics_workbench` is a thin slice; the full plane includes session attribution, route-replay results, and bottleneck audits.
one_line: An agent records its own execution trace as typed events (route taken, banned route avoided, kernel call vs direct action, ceremony tier used) and can compare its session against a navigation expectation.
title: Type A agent self-observability plane
why_load_bearing: Lets the system audit agent behavior independently of agent self-report; navigation training-loop hints (like the ones interrupting this very session) come from this plane.

candidate_fixture: 3 toy idea cards; the score → project → sanitize → fixture-build → gate pipeline; one rejection; one acceptance; the resulting tiny leaf scaffold.
notes: Self-reference makes the substrate teach the cold reader its own production rule.
one_line: The macro paper module that names how microcosms themselves are minted, scored, projected, sanitized, fixture-built, and gated — i.e. the operating system *for* microcosms, currently invisible from inside the microcosm.
title: Idea-microcosm metabolism (the meta loop)
why_load_bearing: If the microcosm contains this paper module, the microcosm is *recursively self-describing* — it shows the rule that produced it.

candidate_fixture: 4 principles with scope; 2 axiom-candidates queued under one teleology; 1 refinement-plan row; 1 capability binding; 1 promotion from candidate → axiom.
notes: This is the missing 'roof' of the current microcosm: AXIOMS.md exists but there is no scoped principle/axiom lattice as a runnable organ.
one_line: Principles have explicit scope (where they apply, what they bind, what triggers a refinement) and axiom-candidates queue up under teleologies with refinement plans and capability bindings.
title: Principle scope + axiom candidate lattice
why_load_bearing: Without scope, principles become slogans; without an axiom-candidate lattice, axioms become a frozen list. This makes both growable under discipline.

candidate_fixture: 3 heartbeat rows; one missed-beat detection; one resume-from-disk; one stale-lease cleanup.
notes: Pairs with `bridge_runtime_dispatch_yield_resume`; could be one leaf or two.
one_line: A heartbeat record proves a controller is alive, names its current lease, identifies its host surface, and feeds the resume protocol when the controller cycles.
title: Controller continuity heartbeat
why_load_bearing: Liveness becomes evidence, not assumption; agent-A's death gets detected by agent-B without manual checking.

candidate_fixture: Toy memory directory; toy session scratch; toy doctrine substrate; 4 sample writes routed to the right tier; 2 deliberate rejects (asked-to-remember but excluded by rules).
notes: Differentiates this substrate from every off-the-shelf agent memory layer (vector store + LRU).
one_line: Memory has three discrete planes (auto-memory file system, in-session scratch, doctrine substrate) with explicit rules about what flows where and what NOT to save — durability semantics, not 'remember everything.'
title: Claude memory plane three-tier contract
why_load_bearing: Without typed memory tiers, agent memory either bloats into noise or collapses into amnesia. The three-tier discipline gives memory a write contract.

candidate_fixture: 3 shelf items with version history; 1 up-propagation event; 1 resume packet derived from a shelf item.
notes: `obsidian/prompt_shelf/items/context_compaction.md` is the canonical example.
one_line: Prompts are first-class items on a shelf with provenance, versioning, up-propagation ledger, and schema-bound source-faithful packets for cross-tool/cross-agent resume.
title: Prompt shelf with durable packets and up-propagation
why_load_bearing: Stops the 'best prompt for X' from being tribal knowledge; prompts become typed durable artifacts.

candidate_fixture: Already exists as a leaf; should be re-projected with the full lifecycle and the scratch/durable boundary explicit.
notes: Current leaf is OK but understates the spine's role; reconstruction should make this a flagship leaf.
one_line: Append-only events project into WorkItems and caps; caps are typed durable backlog entries with lifecycle, satisfaction refs, ranking, validation, and a separate lane for in-turn scratch (TodoWrite) vs durable capture.
title: Task Ledger / WorkItem spine with cap economy
why_load_bearing: This is the spine that lets agent work survive context loss. The provider_native_task_affordance_boundary policy is the line that distinguishes scratch from durable backlog.

candidate_fixture: 3 toy prose drafts with tripwire phrases; the linter catching each; the cap_id citation injected; 1 self-error tripwire row.

title: Cap reflex: capture-before-prose
why_load_bearing: Solves the durability hole that every agent has: 'noticed but uncaptured.' Tripwire-list discipline closes it.

candidate_fixture: Existing leaf is fine; should make the four-way contract role explicit in the paper module.

title: Executable grammar metabolism
why_load_bearing: Standards-as-grammar means the substrate has a type system, not a style guide.

candidate_fixture: Existing leaf is fine; could be sharpened by adding the *46 paper modules in `subdomain_authority_projection`* as reference territory.

title: Status-preserving control plane
why_load_bearing: The 'no silent upgrade' invariant is what stops an AI substrate from cargo-culting itself into false confidence.

why_load_bearing: Without this, observation and mutation drift apart and finalization gets skipped; with it, every change has an envelope.

candidate_fixture: 3 toy pending decisions; one approve, one reject, one defer; the runtime side-effects of each.

candidate_fixture: Existing leaf is `provider_harness_canary`; reconstruction should rename and deepen with the metabolism-ledger and authority-class projection.

candidate_fixture: 3 toy workingness records; one falsification event downgrading a record; one next-test row.

why_load_bearing: Replaces 'I think this works' with a record of what was tested under what conditions, who recorded it, and what would prove it wrong.

candidate_fixture: 6 toy routes with embeddings; a grammar standard; a route-replay; one banned route with replacement.

title: Semantic routing plane with embedding substrate
why_load_bearing: Where naive RAG fails (hallucinated routes, drift, no provenance), the semantic routing plane gives you typed bounded routes.

candidate_fixture: 5 toy worker receipts across 2 providers and 3 tiers; one tier-mismatch finding; one cost-overrun finding.

title: Test impact map: change-to-test routing

candidate_fixture: 1 toy rename across 4 files; the migration receipt; updated callers; one rollback drill.

candidate_fixture: 5 toy kinds; cluster groupings; owner-check rows; freshness diagnostics; one stale-source-coupling finding.

candidate_fixture: 1 toy failed task; the DAG; the failure-class row; the restart point; the teaching capture.

candidate_fixture: 3 turns with state carryforward; one phase transition; one bounded autonomous action.

candidate_fixture: 1 toy thesis; the cascade through 3 layers; propagation receipt; 1 friction point.

title: Runtime maintenance finding
why_load_bearing: Substrate health surfaces as typed findings, not chat-only mention.

candidate_fixture: std_paper_module mini-row; itself validated against its own rules; freshness check; one drift finding.
notes: Sub-pattern of paper_module_authoring_runtime; could be a leaf or fold in.
one_line: `std_paper_module_v8` is itself a paper-module-style standard — it declares its own owned bands, source manifest, content hashes, validation report, and freshness contract; the standard governs itself.
title: Std paper module v8: self-governing standard
why_load_bearing: Strongest possible witness to 'standards as executable grammar': the standard IS its own conformance witness.

candidate_fixture: 3 windows with identities; 1 crash + reidentification; 1 ambiguous-resume finding.
notes: Pairs with bridge_runtime_dispatch_yield_resume.
one_line: `std_bridge_window_identity` declares a typed identity for each live bridge window (host, session id, lease, cohort) so resume-after-crash can identify which window owns which work.
title: Bridge window identity
why_load_bearing: Multi-window agent runtime needs typed identity to avoid cross-window confusion.

candidate_fixture: 1 toy session trace; the replay; 1 divergence finding; 1 corrected trace.
notes: Pairs with agent_route_compliance_audit.
one_line: `std_navigation_trace_event` + `std_navigation_trace_case` + `std_navigation_route_replay_result` declare typed navigation traces so a session's route through the substrate can be replayed and audited.
title: Navigation trace event + case
why_load_bearing: Navigation behavior becomes reproducible; route bugs become typed findings.

candidate_fixture: 1 toy campaign through 3 stages; 1 gate fail + rollback; receipt at each transition.
notes: Could be useful for showing 'iteration without drift.'
one_line: `std_preview_campaign_lifecycle` declares the typed lifecycle for any preview campaign (e.g. a doctrine preview, a feature preview, a release candidate) with stages, gates, and rollback hooks.
title: Preview campaign lifecycle
why_load_bearing: Previews become typed campaigns instead of ad-hoc branches.

candidate_fixture: 5 config decisions; the registry; 1 conflict (two sources claiming the same key); 1 resolution.
notes: Pairs with agent_harness_configuration_audit.
one_line: `std_config_authority_registry` names which file/key owns which config decision (settings.json, env, hook, permission) so 'where does this behavior come from?' is a typed lookup.
title: Config authority registry
why_load_bearing: Eliminates config-source mystery — settings provenance is typed.

candidate_fixture: 3 work items distributed across slots; 2 transitions with receipts; 1 invariant violation.
notes: Deeper version of phase_orchestration_dock_harbor_deposit.
one_line: Three phase slots — deposit (raw intake), dock (active staging), harbor (settled) — with typed transitions and per-slot invariants; the substrate's work has typed stages.
title: Phase deposit / dock / harbor (full)
why_load_bearing: Stages stay typed; cross-stage drift becomes a typed finding.

candidate_fixture: 4 toy skill types; 6 skills across types; 1 type-mismatch finding.
notes: Sub-pattern under typed_skills_with_type_plane.
one_line: `std_skill_types` declares the kinds of skill (annex / generated / hand-authored / domain / kernel / dissemination / raw_seed / reasoning) so the skills layer is itself typed.
title: Std skill types: versioned, typed skills
why_load_bearing: Skill discovery routes by type; the 210-skill library becomes navigable.

candidate_fixture: 3 toy adapter terms across 2 actors; the grammar; 1 translation; 1 missing-translation finding.
notes: Pairs with agent_entry_surfaces.
one_line: `std_navigation_rosetta_grammar` declares a translation grammar that maps actor-specific surface terms (Claude vs Codex vs Cursor) onto a shared canonical route — adapters converge on shared routes via translation, not duplication.
title: Navigation rosetta grammar
why_load_bearing: Multi-adapter substrate without adapter-specific drift.

candidate_fixture: Toy facts-at-a-glance projection with 5 rows + lineage to source standards.
notes: Tiny but powerful.
one_line: A typed `system_facts_at_a_glance` projection enumerates the system's load-bearing facts (defaults, authority chain, availability ladder) so a cold agent sees the substrate's spine in one row.
title: System facts at a glance
why_load_bearing: Cold-start compression: minimum sufficient read.

candidate_fixture: 5 toy agent principles with card-band projections; 1 lens query; 1 principle-vs-behavior diff.
notes: Pairs with type_a_type_b_actor_axes.
one_line: The kernel emits an `--agent-principles` band of cards specifically for agent behavior (Type A/B, proof binding, projection ownership, transaction closeout) — principles for *agents* are a typed lens, distinct from principles for code.
title: Agent principle lens
why_load_bearing: Agent-specific principles get their own optic; the substrate doesn't conflate code-principle with behavior-principle.

candidate_fixture: 1 toy pipeline with 5 stages; receipts at each stage; 1 stage-skip finding.
notes: Pairs with population_lane_generic.
one_line: `system_lib_pipeline_stages.md` declares the typed stages any system-lib pipeline goes through (intake / parse / classify / route / project / validate / settle) so pipelines compose under shared stage semantics.
title: System lib pipeline stages
why_load_bearing: Cross-pipeline composition becomes possible; new pipelines reuse stage primitives.

candidate_fixture: 1 toy markdown doc with 3 marked regions; 1 region extraction; 1 missing-region finding.
notes: Pairs with json_facets_projection and mechanism_facet_manifest.
one_line: `std_marked_region_projection` declares typed marked regions (in markdown, code, JSON) that downstream consumers extract by region name, so subsetting an artifact is typed rather than ad-hoc.
title: Marked region projection
why_load_bearing: Region-based extraction is durable across edits; downstream consumers don't reach into raw content.

candidate_fixture: 1 parent + 1 subagent; injection contract; 1 inherited principle lens; 1 missing-context finding.
notes: Pairs with subagent_cohort_delegation.
one_line: When a subagent starts, the hook ladder injects typed actor context (Type A/B, host, lease, banned routes, principle lens) so the child inherits typed contract, not raw prompt.
title: Actor context injection (SubagentStart)
why_load_bearing: Subagent setup is typed and inherited; child agents don't re-derive context from chat.

candidate_fixture: 3 toy resources with capacities; 1 lease; 1 exhaustion event + handler.
notes: Pairs with autonomy_runtime_metabolism.
one_line: `std_resource` declares typed resources (compute, API tokens, time-windows) with capacity, lease semantics, and exhaustion handlers — resource exhaustion becomes a typed event, not a silent fail.
title: Resource: typed capacity
why_load_bearing: Capacity issues surface; downstream waves can plan around limits.

candidate_fixture: A toy full artifact projected to atom/flag/card bands; each low-band row carries peer-level omission_receipt fields; a validator fails a lossy summary with no drilldown and passes the reversible row.
failure_mode_prevented: Silent authority deletion during compression or compact command output emission.
notes: Existing rows mention omission receipts inside other organs, but this is the standalone compression organ: omission is not loss when it is a typed, reversible boundary.
one_line: Every compact projection names what it intentionally omitted, why that omission is safe at the current band, and the exact drilldown or evidence command that restores the missing authority.
title: Omission receipt as reversible projection boundary
why_load_bearing: This is the safety valve that lets the substrate compress without deleting authority. Without it, cards, entry packets, command projections, and public-safe excerpts become lossy summaries that future agents cannot reverse.

candidate_fixture: Two authored toy sources plus one generated index; builder writes source_manifest and source_fingerprint; a dirty-source rerun emits source_coupling=false; scoped commit refuses generated-only landing.
failure_mode_prevented: Hand-edited or stale generated surfaces masquerading as source authority, especially under concurrent dirty-tree work.
notes: Sharper than a generic generated-projection row because it names the operational organ: one builder per projection family plus source-coupling commit discipline.
one_line: A family of deterministic builder CLIs regenerates generated sidecars and managed markdown regions from authored substrate, then source-coupling receipts decide whether outputs are fresh, stale, or unsafe to commit without their sources.
title: Builder factory: source-coupled projection lane
why_load_bearing: Generated projections are the main read surface for cold agents, but they are not authority. The builder lane keeps that split reproducible and prevents non-reproducible HEAD states where generated artifacts contain uncommitted source effects.

candidate_fixture: Three synthetic mutation proposals: green passes deterministic verifiers and auto-applies with rollback receipt; amber retries once with stronger verifier; red emits a compact exception packet with decision_needed and safe_fallback.
failure_mode_prevented: Routine machine-checkable work being parked behind the operator, or unsafe work being auto-applied without rollback and route probes.
notes: Not release authority. It is an internal mutation gate and should stay separate from publication/go-no-go gates.
one_line: Bounded, reversible, path-scoped, machine-verifiable mutation routes through green auto-apply, amber bounded retry, or red exception packet instead of defaulting to operator review.
title: Autonomous proof gate three-color default
why_load_bearing: It converts safety from a human bottleneck into a verifier-backed metabolism. The operator is reserved for policy, privacy, irreversible mutation, or inconclusive validators rather than every routine docstring, routing-atom, or projection rebuild.

candidate_fixture: Toy audit rows with failed_historic, failed_dispositioned, and failed_unresolved classes; alert_count is derived only from actionable_items; regression test proves historic-only rows produce no need-action wording.
failure_mode_prevented: False urgency from raw counts, plus provenance loss from deleting historic rows to silence alarms.
notes: This should be a prime microcosm specimen because it is easy to fixture and visibly changes agent behavior.
one_line: Status, pulse, audit, and dashboard surfaces derive urgent or need-action language from enumerable actionable item sets, while raw, historic, stale, or dispositioned counts remain visible as context-only evidence.
title: Actionability-driven alerting
why_load_bearing: The substrate steers agents through status surfaces. If raw lifecycle arithmetic drives alarms, agents spend waves repairing history; if history is hidden, provenance is lost. D4 keeps both truths separated.

candidate_fixture: Three toy principles with matching anti-profiles; validator enforces shared teleology_refs and forbids success-goal fields; a route packet uses the anti-profile to recover from a simulated failure.
failure_mode_prevented: Principles becoming aspirational slogans with no failure-attractor, detection, or recovery grammar.
notes: Use synthetic examples in any public projection; the macro rows can reference private source registries, but public leaves should not expose private raw-seed content.
one_line: Anti-principles and anti-axioms are routed negative-space maps around the same teleology as their parent principle or candidate axiom, carrying failure modes, detection signals, prevention, risks, recovery routes, and boundary conditions without inventing a new desire.
source_refs: obsidian/okay lets do this/09 - Raw-Seed Preservation, Semantic Reset, and Fresh Execution Spine/raw_seed/raw_seed_principles.json
obsidian/okay lets do this/09 - Raw-Seed Preservation, Semantic Reset, and Fresh Execution Spine/raw_seed/system_axiom_candidates.json
title: Negative-space anti-profile failure mirror
why_load_bearing: The system can teach agents what not to normalize. Desired principles alone do not expose why a violation feels tempting, how to detect it, or how to recover; anti-profiles make failure modes first-class without turning them into goals.

candidate_fixture: A toy bridge response with malformed-but-salvageable JSON, two traceable rows, and one untraceable row; salvage parses it, imports two, quarantines one, emits bridge_receipt.status=partial and import_count=2.
failure_mode_prevented: All-or-nothing provider batch failure, or untraceable model rows silently imported as if validated.
notes: Distinct from broad bridge continuity: this is the validator membrane between provider text and durable substrate rows.
one_line: Bridge model responses are parsed tolerantly, then row-validated with traceability anchors; valid rows land, bad rows quarantine with machine-comparable reasons, and a partially quarantined payload is partial rather than failed.
title: Bridge response salvage with quarantine semantics
why_load_bearing: It prevents provider schema quirks or one bad row from destroying useful bridge output while also preventing untraceable rows from entering the corpus. This is the bridge version of typed receipt validation.

candidate_fixture: Three proposal IR rows; one rejected, one accepted node, one accepted tombstone; overlay validates stable ids and graph-native shape; authored doctrine source hash stays unchanged.
failure_mode_prevented: Doctrine proposal machinery rewriting raw seed, concept files, mechanism files, or principle rows before reviewed graph-native approval exists.
notes: Sharper sub-pattern under doctrine population: it is the additive membrane between compiler IR and authored doctrine authority.
one_line: Accepted doctrine compiler proposals land in an additive reviewed overlay as graph-native nodes, edges, tombstones, and directives, while authored doctrine sources and raw-seed authority remain untouched until a governed migration chooses otherwise.
title: Doctrine approved overlay as additive review membrane
why_load_bearing: It lets doctrine evolve under review without smuggling edits into operator-voice or authored nodes. Proposal IR stays audit state; approved overlay becomes durable compiler input; source mutation remains a separate lane.

candidate_fixture: A toy hook receives huge stdin and nearby large state; budget wrapper permits compact append-only sidecar write, blocks full JSONL read, returns no-context under overload, and a test asserts max output and forbidden path access.
failure_mode_prevented: Control-plane self-interference through observer-plane recursion under automatic lifecycle cadence.
notes: This is stronger than a generic runtime-hook row: it names the resource and recursion boundary that keeps hooks from becoming another unbounded agent.
one_line: Automatic hooks, callbacks, bridge interceptors, and lifecycle collectors are hard resource membranes: they may append compact telemetry and make tiny deterministic decisions, but may not synchronously read the elephant state they emit, observe, index, mutate, or govern.
title: Lifecycle surface resource membrane
why_load_bearing: Lifecycle surfaces sit inside the agent loop. If they recursively consume observability history, large JSONL files, SQLite state, or private-root evidence, the control plane can self-interfere or panic while trying to observe itself.

candidate_fixture: Fully synthetic old-idea corpus with will_voice, ai_output, mixed, and paper_draft files; classifier assigns provenance/depth; mining drops fully-covered gestures, emits one new_dimension shard with coverage_check, and rejects missing coverage.
failure_mode_prevented: Archaeological mining producing duplicate doctrine, importing AI output as operator voice, or flattening historical vocabulary into current claims without inference flags.
notes: Public projection must be synthetic. The macro organ is high value because it generalizes raw seed and annex into a three-substrate metabolism family.
one_line: Older operator-voice and idea-corpus material is metabolized as a distinct archaeological substrate, classified by provenance and depth, then mined only when coverage checks prove the gesture is not already captured in current doctrine.
title: Voice archaeology as coverage-aware third substrate
why_load_bearing: It lets the system learn from its pre-system history without retreading already-metabolized ground or stealing AI-output text as operator voice. Archaeological distance becomes an extraction dial, not a reason to copy old claims literally.

candidate_fixture: A synthetic pasted trace is structured through a stdlib HTTP surface; `/save-json` enforces a payload bound and safe filename, writes to a temp downloads directory, and the resulting JSON is fed to a toy ingest validator.
failure_mode_prevented: External/manual traces being discarded, hand-transcribed into chat, or forced into controlled ingest paths before they are shaped.
notes: This is the intake side, not the ingest side. Public projection should show the boundary and avoid implying the app mutates repo state.
one_line: A small operator-facing HTTP/macOS app lets manually pasted or exported agent traces be shaped into bounded JSON files outside the repo before the operator chooses a typed ingest path.
title: Operator trace structurer for manual intake
why_load_bearing: Not all agent traces originate inside controlled runtime hooks. This organ prevents ad hoc scripts or malformed payloads from becoming the only bridge between external traces and the observability substrate.

candidate_fixture: Six toy lane snapshots feed one orchestrator; it emits state, brief, selected action, and event row; a direct focus-directive write is rejected in favor of the sanctioned helper.
failure_mode_prevented: Runtime-control truth split across lane drivers, manual focus-directive mutation, or stale orchestration claims derived outside the synthesizer.
notes: This fills the control_surface family gap in the ledger; it is macro-only unless a future microcosm can synthesize safe lane snapshots.
one_line: `system/control/orchestration.py` composes phase, factory, mission, docs-route, bridge, apply-staging, and bootstrap state into the single runtime-control state/brief/event model consumed by control-room and docs-route surfaces.
title: System control orchestration composition root
why_load_bearing: It prevents agents from reconstructing current orchestration by reading each lane independently and inventing a gate model. One synthesizer reads broadly, writes narrowly, and keeps persisted artifacts under the control lane.

candidate_fixture: A synthetic prompt corpus with A0/B1/B2/B3 slots; the index reports coverage and receipts, refuses duplicate run ids, and proves raw user/provider bodies are absent from summary rows.
failure_mode_prevented: Cold agents or classifiers scanning raw prompt shelf traces wholesale, leaking private prompt/provider bodies, or training on slot-skewed capture evidence.
notes: Fold with prompt_shelf_durable_packets if the public microcosm needs one prompt-shelf cluster; keep as a distinct macro row because it is the privacy/navigation membrane, not the packet family.
one_line: The prompt shelf run corpus is browsed through a metadata-only index that exposes slot coverage, receipts, byte counts, hashes, and lint status while omitting raw prompt and provider bodies until a selected row is explicitly opened.
title: Prompt shelf metadata privacy index
why_load_bearing: Prompt shelf traces are a major Type B and up-propagation evidence source, but the raw event corpus is too large and too private to be a first-contact surface. This organ makes the corpus usable without turning browsing into leakage or wholesale scan behavior.

candidate_fixture: One synthetic receipt shard becomes a provider application discovery row job; the provider may emit at most three candidates under a no-retry budget; validator rejects any write outside receipts or draft row patches.
failure_mode_prevented: Unbounded provider queue drain, retry fanout under rate limits, provider-authored source mutation, and dormant capability resurfacing only through old chat memory.
notes: This is a stronger reusable organ than a generic provider lane: it names the row-job membrane that keeps compute useful, finite, and non-authoritative.
one_line: Provider-plane applications are materialized as finite, pull-triggered row jobs with authority ceiling `provider_endpoint`, explicit source refs, zero-dollar/no-retry budget defaults, reducer-gated row patches, and forbidden-source mutation boundaries.
title: Provider plane finite pull row job
why_load_bearing: The system needs provider cognition, but provider output must not become an always-on daemon, hidden backlog drain, or source-writing actor. This organ converts provider value into bounded advisory receipts that Type A can promote or reject.

candidate_fixture: A toy prover benchmark emits one context-hypothesis receipt and one forbidden proof-body packet; reducer accepts the contextual receipt, policy-rejects the proof body, and requires a separate proof receipt before a claim becomes green.
failure_mode_prevented: Model-generated or private proof bodies entering the formal substrate as if they were verified Lean receipts.
notes: Pairs with the existing Lean self-contained proof organ; this row captures the provider membrane around formal proof rather than the proof artifact itself.
one_line: Formal provider jobs may receive bounded context, hypotheses, strategy classes, and receipt metadata, but forbidden proof-body surfaces such as Lean proof payloads are policy-rejected; formal authority remains with claim-bound proof receipts.
title: Formal prover payload policy boundary
why_load_bearing: Formal proof work is high-value only if proof authority is not laundered through model output. The payload boundary lets providers assist around the proof without receiving or manufacturing the authoritative proof body.

candidate_fixture: A synthetic root self-model renders two packets: a compact Type B primer and a wide system packet; validation fails if raw private identifiers leak, if commands become receiver assignments, or if the packet omits its authority boundary.
failure_mode_prevented: Cold-agent packets leaking private raw seed, becoming stale source authority, or assigning repository commands to actors without repo authority.
notes: This should not become dissemination packaging. The reusable organ is the governed render profile for self-comprehension projections.
one_line: Public-safe system packets are governed by render profiles that bind a root self-comprehension model to output paths, last-green/candidate sidecars, forbidden-output lists, validation probes, refresh owners, and a command-as-evidence-not-assignment rule.
title: System self-comprehension render profile
why_load_bearing: The system needs cold Type B and human review packets, but those packets are projections, not authority. The render-profile organ makes self-description reproducible, leak-checked, and actor-safe without handing receivers private repo commands or raw voice.

candidate_fixture: Three synthetic microcosm candidates are clustered into cells; one is public-safe, one private-only, and one blocked until proof receipt; a validator refuses release if the decision or boundary is absent.
failure_mode_prevented: Treating generated microcosm candidates or release rows as public authority before cluster decisions, boundaries, and proof receipts exist.
notes: High leverage for future microcosm construction because it compresses several organs without prematurely turning them into leaves.
one_line: System Atlas compound cells group multiple candidate microcosms and included patterns under explicit decisions such as `use_existing`, `private_only`, or `blocked_until_receipt`, with public/private boundary and next-drilldown receipts attached.
title: Compound microcosm cell decision projection
why_load_bearing: It prevents the current public leaf set from becoming authority while still preserving reusable clusters. The cell is a decision-bearing compression unit: it can say combine these, keep private, synthesize later, or block until proof exists.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic TSX files include a component, utility, hook, default alias, and wrapper; extractor emits stable ids and confidence labels, adapter counts only browse-safe rows, and tests prove utilities/hooks stay low-confidence.
failure_mode_prevented: Cold agents treating TSX files, route strings, utility exports, or low-confidence regex hits as first-class frontend components.
notes: Likely folds into a larger frontend observability cluster, but it is a distinct organ because it governs static component comprehension rather than runtime capture.
one_line: The frontend component index projects TSX source into stable component rows with source spans, declaration kinds, wrapper hints, and high/medium/low confidence, while explicitly omitting full TSX bodies, runtime DOM closure, and low-confidence browse authority where adapters need a clean option surface.
title: Frontend component index confidence membrane
why_load_bearing: The frontend cockpit is too large for cold agents to navigate by file scan. This organ makes components enumerable and testable while preserving source authority and preventing low-confidence extraction from becoming architectural truth.

candidate_fixture: Four synthetic route-failure anti-patterns are fed to a hook shadow checker; three map to route repairs and one lacks authority; the report exposes coverage, suggested sequence, fallback surface, and evidence command.
failure_mode_prevented: Deploying hooks from intended behavior alone, or letting hooks intervene without evidence that they address real route-failure classes.
notes: Stronger than a generic runtime-hook pattern: it captures the proof membrane between recent bad behavior and automatic intervention.
one_line: Before navigation hooks are trusted as live behavior, the substrate replays recent process-audit anti-patterns against route-repair suggestions and reports whether the hook would have intervened, with missing authority called out per pattern.
title: Runtime hook shadow intervention coverage
why_load_bearing: Hooks sit in the agent loop and can become noisy or ceremonial. Shadow coverage connects hook logic to observed failures, proving intervention coverage before the hook is treated as a reliable navigation organ.

candidate_fixture: A toy route anti-pattern appears in process audit, then an observed accepted mechanism row covers it; metabolism marks the debt retired, keeps advisory evidence, and reactivates if a fresh violation appears.
failure_mode_prevented: Stale route anti-pattern debt continuing to steer agents after the system has observed a corrective mechanism, or evidence being deleted to make counts look clean.
notes: This is one of the clearest self-improvement metabolism organs found in this pass.
one_line: Navigation metabolism accepts observed mechanism rows, projects them, and retires matching process-audit anti-pattern debt while preserving advisory evidence and regression visibility instead of deleting the history.
title: Observed anti-pattern debt retirement
why_load_bearing: Self-improvement needs negative evidence to expire when behavior changes, but it also needs provenance to remain inspectable. This organ turns behavior correction into a typed debt-retirement event rather than a quiet count drop.

candidate_fixture: A synthetic scoped commit lands while Task Ledger files are dirty; direct receipt write is blocked, intake request is enqueued with idempotency key, Work Ledger closes, exact drain records the receipt, and duplicate drains become no-ops.
failure_mode_prevented: Losing execution receipts, broadly staging shared Task Ledger state, or leaving WorkItems apparently unclosed after a valid scoped commit.
notes: This is a closeout metabolism organ, not just task-ledger plumbing; it protects the commit discipline in a live shared tree.
one_line: When scoped commit landing cannot write a Task Ledger execution receipt directly because of same-file entanglement or claim collision, the lane enqueues an exact idempotent receipt request and drains that precise request after Work Ledger closeout.
title: Task Ledger exact receipt drain
why_load_bearing: Work closure depends on receipts, but the Task Ledger is itself a shared authority file. This organ lets scoped commits converge without broad staging, lost receipts, or manual chat-only closeout under dirty-tree concurrency.

candidate_fixture: A toy atlas generated from standards and frontend sources becomes stale after source edits; option-surface browse still works, but commit safety fails until sources and generated output land together or the builder refreshes.
failure_mode_prevented: Generated System Atlas rows being committed alone after source changes, or agents refusing to use the atlas at all when source coupling is stale.
notes: Sharpened sub-pattern under builder_factory_source_coupled_projection_lane, kept because System Atlas is the high-fanout place where stale-but-browsable behavior matters most.
one_line: System Atlas option surfaces remain browsable when source families move, but their generated outputs carry stale-source coupling and `safe_to_commit_without_sources=false` so agents can read projections without landing generated-only authority drift.
title: System Atlas source-coupling gate
why_load_bearing: The atlas is a core cold-agent map, yet it is generated from many moving substrates. This gate keeps the map useful during churn while preventing stale generated rows from being committed as if they were source-synchronized truth.

candidate_fixture: Toy CP-like dicts: one valid, one missing field, one out-of-universe ticker, one bad ledger_id; validator returns ValidationResult with sorted-unique errors; mutation attempt on errors raises; validator never mutates input dict.
failure_mode_prevented: Engine acting on malformed CP artifact without a stable classify-and-retry gate; ad-hoc per-caller validation that drifts; engine/server/bridge import creep into validation.
notes: ValidationResult.errors is always sorted(set(...)) as a locked decision (LD9). Config-driven ledger_id regex and OUT_OF_UNIVERSE check against runtime ticker sets make it validator-as-contract-enforcer rather than validator-as-linter. _DEFAULT_CONFIG fallback for isolated test environments is a deliberate isolation seam.
one_line: Pure-function validator over CP1/CP2/oracle artifact dicts returns a frozen ValidationResult with deterministic sorted-dedup error codes and never mutates its input, giving every caller one stable retry-vs-hard-fail vocabulary.
title: CP1/CP2 artifact schema validator with stable error-code semantics
why_load_bearing: Engine retry, hard-fail, and persistence decisions depend on a single stable error-code vocabulary (MISSING_FIELD, INVALID_LEDGER_ID, GROUNDING_FAILED_WRONG_LANE, OUT_OF_UNIVERSE, TEMPORAL_MISMATCH, etc.). Without this organ, callers accumulate ad-hoc checks that drift, and engine/server/bridge imports creep into the validation layer.

candidate_fixture: A toy substrate with append, --validate, --self-test modes; tests assert determinism, no-write under --validate, write-only under --self-test, and per-actor admission gates; mutation of the contract makes the test fail loudly.
failure_mode_prevented: Silent substrate contract regression (mode boundaries, actor gates, determinism, idempotency) that prose-only documentation cannot detect.
notes: test_lattice_event_invariants.py is the clearest specimen: asserts --validate is no-write against the live repo ledger (not a stub), inspects source text to confirm --self-test is evidence-writing, parametrizes M3/M4 actor-gate rejection. test_agent_entry_surface_standard_contract.py reads the live standard JSON and asserts forbidden_action_classes string membership. Substrate proof tests, not coverage tests.
one_line: A test class where the assertion target is a substrate invariant (deterministic event_id, --validate is no-write, actor-gate rejection, idempotency lookup) rather than feature coverage, exercising the live substrate with monkeypatched isolation so prose guarantees become enforced.
title: Test as machine-checkable substrate contract
why_load_bearing: Without machine-checked invariant proofs, substrate contracts exist only in doctrine prose. When the substrate drifts, prose does not catch it; this class of test is the only feedback loop that turns documented invariants into enforced invariants and prevents the test layer from collapsing into feature-coverage.

candidate_fixture: Toy bundle with one valid shard, one voice_anchor mismatch, one architecture-leak shard, one routing-ref leak; advisory mode reports all as warnings, strict mode rejects, force_accept passes with warnings recorded; to_report_payload() returns sorted machine-readable record.
failure_mode_prevented: Architecture-leak shards, routing-reference leaks, untraceable voice anchors, or hedge-flattened high-confidence shards entering the doctrine substrate.
notes: force_accept=True path enables Opus gold-seed bootstrap without suppressing quality signals. REVERSAL_MARKERS check is bundle-scoped (not per-shard) and enforces the D1 split-reversals rule at machine level.
one_line: Multi-mode validator (advisory/strict/force_accept) for raw-seed distillation bundles classifies per-shard failures (voice_anchor_substring_match, compression_bounds, routing-ref leak, architecture leak, confidence calibration, reversal preservation) and emits a structured ValidatorResult with to_report_payload(), letting bridge and subagent lanes share one quality gate while letting the caller decide rejection policy.
title: Distillation bundle validator with advisory/strict/force-accept modes
why_load_bearing: Distillation quality depends on six per-shard invariants. Without this validator, bridge and subagent lanes bypass these checks and architecture-leak or unprovenanced shards silently land in extracted_shards.json. The advisory/strict split is policy, not arbitrary toggles: it lets gold-seed calibration record issues as warnings while production runs reject them.

candidate_fixture: Toy test suite with one unmarked unit, one @pytest.mark.smoke, one @pytest.mark.slow_control_plane; collection under -m 'not slow_control_plane' returns only the first two; collection under -m smoke returns one.
failure_mode_prevented: Slow or state-mutating tests running in fast slices, causing false failures or latency; flat suites that hide cost asymmetry from runners.
notes: Observed marks: integration_git_control, slow_control_plane, integration_generated_state, smoke. full_drainer_settlement skipif adds conditional skip on top. Vocabulary lives in the test source; routing happens at pytest collection.
one_line: A pytest mark vocabulary (smoke, slow_control_plane, integration_git_control, integration_generated_state) routes tests by cost class at collection time, isolating real-subprocess, real-git, and live-projection tests from the fast unit feedback loop.
title: Pytest mark taxonomy for tiered test isolation
why_load_bearing: Without mark-based tier separation, every fast pass executes any test that constructs a real git bare repo, runs a real preflight subprocess, or settles live projections — turning the test layer into untriaged friction. Marks are the only mechanism that lets CI and dev runners select cost-appropriate slices without coupling test names to runner config.

candidate_fixture: Toy fast and full preflight invocations sharing one cache file; fast hits cached full-success and skips; full ignores cached fast-success and reruns; lookup/store/invalidate/describe form the API.
failure_mode_prevented: Redundant full preflight reruns within a single work session; fast-mode requests re-running after a recent full-mode success.
notes: Test proves the negative path (full ignores cache) which is harder than the positive path. Atomic write via tmp+rename keeps multiple concurrent surfaces consistent.
one_line: A JSON sentinel at state/bridge_preflight_cache.json gives every surface that invokes preflight a single shared TTL-keyed skip window, with mode asymmetry (full-success satisfies fast-request; fast-success does NOT satisfy full-request).
title: Bridge preflight skip-window cache
why_load_bearing: Without a shared skip window, every surface invoking preflight re-runs the full browser boot. The mode asymmetry encodes that full-mode is always-fresh while fast-mode reuses recent success — the cache is policy, not a naive equality check.

candidate_fixture: Two toy run manifests across two engines and two viewports; compute_image_diff produces overlay PNGs with changed pixel counts; compare_manifests records changed_pixel_count per (engine, view_slug, viewport_slug) tuple.
failure_mode_prevented: Silent visual regression — a cockpit edit ships with no proof of pixel-level impact across engines.
notes: compute_image_diff produces a red-highlight composite PNG where pixels changed. compare_manifests indexes by full matrix tuple. Tests cover pixel count, changed_percent, overlay existence.
one_line: Pixel-level diff overlay between two station-render runs across the (engine, view_slug, viewport_slug) matrix, with changed-pixel count, changed-percent, and red-highlight composite PNG output.
title: Station render pixel-diff compare engine
why_load_bearing: Without this organ, cockpit edits ship with no proof of pixel-level impact across Chromium and WebKit. The diff overlay is the only artifact that turns 'looks fine' into a comparable receipt across runs, and the engine×viewport matrix indexing prevents per-engine regressions from being hidden behind aggregate pass.

candidate_fixture: Two toy runs writing append-only log entries with load_ms and runtime_context; rebuilt index has sample_count=2, p50/p95 computed, latest_receipt_ref pointing at run_stamp/manifest.json; one degraded runtime_context.contracts row visible in the index.
failure_mode_prevented: Render timing data is ephemeral with no cross-run trend or receipt linkage; backend contract degradation invisible in timing record.
notes: Lifecycle schema role 'generated_latest_projection'. Durable receipt source is <run_stamp>/manifest.json. Proven in test via two-run accumulation with preload_output_path, readiness_attrs, runtime_context.
one_line: Append-only render_load_log.jsonl plus rebuilt render_load_index.json keyed by (view_slug, run_stamp) carries p50/p95 load_ms, latest readiness_attrs, runtime_context.contracts state, and durable receipt pointers — so render timing is queryable across runs and bound to backend contract state.
title: Station render load index timing ledger
why_load_bearing: Without the load index, render timing is ephemeral per-run data with no cross-run trend and no link from a slow render to whether the backend was missing data. The runtime_context.contracts payload in the index lets a reader distinguish 'slow render' from 'backend contract missing'.

candidate_fixture: Toy nav graph A→B; three scenarios (direct, action replay, hybrid); each emits a receipt; one scenario with missing ready_selector emits typed blocker; suite receipt aggregates three scenarios.
failure_mode_prevented: Declared nav edges are untraversable at runtime — the graph says A→B is reachable but no Playwright run has ever confirmed it.
notes: Three modes: direct (station_render + ready_selector), embodied (Playwright trigger + wait), hybrid (direct_entry + action_replay). Each emits a typed receipt (frontend_wayfinding_receipt_v1 / _action_receipt_v1 / _scenario_receipt_v1) with plan_hash, action_trace_hash, focus_observed, readiness_attrs.
one_line: Given a source and target view, plans the shortest available affordance path, then executes it in one of three modes (direct route, embodied Playwright action replay, hybrid) and emits a typed receipt with plan_hash, action_trace_hash, focus_observed, and readiness_attrs.
title: Frontend wayfinding proof lane — executable source→target receipt
why_load_bearing: Navigation intent is cheap to declare in surfaces.ts. This organ closes the gap between declaration and proof by actually driving the browser through declared paths and surfacing typed blockers (trigger_missing, target_ready_selector_missing, origin_unreachable) when declared edges are untraversable at runtime.

candidate_fixture: Toy hook with COCKPIT_SOURCE_PREFIX and COCKPIT_EDIT_TOOLS set; an Edit on system/server/ui/foo.tsx triggers additionalContext naming the owned engine and CLI commands; an Edit elsewhere emits nothing.
failure_mode_prevented: Agent routes cockpit verification to generic preview_* MCP, bypassing the station_render readiness protocol and pixel-diff compare.
notes: _cockpit_edit_counterinject (lines 912-973). Fires on PostToolUse when tool_name is in COCKPIT_EDIT_TOOLS and path starts with COCKPIT_SOURCE_PREFIX. Runtime enforcement of a policy already in doctrine.
one_line: PostToolUse hook detects edits under system/server/ui/ and counter-injects additionalContext that overrides the harness <preview_tools> default with the repo's owned station_render pipeline (preflight, render --view, diff --run-a --run-b).
title: Cockpit edit → station_render verification override (hook counter-injection)
why_load_bearing: The Claude Code harness preamble instructs agents to use preview_start/preview_snapshot for UI changes. Without this hook override, agents silently route cockpit edits to the wrong verification surface, defeating the artifact-first capture contract and skipping the readiness-protocol pixel-diff gate.

candidate_fixture: Toy App.tsx, surfaces.ts, station_views.json with three routes; one route has no capture contract; one surface has no router backing; builder emits drift_signals.routes_without_surfaces and capture_rows_without_surfaces; --check exits non-zero.
failure_mode_prevented: Nav graph infers coverage from partial evidence — a route with no capture contract or a surface edge with no router backing ships silently.
notes: Five sources: App.tsx, surfaces.ts, overlays.ts, StationLens.tsx, station_views.json. Drift classes: routes_without_surfaces, surfaces_without_routes, capture_rows_without_surfaces, undeclared_cul_de_sacs. Schema v7.
one_line: Builds a typed navigation graph from three evidence sources (App.tsx routes, surfaces.ts registry, station_views.json capture manifest), surfacing drift_signals when any source lacks a counterpart in the other two and refusing to infer coverage from partial evidence.
title: Frontend navigation graph three-source consistency enforcement
why_load_bearing: Navigation in a React Router SPA drifts silently: a route exists in App.tsx but has no capture contract, or a surface declares a nav edge with no router backing. This organ makes all three-way inconsistencies explicit and loud, with an undeclared-cul-de-sac honesty rule that prevents the graph from inferring reachability it cannot prove.

candidate_fixture: Toy std_standard_type_plane.json with two artifact kinds; build_renderer_passports emits a passport per kind with governing_standard_refs, density_bands, allowed_actions, receipt_policy; --check detects drift after manual edit of stored passport.
failure_mode_prevented: Frontend renderer has no stable contract for an artifact kind — displays wrong density bands, exposes mutation actions without a mutation lane, or loses governing ref linkage.
notes: Two passport origins: explicit_profile_with_type_plane_row, type_plane_row_auto_derived. mutation_lane field gates which artifact kinds are exposed for UI mutation actions. --check exits non-zero on drift.
one_line: Derives per-artifact-kind renderer contracts (governing refs, source authority, density bands, allowed actions, receipt policy, public projection policy) from std_standard_type_plane.json so frontend views read one stable passport instead of re-deriving rendering rules ad hoc.
title: Renderer passport — type-plane-derived rendering contracts
why_load_bearing: Without this organ, each frontend view that renders an artifact kind re-derives its governing refs, drilldown commands, and mutation lanes; the renderer can silently expose mutation actions for artifact kinds that have no mutation lane, or display wrong density bands. The passport is the stable contract the renderer reads, and --check detects drift between the live type plane and the stored projection.

candidate_fixture: Toy events.jsonl: one trace captured, indexed, digested, selected_for_adoption, bound to a toy workitem, mutated owner surface, validated, projected to entry, observed in future run; posture projection reflects terminal state.
failure_mode_prevented: Prompt-improvement lessons accumulate as captured telemetry but never land in the prompt body; the system mistakes 'evidence captured' for 'lesson applied' and iterates without learning.
notes: ADOPTION_STATES tuple (line 61), ADOPTION_STATE_ORDER, _build_adoption_posture() (line 608), source_drift / adoption_posture view paths. EVENT_TYPES includes prompt_trace.adoption_state_changed and prompt_trace.observed. Spans the consumer-side up-propagation loop named under-mined in the prior pass.
one_line: Event-sourced lifecycle walks a captured prompt run through nine adoption states (captured → indexed → digested → selected_for_adoption → bound_to_workitem → mutated_owner_surface → validated → projected_to_entry → observed_in_future_run), closing the loop between a Type B use and a durable prompt-shelf edit.
title: Prompt trace adoption state machine
why_load_bearing: Without this state machine, captured prompt runs accumulate without any governed path to prompt mutation. The adoption-state ladder is what makes prompt-improvement evidence-driven rather than taste-driven; skipping it means lessons are silently ignored or silently applied without proof. The 'observed_in_future_run' terminal state is the organ that closes the feedback loop.

candidate_fixture: Toy prompt family with three captured runs; quality packet scores each on RUBRIC_TERMS axes (intent_preservation, evidence_honesty, action_strength, lineage_preservation, compression_quality); gaps trigger one specimen-run request per failing axis.
failure_mode_prevented: Prompt edits improve structure but do not shift model behavior; behavioral regressions invisible until an operator manually notices output degradation.
notes: RUBRIC_TERMS dict (line 77) defines behavioral contract axes. POST_TELOS_COMMIT_AT sentinel creates a postpatch vs prepatch sample window so postpatch behavioral regression is detectable. SPECIMEN_FAMILIES dict closes the loop from gap to actionable re-run request.
one_line: Two-stage evidence pipeline compares what a prompt's telos contract promises against what captured run outputs actually demonstrate (rubric scoring), then generates targeted specimen-run requests for any behavioral gap — closing the prompt outcome diff metabolism loop.
title: Prompt family behavioral quality gate
why_load_bearing: A prompt can be structurally correct (lint passes, fingerprints match, cockpit mirrors) yet consistently fail to produce the behaviors its telos contract claims. This organ is the only gate that checks behavioral intent vs outcome across real captured runs. Without it, prompt quality is assessed by structure rather than by performance.

candidate_fixture: Toy run emits three movement blocks (one navigation, one doctrine, one standards); index keys by recurrence_key; same key appearing across 3 runs is flagged as repeated_signal; non-terminal-cluster blocks ignored.
failure_mode_prevented: A Type B run notices a substrate gap and either buries it in prose (invisible) or emits a heavy WorkItem capture at closeout when the right move is recurrence-weighted advisory routing.
notes: REQUIRED_FIELDS_V1 (line 90) names 10 required fields. Terminal-cluster placement rule prevents non-terminal quoted examples from polluting the index. recurrence_key enables cross-run repetition detection without manual correlation.
one_line: A structured sidecar transport (aiw:movement v=1) lets a Type B run emit a typed substrate-motion proposal (owning_plane, signal_kind, recurrence_key, movement, promotion_boundary, do_not_do, evidence_anchor, validation_target) without silently mutating any owning surface.
title: AIW movement typed cross-plane substrate proposal
why_load_bearing: Type B synthesis surfaces gaps that belong to a different plane. Without a typed cross-plane proposal format, those observations leak as operator-facing prose (invisible to the system) or silently land in the wrong place. The movement block enforces explicit owning plane, recurrence detection, and a recorded promotion boundary so motion signals are routed by typed semantics, not buried in chat.

candidate_fixture: Toy ephemeral markdown ledger with three fenced blocks following introducer phrases; extractor writes three exemplar rows with constraint/closeout/model-tier classification; --live promotion gate is required to update the shelf.
failure_mode_prevented: Curation decisions made from operator memory rather than from queryable evidence; one-time candidates promoted while well-worn patterns fail the multi-case rule.
notes: _INTRODUCER_RE, _CONSTRAINT_RE, _CLOSEOUT_RE, _MODEL_TIER_PATTERNS are classification organs. Three-layer architecture: ledger → exemplar cache → prompt shelf → heavy doctrine. --live flag is the explicit promotion gate.
one_line: Fenced-block mining organ extracts paste-ready prompt exemplars from ephemeral markdown ledgers into a queryable evidence cache, answering 'have we seen this prompting move before?' without promoting the ephemeral source to canonical state.
title: Prompt exemplar evidence cache
why_load_bearing: Prompt shelf curation requires evidence that a candidate pattern appeared in multiple real cases. Without a structured exemplar cache, curation relies on the operator's memory. The cache separates evidence-mining from authority promotion: extraction is evidence-only; only --live runs touch the canonical shelf, preserving the three-layer separation between raw paste history and curated shelf.

candidate_fixture: Toy provider with two concurrent surfaces; first surface acquires slot, gets rate_limit, writes cooldown row; second surface blocked by cooldown without re-trying; cooldown expiry releases gate; provider_pressure_retry_plan emits typed schedule.
failure_mode_prevented: Each dispatch surface inventing its own cooldown causes simultaneous retries that amplify rate-limit pressure instead of honoring the shared backoff schedule.
notes: Key organs: acquire_provider_claim, release_provider_claim, claimed_provider_slot (context manager), call_with_provider_claim, provider_pressure_retry_plan. _pressure_kind classifier maps seven failure-text patterns to typed kinds. Backoff schedule is config-driven via metabolism store.
one_line: Acquires a TTL-bound provider slot before dispatch, classifies pressure failures into typed kinds (rate_limit, provider_busy, host_occupied, concurrency_cap, serialization_key, cooldown, provider_interrupt) and writes a shared cooldown store, so every dispatch surface sees the same provider pressure state and honors one backoff schedule.
title: Provider slot claim, cooldown, and backpressure gate
why_load_bearing: Without this organ, concurrent dispatch surfaces (campaign, trickle, daemon) freelance their own backoff logic and fight over the same provider. The shared cooldown store is the single pressure truth that prevents simultaneous rate-limit storms.

candidate_fixture: Toy candidate feed of three rows from two providers; toy scorecard with one high-fail provider; snapshot demotes high-fail provider's score; cpu_gate suppresses one row; why_nothing_ran explains the idle outcome.
failure_mode_prevented: Without scorecard feedback the selector keeps dispatching to providers with high schema-fail rates; stale receipts silently block promotion without surfacing why.
notes: Three sub-organs: derive_provider_scorecard (counts schema_pass/fail/veto per key from disk receipts), _apply_scorecard_evidence (adjusts score vectors), derive_metabolism_selector_snapshot (joins all signals). openrouter paid-spend guard is a hard veto enforced here.
one_line: Joins candidate row jobs with governor gates, CPU load admission, per-provider pressure, stale-source-fingerprint detection, and historical receipt scorecards (schema_pass/fail/veto counts per provider+model+task_class key) to produce a read-only selector snapshot that explains why each candidate is or is not claimable now.
title: Provider candidate selector snapshot with scorecard evidence
why_load_bearing: Without this organ the scheduler has no principled way to pick between candidates from different providers and no way to explain idle states. The scorecard evidence adjusts score vectors (failure_history, structured_output_adherence, review_burden) based on observed history, so selection quality improves with each dispatch cycle rather than treating every dispatch as a first attempt.

candidate_fixture: Toy provider plane with three lanes (one healthy, one backpressured by repeated timeouts, one dormant with no successful receipt); liveness surface emits lane_health, yield_metrics (schema_fail_rate_7d, timeout_rate_7d), safety block with five invariants, next_action gated by safety.
failure_mode_prevented: Without the finite-pull contract being explicitly machine-readable, a metabolism agent could mistake the provider plane for a daemon-style always-on loop and trigger continuous provider calls that exhaust quotas.
notes: bounded_scan vs full_scan with month-bucket path heuristic. dormant_lanes from lanes that never saw a successful receipt. provider_backpressure from repeated-timeout receipts. yield_metrics block: schema_fail_rate_7d, timeout_rate_7d, hallucinated_ref_rate_7d. provider_plane_pulse_lines() is the --pulse slice.
one_line: Projects transform-jobs, receipts, row-patches, and reviews into a bounded liveness surface that enforces the finite_pull_triggered_batches execution shape, surfaces backpressured and dormant lanes, and emits recommended next actions gated by safety invariants (daemon=disabled_by_default, source_mutation=forbidden).
title: Provider plane finite-pull liveness and dormant lane detection
why_load_bearing: This is the safety guard that prevents daemon-style continuous provider polling. The execution_shape contract and safety invariants are only machine-checkable through this surface; without it, a metabolism agent could mistake the provider plane for an always-on loop and exhaust quotas.

candidate_fixture: Toy row_patch with five reviews (one of each outcome); summarize_row_patch_reviews emits 7d counts; immutable sidecar keyed by patch_id + reviewed_at; promotion lane refuses to act without an accept_as_advisory_signal record.
failure_mode_prevented: Provider output silently becoming source authority when no review record exists; review counts derived from prose notes that cannot be machine-aggregated.
notes: Five typed outcomes: accept_as_advisory_signal, reject, retry, bridge_escalate, record_no_op. Immutable sidecars keyed by patch_id + reviewed_at. summarize_row_patch_reviews produces 7d counts consumed by liveness and selector snapshot.
one_line: Records immutable Type A reducer decisions (accept_as_advisory_signal / reject / retry / bridge_escalate / record_no_op) over provider row_patches as machine-readable sidecar artifacts so provider output cannot self-promote into source authority without a typed evaluator decision.
title: Type A reducer row-patch review gate (evaluator authority boundary)
why_load_bearing: This is the evaluator authority gate. The distinction between provider-produced evidence and source-authority doctrine is only enforced because every row_patch must receive a typed review record before any promotion. Without the sidecar, the promotion lane has no machine-readable basis to confirm evaluator approval, and review counts cannot be aggregated into liveness or selector surfaces.

candidate_fixture: Toy three-feed run: one ok, one warn (low freshness), one block (sparse sample); ArtifactQualityStatus aggregates; quality_grade_override returns red; Oracle grading demotes the run without marking it a prediction failure.
failure_mode_prevented: Feed degradation silently absorbed into Oracle grading as though data quality were normal, causing Evolve to learn from bad substrate.
notes: ArtifactQualityStatus is a frozen dataclass. normalize_quality_tone is the canonical parser. quality_grade_override: any block → red, any warn → amber, else None. Additive-only contract: missing quality blocks are no-opinion, not failure.
one_line: Normalizes artifact-attached quality tones (ok/warn/block) across all feed lanes into a canonical ArtifactQualityStatus and derives a three-color (green/amber/red) grade override so the Oracle grading surface can demote a run without treating feed degradation as a prediction failure.
title: Feed quality tone gate and grade override
why_load_bearing: Without this organ, feed quality stays embedded in per-lane shapes and cannot be aggregated. quality_grade_override is the mechanism by which a block tone from any feed escalates to a red grade that stops downstream reasoning from treating degraded data as valid prediction evidence — the named 'downgrade semantics' organ.

candidate_fixture: Toy lane emits 1000 warnings; envelope clips to 500 with overflow_counter; secret 'API_KEY=sk_test_xxx' in URL is redacted in warning_event; fetch_outcome counters increment success/failure; CANONICAL_METADATA_KEYS pinned in test.
failure_mode_prevented: Lane-specific metadata drift causing Station/Oracle feed_health to misparse diagnostics; unbounded warning lists exhausting memory during transient outages; secrets leaked into diagnostic logs.
notes: Key exports: UtcNow, utc_now, resolve_as_of, resolve_run_id, new_diagnostics, append_warning (bounded), append_partial_failure (bounded structured), warning_event (with secret redaction), record_fetch_outcome, redact_secret_values. CANONICAL_METADATA_KEYS + CANONICAL_DIAGNOSTICS_KEYS pinned in tests.
one_line: Every feed lane shares one envelope ABI: a single UtcNow timestamping root, bounded warnings (500) and partial_failures (200) lists, fetch-outcome counters, secret redaction, and a canonical metadata+envelope builder so per-lane diagnostics cannot drift in shape.
title: Feed envelope diagnostics contract with bounded partial-failure accounting
why_load_bearing: Without a shared envelope, each lane's diagnostics drift and downstream consumers (Station, Oracle feed_health) must handle N different metadata layouts. Bounded lists prevent artifact explosion when upstream outages flood messages; the secret redactor prevents API keys from leaking into diagnostic events.

candidate_fixture: Toy generated packet with root_handle + root_handle_sha256_at_render_time + render_profile_id + substrate_snapshot_sha256 + current_artifact_sha256 + last_green_sha256; tamper with substrate then re-render — packet's last_green_rendered_at differs from last_rendered_at; consumer detects divergence.
failure_mode_prevented: Cold agent mistakes generated packet for source authority, cites stale or wrong system identity, bypasses raw-seed governance.
notes: Gold-standard pattern in system_packet_status.json: root_handle + root_handle_sha256_at_render_time + render_profile_id + source_model + source_substrate_snapshot_sha256 + current_artifact_sha256 + last_green_sha256 + last_rendered_at + last_green_rendered_at. Divergence audit recommends promotion to std_self_description_render_status.json.
one_line: Every generated self-description surface carries an explicit root_handle, render_profile_id, substrate fingerprint, and substrate-snapshot SHA so consumers can distinguish projection from source authority without reading doctrine.
title: Root-handle binding protocol for generated projections
why_load_bearing: Without explicit root_handle fields, cold agents conflate generated packets with canonical source authority. The divergence audit found 8 medium-severity surfaces lacking binding, including atlas-governing doctrine and system_facts_at_a_glance. The pattern also enforces that a rendered packet cannot silently diverge from its declared root.

candidate_fixture: Toy branch set with three branches; one has paper module + route-probe pass + runtime receipt + workingness judgment (grade 7 candidate); ceiling dict caps it at grade 5; per-branch override raises a fourth branch's ceiling; PUBLIC_PROJECTION_STATUS table determines exposure.
failure_mode_prevented: Premature release readiness claims from route-probe passes without workingness judgment; silently overclaiming public-safe status for private-trace branches.
notes: 15 branch specs, 7 doctrine-layer specs, per-branch ceiling table, PUBLIC_PROJECTION_STATUS_BY_ID, PRIVATE_BOUNDARY_STATUS_BY_ID, WORKINGNESS_EVIDENCE_BY_ID, NEXT_REPAIR_BY_ID all encoded as static dicts in builder.
one_line: A 7-grade coverage scale (absent → named → routable → described → indexed → operationally_evidenced → status_bound) with per-branch ceilings (COVERAGE_GRADE_CEILINGS dict) hard-wires that route-health checks cannot overstate workingness, receipts, or public-release readiness for any substrate branch.
title: Self-comprehension branch-coverage grade with per-branch ceilings
why_load_bearing: Without ceilings, a branch whose paper module exists and route-probe passes could silently claim grade-7 release-relevant status when runtime receipts or workingness judgments are missing. The ceilings encode that python_substrate and agent_runtime_bridge cannot exceed grade 5 regardless of route checks, preventing premature release claims.

candidate_fixture: Toy 4-facet registry (identity, activity, workingness, release); each facet has route_ids, authority_surface, evidence_streams, receipt_artifacts, answerability_status (resolved/missing/partial); derived missing-answerability list shows which facets need work.
failure_mode_prevented: False completeness claim from identity-route green; incapacity to answer operational questions (capability, release, health, next-action) in structured answerable form.
