notes: Facet fields: facet_id, facet_role, question_family, route_ids, authority_surface, supporting_surfaces, evidence_streams, projection_artifacts, receipt_artifacts, route_status, answerability_status, evidence_status, receipt_status, workingness_status, frontend_consumer, omission_policy. Missing-answerability list is the derived gap surface.
one_line: A typed facet map (identity, activity, work-spine, capability, workingness, release/demo, compile-chain, frontend, product-loop, current-frame) assigns per-facet answerability, evidence, receipt, and workingness judgments so coverage gaps surface as structured missing-facet findings rather than prose observations.
title: Self-comprehensibility facet registry
why_load_bearing: Without the facet registry, a system that passes all route-probe checks still cannot answer 'what can this system do', 'release readiness', 'what is blocked', or 'next action'. The diagnostic pass found 5 of 10 facets unresolved at the route plane; this organ names the gap class so repair is actionable rather than exploratory.

candidate_fixture: Toy three-candidate registry: one with only rediscovery signal (rejected by ≥2 class floor), one with rediscovery + route coverage + atlas unknown (promoted to candidate), one with five classes + operator anchor (authored); audit detects two false-positives where module already exists.
failure_mode_prevented: Paper-module sprawl from single-signal pressure; duplicate authoring over existing modules; stale queue entries training false candidates across sessions; alternatively, under-authoring from single-signal suppression.
notes: Distinct from doctrine_population_loop (pipeline) and paper_module_authoring_runtime_self_describing (authoring step). This is specifically the convergent-pressure scoring and promotion gate. Names 5 gap-class types: routing_gap, content_coverage_gap, projection_lag, decision_gap, residual_gap.
one_line: Seven typed pressure-ledger classes (explicit candidates, validation queues, rediscovery mining, route coverage, atlas unknowns, WorkItem orbits, raw-seed anchors) must converge at ≥2 classes before a module is promoted to candidate and ≥3+operator anchor before first-authoring, preventing paper-module sprawl from single-signal evidence.
title: Paper-module convergent pressure metabolism
why_load_bearing: Without convergent-pressure gating, cold agents treat any stale rediscovery finding as a mandate to author markdown, creating duplicate or spurious modules. The ≥2 class floor encodes epistemic humility about single-signal coverage pressure.

candidate_fixture: Toy 5-surface registry with first_contact_collision scores (none/low/medium/high), search_aliases_overlap_with_root flags, severity rollup, recommended_action per surface; audit detects one high-severity collision and assigns bind/fix_generator/demote_aliases dispositions.
failure_mode_prevented: Cold-agent first-contact collision with superseded or compatibility-evidence surfaces; silent authority-posture inflation as new generated surfaces accumulate without root-binding declarations.
notes: Audit fields: section, path, kind, root_handle, profile_id, substrate_snapshot_handle, freshness_sidecar, authority_posture, first_contact_collision, search_aliases_overlap_with_root, severity, recommended_action. Summary: 1 high, 8 medium, 18 low, 8 none across 35 surfaces. Repair classes: bind (6), fix_generator_then_bind (1), demote_aliases (1), redirect_or_retire (1).
one_line: Per-surface binding audit scores severity of first-contact alias overlap, root_handle presence, substrate fingerprint, and freshness sidecar across every self-description surface, with a typed disposition table (leave / bind / fix_generator_then_bind / demote_aliases / redirect_or_retire) keeping cold-agent landing disambiguation provably correct as generated surfaces proliferate.
title: Surface authority collision detection — first-contact alias drift audit
why_load_bearing: When 35 surfaces could answer 'what is this system', a route-health check cannot detect that a legacy compatibility surface still carries first-contact aliases that intercept a cold agent before the canonical root. The audit disposition table is the structural organ that closes this gap.

candidate_fixture: Toy ledger with capture_triage, execution_menu, missing_contracts, and one mechanism cluster; cluster_flag rows expose organizer_role, recommended_next_events, event_command_hints, and routing_scent_not_authority=true; attempts to mutate from the view are rejected in favor of task_ledger_apply events.
failure_mode_prevented: Agents treating a capture-heavy view as priority authority, duplicating command templates into every row, or acting on organizer scent as though it were source evidence.
notes: Sharpens task_ledger_workitem_spine. Cluster_flag is a contents page: repeated command templates are hoisted to event_command_hints; per-row source/file/integration detail is deferred to organizer-report or selected WorkItem flag/card rows; mechanism clusters are summarized by default and exact mechanism:<mech_id> rows require id drilldown.
one_line: A standard-owned option surface turns high-cardinality Task Ledger views into organizer-role cluster rows with supported event hints, conceptual gaps, and routing_scent_not_authority=true so agents can choose drilldowns without treating the browse surface as mutation or priority authority.
title: Task Ledger organizer routing surface
why_load_bearing: The ledger now has thousands of WorkItems. Without a role-bearing organizer surface, agents either grep raw projections or mistake capture volume for execution order. This organ preserves cheap browsing while keeping priority, mutation, and evidence authority in the event log, apply commands, organizer report, and selected flag/card rows.

candidate_fixture: Toy upstream/downstream pair where downstream is promoted ready while upstream is captured: schedulable_by_rank is empty, dependency_blocked names unsatisfied_dep_ids, dependency_anomalies emits ready_with_incomplete_hard_deps; after signoff or explicit dependency_resolutions waiver, downstream becomes schedulable and unlock edges change status.
failure_mode_prevented: Promoting or claiming work that is only rank-ready, not dependency-ready; silently treating retired dependencies as satisfied; losing the downstream value of completing a blocker.
notes: The row is an organ inside Task Ledger, not a separate scheduler daemon. Key fields: dependency_status.schedulable, hard_dep_count, satisfied_dep_ids, unsatisfied_dep_ids, dangling_dep_ids, upstream_dependency_edges, downstream_unlock_ids, downstream_unlock_edges.waiting_on_this, downstream_schedulable, downstream_unsatisfied_dep_ids, unlock_status, anomaly_refs.
one_line: The Task Ledger dependency reducer projects schedulability, upstream dependency edges, downstream unlock edges, anomaly rows, and unlocks_by_rank so agents can see which WorkItems are blocked, which completed upstreams unlock downstream work, and which ready rows are invalid because hard dependencies are unresolved.
title: WorkItem dependency unlock scheduler
why_load_bearing: Task order is not a rank sort. Dependency status makes ordering machine-readable: incomplete hard dependencies remove rows from schedulable_by_rank, retired upstreams require explicit resolution, cycles/dangling/self edges fail validation, and downstream unlock edges show whether completing one WorkItem actually frees another.

candidate_fixture: Toy ledger with one captured WorkItem lacking both satisfaction and integration contracts plus four terminal rows; missing_satisfaction_contract, missing_integration_contract, missing_contracts_ranked, and incomplete_work_items all select only the nonterminal incomplete row.
failure_mode_prevented: Executing or signing off a WorkItem whose served outcome or landing surface was never made explicit; repeatedly rediscovering the same missing-contract class from raw rows.
notes: Sharpens task_ledger_workitem_spine by extracting the contract-shaping organ. It feeds CAP reflex passes because missing contracts are often the safest improvement slice when implementation authority is unclear.
one_line: Contract-gap projections split incomplete work into missing_satisfaction_contract, missing_integration_contract, missing_contracts_ranked, and incomplete_work_items, while excluding terminal done/propagated/retired/signoff rows, so agents shape the missing contract class before execution.
title: WorkItem contract-gap triage views
why_load_bearing: A WorkItem can be captured, important, and still unexecutable. This organ keeps 'incomplete' from becoming an undifferentiated pile: satisfaction names what the work serves, integration names where it lands, ranked contract gaps make the next shaping move explicit, and terminal rows do not keep polluting the active gap view.

candidate_fixture: Toy cap universe with typed cap, cap-prefixed execution candidate, blocked future cap, cap-like nonprefixed row, and unknown cap; cap_census assigns namespace/temporal/semantic/readiness fields, cap_cartography emits clusters, LOD nodes, bounded edges, lineage, overflow warnings, unclassified_index, and source_route_metadata for drilldowns.
failure_mode_prevented: Treating a 2000+ CAP backlog as an undifferentiated queue; over-rendering the full graph in a front end; hiding unknown semantic roles or omitted edges as if the overview were complete.
notes: Current live summary: cap_universe_count 2339, integration_grounded_count 1495, integration_missing cluster 819, proof_backed_count 493, unclassified_count 19, overview_complete=false with explicit edge_limit warning. These counts are dated evidence, not stable doctrine.
one_line: cap_census and cap_cartography classify the CAP-like universe by namespace kind, temporal role, semantic role, proof refs, integration readiness, current operating-picture membership, bounded graph edges, lineage, overflow, and source-route metadata, turning thousands of captures into a map-ready readiness surface.
title: CAP cartography readiness map
why_load_bearing: CAP pressure is too large to browse as rows. This organ makes autonomous cap-working possible by separating proof-backed, integration-grounded, integration-missing, current-mission, nonprefixed-cap-like, and unclassified pressure while keeping the overview bounded and each cluster drilldown recoverable to source rows.

candidate_fixture: Toy projection with one active mission_blackboard row, one foreground meta_mission with imagined_state_refs, one execution-menu candidate, and one anchor row; mission_operating_picture buckets them, adds serves and missing_umbrella edges, and rolls up prompt-ledger current_receipt_ref with safety_authority=false.
failure_mode_prevented: Mistaking a prompt trace dry-run or phase label for live safety authority; missing that a current mission lacks umbrella/imagined-state refs; losing active mission-blackboard rows outside Task Ledger rank views.
notes: Live surface currently buckets runtime_active, foreground_mission, execution_candidate, candidate_meta_mission_capture, and anchor_or_registry_row, and reports missing_umbrella_refs with candidate_sources_to_check. Prompt trace rollup carries current_receipt_ref and next_safe_action but explicitly safety_authority=false.
one_line: A graph-shaped read model composes active WorkItems, active mission-blackboard rows, foreground meta-missions, schedulable execution candidates, candidate meta-mission captures, anchor rows, umbrella gaps, dependency edges, and a prompt-ledger mission trace overlay that is explicitly derived_not_safety_authority.
title: Mission operating picture currentness overlay
why_load_bearing: Currentness is not the same as phase prose, prompt trace, or rank. This organ prevents cold agents from asking the wrong source 'what is live now' by collecting current mission buckets and missing umbrella refs while preserving authority boundaries: prompt-ledger trace is useful context, but the mission operating picture says it is not safety authority.

candidate_fixture: Toy mechanism index with two mech rows and five WorkItems; cluster_flag default emits only compact top clusters, --ids mechanism:<mech_id> emits a row with mechanism_id, match_reason_counts, top_ids, mechanism_drilldown_command, and routing_scent_not_authority=true; vague unmatched rows land in unclassified_pressure.
failure_mode_prevented: Mechanism-related WorkItems remaining invisible until a human remembers the right term; agents creating a parallel mechanism backlog; default cluster pages exploding with every mechanism row.
notes: Live default overview reports 35 available mechanism clusters; top pressure clusters include mech_002 Typed Receipt Validation, mech_016 Doctrine Self-Improvement Loop, and mech_034 Failure-Class Propagation Packet. Counts are route evidence, not stable doctrine.
one_line: The Task Ledger option surface deterministically clusters WorkItems against existing mech_* doctrine rows by direct refs, title/tag/token overlap, and generic mechanism pressure, emitting compact mechanism:<mech_id> overviews and exact drilldowns without making mechanisms a second backlog.
title: Mechanism-affinity WorkItem clusters
why_load_bearing: Self-improvement work needs to know which doctrine mechanisms are under pressure. This organ lets a cold agent see, for example, Typed Receipt Validation or Failure-Class Propagation WorkItem orbits without grepping every row or minting duplicate mechanism TODOs. It keeps mechanism JSON and WorkItem events as separate authorities.

candidate_fixture: Toy WorkItem with two owned paths; begin opens a Work Ledger thread, claims the WorkItem, both paths, and the td thread, emits read/write hashes plus idempotency key, and a repeated begin returns already_open without touching Task Ledger events or Git index.
failure_mode_prevented: Agents editing first and reconstructing ownership later; stale base-head proof; missing read receipt; unclaimed paths; duplicated landing attempts; closeout with no td thread to reconcile.
notes: Sharpens the older mission_transaction_landing row by extracting the pre-mutation binding organ. Its authority explicitly excludes Task Ledger event mutation and Git index mutation; it uses Work Ledger runtime/session/thread authority.
one_line: A pre-mutation WorkItem landing attempt binds exactly one subject, base HEAD, owned path set, Work Ledger session/read receipt, td thread, work item/path/thread claims, read_set_hash, write_set_hash, and idempotency key before source edits touch the substrate.
title: Work Landing attempt binding
why_load_bearing: This turns 'I am about to edit and later commit' into a named transaction root. Without it, a validated slice can lose its subject, claim authority, base evidence root, or finalizer thread and become only chat memory in a dirty multi-agent tree.

candidate_fixture: Toy admission packet: missing attempt returns can_write=false and required_next_command=work_landing.py begin; active attempt with claims returns ready_to_write; released path claim, finalized session, or external-only WorkItem blocks with typed reasons.
failure_mode_prevented: Autonomous agents acting on non-agent/external WorkItems, editing with finalized sessions, editing unclaimed paths, or treating an operator/signoff selection as write authority.
notes: Distinct from work_landing_attempt_binding: begin creates/binds the attempt; admission is the pre-edit decision surface a future write-capable agent should call before mutating.
one_line: A read-only admission check decides whether a write-capable agent may edit a WorkItem path by requiring an agent-executable subject, active Work Landing attempt, open td thread, live session, bound owned paths, active claims, and no claim collisions.
title: WorkItem write admission gate
why_load_bearing: It makes autonomous cap-working less dependent on instruction memory. The gate blocks editing from a bare WorkItem id or stale session and returns the exact next command instead of relying on the agent to remember the ceremony.

candidate_fixture: Toy landing with commit_hash def456; dry-run shows record_scoped_commit_landing then receipt intake then closeout. First apply writes progress, drains the exact receipt, closes the td thread, finalizes the session, releases claims; second apply reports already_done.
failure_mode_prevented: Declaring done after commit only; duplicated receipts; unreleased claims; stale open Work Ledger threads; manual closeout steps that cannot be safely replayed.
notes: Distinct from task_ledger_exact_receipt_drain: exact receipt drain is one finalizer; reconcile is the broader action-plan organ that decides and applies the landing lifecycle.
one_line: The landing controller projects ordered action rows for commit verification, Work Ledger progress, scoped commit binding, Task Ledger receipt intake/drain, td closeout, projection checks, session finalization, claim release, and convergence recomputation, with apply_status, blocked_by, would_mutate, evidence_if_already_done, and idempotent single-action application.
title: Work Landing reconcile finalizer plan
why_load_bearing: A commit hash is not a closed WorkItem. This organ makes post-commit finalizers visible and replay-safe, so closeout becomes a state machine instead of a memory of commands the agent hoped it ran.

candidate_fixture: Toy repo where other_session owns alpha.py; current agent edits alpha.py and calls begin. The result is blocked_but_capsuled with manifest, patch.diff, handoff.md, apply_check_result=passed, adoption_target=other_session, and next_allowed_actions limited to owner adoption/release/wait/supersede.
failure_mode_prevented: Losing a validated or useful diff behind an active claim; committing over someone else's path claim; handing off only a prose summary with no patch SHA or replay command.
notes: This is not public release packaging; it is a private continuity organ for concurrent claims. The proof_staleness_note is essential: the capsule proves patch replayability at creation time, not current validity.
one_line: When Work Landing begin hits an exclusive Work Ledger claim collision but the owned paths have a scoped diff, the controller writes a blocked_patch_capsule with patch, manifest, handoff, base HEAD, current head, patch SHA, apply-check result, blocking claim/session ids, adoption target, proof staleness note, and replay commands.
title: Blocked patch capsule claim handoff
why_load_bearing: It preserves valid work at the moment concurrency makes immediate landing unsafe. Instead of broad staging, waiting in chat, or losing the diff, the substrate turns the blocked slice into an adoptable, hash-addressed handoff owned by the claim holder or future reentry lane.

candidate_fixture: Toy validated slice where scoped commit is blocked by Git metadata authority; context-pack selects cogop_landing_handoff_compiler, validator requires the seven landing_handoff_contract fields, and the dogfood receipt stores a reentry packet with exact owned paths and validation commands.
failure_mode_prevented: Validated work stranded in chat; future agents lacking exact owned paths or validation evidence; generic dirty-tree summaries replacing a reentry rule; route pack missing the operator for commit-blocked prompts.
notes: This complements blocked_patch_capsule_claim_handoff: blocked_patch_capsule preserves a concrete diff under claim collision; landing_handoff_compiler is the cognitive operator for validated slices blocked by commit authority, Git metadata, index, concurrency, or finalizer blockers.
one_line: A governed cognitive operator activates for validated-but-unlanded slices and requires a machine-readable handoff receipt with landing blocker, owned path set, validation evidence, commit authority requirement, handoff packet, status binding, and reentry rule before an agent yields.
title: Landing handoff compiler operator
why_load_bearing: It changes agent metabolism under failure: a commit-blocked validated slice cannot collapse into apologetic prose. The operator makes stranded work recoverable by future agents and route-selectable through context-pack.

candidate_fixture: Seed with a context-pack emitting a route_lease_v0; fixture verifies agent names lease id, selected lane, direct action boundary, and validation return condition before taking action; rejects a broad kernel call as the next move.
failure_mode_prevented: Repeated broad kernel calls after lane selection; navigation-as-latency-debt without action; missing forbidden_followup_routes causing scope creep after lane selection.
notes: Extension contract requires: route_lease_id, lease_selected_lane, direct_action_boundary, consumed_by_action, forbidden_followup_routes, validation_return_condition. Landmine: a route lease is a control-plane handoff, not permission to keep browsing.
one_line: When entry or context-pack emits a route lease, the agent converts that lease into a bounded data-plane action with a named validation return condition instead of repeating broad navigation without a new authority question.
title: Route Lease Executor
why_load_bearing: Agents trained on broad kernel calls before action accumulate navigation latency debt and never consume the selected lane. The Route Lease operator makes the lease-to-action boundary explicit and validator-enforced, converting routing from a repeated overhead into a one-shot control-plane handoff. Without it, process-audit shows broad_route_repeated_without_new_authority_question indefinitely.

candidate_fixture: Pulse exposes 3 competing pressure surfaces; fixture confirms agent selects one, names rejected alternatives with axes, names status-binding target, and runs the bounded action without expanding the others first.
failure_mode_prevented: Broad multi-surface audit loops without a selected action; status not bound after acting on pressure; most-visible backlog selected instead of highest-compounding-reversible.
notes: Extension contract requires: pressure_surfaces, selected_pressure, rejected_pressures, decision_axes, bounded_action, status_binding_target, validation_return_condition. Landmine: a high count is not an action.
one_line: When live ledgers, queues, receipts, or patches expose broad competing pressure, the agent reduces the surfaces to one bounded reversible status-bound action before expanding another broad audit.
title: Pressure-to-Action Reducer
why_load_bearing: Without a validated pressure-selection operator, agents facing multi-surface backlogs keep auditing pressure indefinitely or pick the most-visible surface rather than the highest-compounding reversible one. The reducer requires that selected and rejected pressures both be retained with decision axes and a status-binding target — preventing both inaction and undirected thrashing.

candidate_fixture: Seed proposes a new operator with registry at 4+ active rows; fixture checks agent runs novelty test, names nearest existing operators with contract coverage, chooses install/merge/extend/cap/reject, and names a future reentry rule.
failure_mode_prevented: Cognitive operator sprawl through undifferentiated new rows; capability gap ladder selecting without novelty check; autonomous seed accumulating operators without merge/extend evaluation.
notes: Five decisions (install/merge/extend/cap/reject) with evidence — not a veto-only slogan. Landmine: adding a new operator can be failure to reuse the stack.
one_line: Before an autonomous seed installs another cognitive operator, a novelty gate decides install/merge/extend/cap/reject by checking nearest existing operators and requiring a future-reentry rule so the same decision is not re-derived next seed.
title: Operator Accretion Governor
why_load_bearing: Repeated autonomous seed passes risk accumulating operators that differ only by name, silently diluting the reasoning substrate. The governor is the only enforced mechanism that checks nearest existing operators and a novelty test before installation — and requires a future reentry rule. Without it, the cognitive_operators registry becomes documentation-only operator sprawl.

candidate_fixture: Scoped commit blocked by Git metadata; fixture verifies agent names exact owned paths, validation evidence command+result, authority requirement, status binding in Task Ledger, and reentry commands — and does NOT broad-stage or summarize in chat only.
failure_mode_prevented: Validated work described in chat only and lost across session boundary; next agent broad-staging to resolve a known owned-path slice; closeout without status binding for unlanded validated work.
notes: Distinct from AttentionFrame (stale reads) and Task Ledger cap (future work) — this is specifically for validated unlanded slices.
one_line: When validated work cannot be committed because of commit authority, protected Git metadata, concurrency, or finalizer blockers, the agent compiles a machine-readable handoff packet (blocker, owned paths, validation evidence, authority requirement, status binding, reentry rule) before yielding.
title: Landing Handoff Compiler
why_load_bearing: Validated-but-unlanded slices are the most expensive failure mode in a solo-dev Git repo: the work is done, tests pass, but the next agent starts from scratch because the stranded state was only described in chat. The handoff operator is the only substrate that converts an impasse into a durable reentry packet.

candidate_fixture: 3 external candidates with different profiles: one commodity lib (library-swap), one architecture-carrying repo (annex-for-pattern-transfer), one already evaluated and rejected; classifier routes each correctly; rejected has rejection_reason and reevaluation_trigger; fourth pair tests symbiosis check.
failure_mode_prevented: Commodity libraries routed through annex_discovery and hoarded as annexes; prior rejection forgotten causing re-evaluation churn; symbiotic candidate pairs adopted in isolation; axis overlap creating duplicate coverage.
notes: The D6 row in CLAUDE.md names routing vocabulary; the organ itself is the decision record + unrecorded-rejection prevention. Failure modes named in external_adoption_routing.md anti-patterns: route-everything-to-annex-discovery, unrecorded-rejections, axis-collision, symbiosis-blindness.
one_line: Before annexing any external candidate, classify it into one of six adoption lanes (library-swap, annex-for-pattern-transfer, upgrade-ours, hybrid, reject, substrate-wedge) and emit an immutable decision record — preventing re-evaluation loops and axis collisions across agent sessions.
title: External adoption pre-triage routing with decision record
why_load_bearing: Without pre-triage, every external candidate is funnelled into annex_discovery, producing either substrate hoarding (commodity libs become annexes) or missed doctrine (pattern-carrying repos stay invisible). The decision record makes routing irreversible per candidate: a future agent cannot silently re-evaluate without seeing the prior decision, reason, and re-evaluation trigger.

candidate_fixture: One annex passes all five preconditions (structural overlap, interacting invariants, MIT licence, no existing local artifact, refactor plan); verify wedge commit message includes annex slug + upstream SHA + licence; verify ≥2 per-board replacement commits with provenance; verify annex remains in annexes/ as historical reference. Negative: AGPL-licensed annex with identical fit is blocked and falls back to Lane 2.
failure_mode_prevented: Wedge-and-freeze (substrate installed without per-board refactor plan); AGPL/GPL silently pulling repo under copyleft; mega-commit Theseus-ship losing per-board audit trail; Lane 6 chosen for mature owner-maintained libraries.
notes: Lane 6 deliberately excluded from std_annex_distillation.json pattern_row_shape.adoption_lane (1-5 only). Theseus-ship metaphor is operator-voiced. Maturity heuristic 2026-04-24: Lane 6 fits young tooling we want to OWN; mature libraries should go Lane 4 HYBRID.
one_line: When an annex's structural fit is high enough that board-by-board pattern-transfer is busywork, install the full upstream substrate as one wedge commit, bind seams, then iteratively replace boards one commit at a time until the code is wholly ours — gated by a five-question precondition check and a hard licence block on strong-copyleft candidates.
title: Substrate-wedge Theseus-ship lifecycle with licence gate
why_load_bearing: This is the only annex lane that installs external substrate into the live repo tree. Without the five-question gate and per-board refactor discipline, the lane collapses into substrate hoarding dressed as assimilation (wedge-and-freeze anti-pattern). The licence gate prevents AGPL/GPL substrate from pulling the combined work under copyleft.

candidate_fixture: Worker bundle with 5 pattern rows: 3 clean, 1 flagged uncertain, 1 lane-6 wedge attempt. Verify: 3 clean rows become adoption_status=evaluated; flagged row stays proposed; lane-6 row rejected with note routing it to annex_install_and_refactor. Verify placeholder distillation.json is valid intermediate state.
failure_mode_prevented: Proposal-theater (distillation rows stalling at 'proposed' indefinitely); lane-6 stuffed into distillation pattern rows losing wedge audit trail; placeholder rows treated as bugs triggering false repair waves.
notes: Operator-voiced 2026-04-25, family-09 raw seed: agents will not audit AI-mined rows row-by-row, so proposed-awaiting-operator-promotion is gating theater. Controller-merge IS the trust gate.
one_line: Controller-merge of a worker's distillation bundle promotes pattern rows to 'evaluated' by default — not 'proposed' — and validates adoption_lane as 1-5 (lane 6 is a WorkItem, not a row value), preserving placeholder rows as a valid pipeline stage so scan results survive the session without requiring operator audit of every row.
title: Annex distillation controller-merge trust default with placeholder lifecycle
why_load_bearing: Without the trust-default inversion (evaluated not proposed), the distillation layer becomes a parking lot requiring operator approval that never comes — gating theater. Without lane-6-as-WorkItem enforcement, agents stuff substrate-wedge decisions into pattern rows, losing the lifecycle contract that makes wedge commits auditable.

candidate_fixture: One annexed repo with known high-value pattern; verify wave: (1) checks git status for sibling-agent overlap before starting, (2) selects exactly one local seam from cross-axis priority cohort, (3) implements and validates one concrete artifact change, (4) deposits one protocol lesson back into the generalized skill, (5) rewrites autonomous seed. Negative: sibling agent holds target path → wave aborts with carve-out note.
failure_mode_prevented: Indefinite reading loop ('use this annex' never lands a proof); concurrent agents silently writing same files; protocol lesson dying with session; premature parallelization spreading divergent protocol versions.
notes: Sequential-not-parallel constraint operator-voiced: run sequentially until the generalized protocol quiesces (N back-to-back instances produce no closeout edits), then parallelize.
one_line: Given an annex or external source, execute one controller-owned pattern-to-proof wave that identifies the highest-value local seam, implements a bounded verifiable improvement, up-propagates the reusable protocol rule, and rewrites the autonomous seed — blocking wave start if a sibling agent holds overlapping substrate.
title: Annex system improvement: one bounded wave with seed rewrite and inter-agent substrate check
why_load_bearing: This lane turns distillation candidates into actual local artifacts. Without the 'one bounded wave' contract, 'use this annex' instructions produce indefinite reading loops that never land a proof. Without the inter-agent substrate collision check, concurrent agents corrupt each other's dirty trees. Without the seed rewrite, each wave's protocol improvement dies with the session.

candidate_fixture: Toy memory write attempt; gate checks five invariants and rejects three of four candidate writes; only the user-identity carve-out (user identity/hardware/contact, ~3 files) passes.
failure_mode_prevented: Shadow doctrine accumulation (private memory silently duplicating gated plane artifacts); cross-host invisibility (Claude-private writes unreachable by Codex/world-model server); ungated doctrine mutation bypassing dry-run/plan-review/bidirectional-edge validation.
notes: Carve-out (user identity / hardware / contact, ~3 files) is the only valid memory/ write. 'Shadow doctrine' names ungated content that looks like memory but never gates through apply lanes.
one_line: Before any Write→memory/, the agent checks five plane invariants (par_* anchors, bidirectional edges, controlled routing vocabulary, cross-host visibility, apply-lane gating) that private memory breaks — ungated writes produce shadow doctrine that silently accumulates duplicates of gated plane artifacts.
title: Plane-first memory gate — five-invariant shadow-doctrine prevention
why_load_bearing: Without explicit invariant enumeration, the path of least resistance (Write→memory/) is undetectable as a violation. The five-invariant gate converts an invisible failure mode (shadow doctrine) into a checkable pre-condition. All five invariants are load-bearing: anchors trace content to operator voice; edges allow graph traversal; routing makes content discoverable; cross-host visibility lets Codex and the world-model server read it; apply-lane gating validates against the doctrine graph.

candidate_fixture: 6 candidate would-be-memory items; tree routes one to user-identity carve-out, one to raw_seed (Will-voice), two to existing paper modules, one to a skill, one to ephemeral (nowhere); 8-row table cites destination plane per content kind.
failure_mode_prevented: Duplicate artifact authoring (skipping existing-artifact check); Will-voice transcription into memory without par_* anchor (voice-theft); ephemeral-as-durable confusion.
notes: 8 destinations: named-move→skill; subsystem-fact→paper module; schema→standard; architectural-fact-with-edges→concept/mechanism apply-lane; Will-voice→raw_seed; Claude-voice→agent_seed via --append-agent-seed; external-pattern→annex note; runtime-state→agent_bootstrap.json. Session-ephemeral→nowhere.
one_line: Any candidate 'would-be memory' content passes a 6-step decision tree (user-identity carve-out → Will-voice → existing artifact → paper module → skill → ephemeral) and an 8-row assimilation table mapping content kind to authoring-skill destination before any Write→memory/ executes.
title: Memory routing decision tree — 6-step pre-write lookup + 8-row assimilation table
why_load_bearing: Without the routing table, every agent turn re-derives 'where does this learning go?' and the cheapest answer is Write→memory/. The 6-step tree enforces a fixed resolution order (carve-out first, ephemeral last) that closes the question. The 8-row table makes the plane destination explicit and citeable. Skipping the 'does an existing artifact already cover this?' step is the most common failure: it authors duplicate artifacts.

candidate_fixture: Three synthetic intake cases: (A) Will-direct prose with intentional spirals/typos; (B) AI summarizing operator gesture; (C) AI utterance-paste. Validator accepts (A) as-voiced, accepts (B) as gesture-into-clarity, rejects (C) with anchor missing.
failure_mode_prevented: Voice-theft (AI paraphrases operator voice without par_* anchor); utterance-paste confusion (chat message as seed); voice-signal erasure (AI cleans up Will's directly-authored prose).
notes: Three wrong shapes: (A) voice-theft (AI paraphrase with no par_* anchor); (B) mechanical transcription (raw spiral voice verbatim in paper-module body); (C) utterance-paste (AI copies a chat message verbatim into raw_seed.md). Right shape: Will-direct → raw_seed as-voiced; AI-mediated → gesture-into-technical-clarity; both → paper module distills intent with motifs preserved, anchored to par_*.
one_line: Raw-seed intake posture is determined by the writer, not a fixed rule: when Will authors directly, preserve as-voiced (typos, spirals, reversals are signal); when AI mediates an append, transcribe gesture into technical clarity — verbatim utterance-paste into raw_seed.md is the wrong shape in both directions.
title: Writer-conditional voice discipline — as-voiced vs gesture-into-clarity by author
why_load_bearing: Collapsing the two postures into one rule in either direction produces canonical failure modes: 'always preserve as-voiced' leads AI-mediated appends to paste chat utterances verbatim (confusing access-path with seed); 'always rewrite into clarity' leads Claude to clean up Will's direct voice and erase the voice-signal. The writer-conditional discriminator is the only invariant that handles both correctly.

candidate_fixture: Toy session with 20 tool calls; rolling deque records {path, verb, result_hash, ts}; step-decider fires on edit-without-prior-read of known-substrate path and returns one advisory substrate reference; injection surface is hook_injection via runtime_hook.py PostToolUse.
failure_mode_prevented: Repeated reactive re-fetch (long sessions repeatedly asking for same paper module); context pressure from re-discovery; authoritative-injection mistake breaking reread invariant.
notes: Active tier proposal: rolling N=20 tool-call deque; step-decider fires after K calls or on signals (error retry, repeated-path ≥3 in window, edit-without-prior-read on known-substrate path); returns at most one substrate reference per fire. Adapted from aperant prepareStep/StepMemoryState; not imported wholesale.
one_line: Memory injection splits into three tiers: passive (bootstrap system-prompt assembly, once per session), reactive (agent-initiated substrate fetch via kernel.py --paper-module/--skill-find, on demand), and active (runtime watches rolling N tool-call window and proactively injects relevant substrate per step — not yet implemented); the active tier is the highest-leverage missing primitive.
title: Three-tier memory injection — passive/reactive/active with active not yet built
why_load_bearing: Passive and reactive tiers both require the agent to already know what to fetch. The active tier closes the gap where a long session repeatedly re-fetches the same paper module reactively because no runtime watches tool-call paths and surfaces substrate before the agent has to ask. The advisory-not-authoritative invariant is load-bearing: making active injection authoritative would break the reread-substrate axiom and produce pre-compressed rather than reread substrate.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic agent append via --append-agent-seed writes to agent_seed.md with par_agent_* id and authored_by/source_substrate fields; raw_seed.md left untouched; downstream tooling distinguishes the two by id prefix and grep.
failure_mode_prevented: Authority collapse (AI-authored sections indistinguishable from operator voice and treated as doctrine); solo doctrine minting (agent prose proposing principles without operator review); AI voice pollution of operator-only substrate.
notes: The substrate split solves authority collapse without deleting AI-authored history: agent prose still gets ids and enters metabolism lanes, but under separate file, ids, provenance, and posture. Migration lane (--migrate-agent-section) moves historical agent sections.
one_line: AI-authored durable seed prose lives in a separate family-scoped agent_seed.md file with par_agent_*/sec_agent_* ids, required authored_by and source_substrate=agent_seed fields, and a sanctioned CLI path (--append-agent-seed), preventing AI voice from polluting raw_seed.md's operator-only namespace and making agent-origin content distinguishable at the id level.
title: Agent-seed substrate isolation — separate AI-voice namespace
why_load_bearing: Without the separation, AI-authored sections inside raw_seed.md receive the same par_* namespace and are structurally indistinguishable from operator voice to downstream tooling — creating authority collapse where agent synthesis is treated as operator-authority-equivalent. The grep-distinguishable id namespace (par_agent_* vs par_*) is the machine-checkable enforcement.

candidate_fixture: Toy repo with two concurrent commit attempts to same branch; first lands via private index + CAS; second's update-ref fails atomically because expected_parent no longer matches HEAD; second retries with fresh parent; multi-hunk full-path ownership refused without --allow-multi-hunk-full-paths.
failure_mode_prevented: Silent last-write-wins HEAD advance when two agents commit to same branch concurrently; shared-index contamination from unrelated dirty staged paths; multi-hunk full-path ownership ambiguity in concurrently dirty files.
notes: Three subcommands (full-paths, patch, tracked-removals) share the private-index + CAS invariant. Remote-fallback lane (temp checkout, push commit object, never writes live .git) is a fourth mode for sandbox-blocked environments. Work Ledger mutation guard runs inside every non-dry-run path.
one_line: Stages declared paths into a temporary GIT_INDEX_FILE, writes a tree, and lands via update-ref CAS — so another actor advancing HEAD fails the commit atomically rather than silently overwriting concurrent work.
title: Scoped commit via private index + HEAD CAS
why_load_bearing: The CAS (update-ref <branch> <new_sha> <expected_parent>) is the only mechanism that makes concurrent commit attempts fail explicitly instead of producing a silent last-write-wins merge. Without it, two agents racing to commit the same branch interleave their trees non-deterministically. The private index also prevents shared-index contamination from unrelated dirty staged paths riding along.

candidate_fixture: Toy session acquires a claim with leased_until=now+1h; simulated crash; sweep marks expired_at=leased_until with release_reason=lease_expired; subsequent actor sees expired record (not nothing) and can audit prior state; bounded recent_sweep_events log shows the expiry event.
failure_mode_prevented: Ghost claims from crashed sessions permanently blocking unrelated actors; post-crash re-entry appearing clean when prior session had in-flight path ownership; collision math counting stale leases as live contention.
notes: Pattern transferred from Graphiti bi-temporal facts (valid_at/invalid_at/expired_at). Claim lifecycle: active → expired (lease_expired) → released. Orphan sweep auto-finalizes sessions after 24h and triggers claim expiry. Bounded recent_sweep_events log (cap 50) makes sweep pressure observable.
one_line: Work Ledger claims carry a leased_until window; a crash or abandoned session leaves released_at=null and expired_at=leased_until rather than silently deleting the claim, so collision math can distinguish live vs stale leases and the sweep reconstructs what was held.
title: Bi-temporal claim lease with explicit expired-at audit trail
why_load_bearing: Silent claim deletion after a crash would make post-crash re-entry look clean even when the prior session had dirty state in flight. The explicit expired_at preserves the 'was-claimed-but-never-released' signal so subsequent actors can audit whether a concurrent session actually completed cleanly before assuming ownership.

candidate_fixture: Toy session-start snapshot shows path P as blocked by claim C; before commit, concurrent landing releases C and cleans P; live re-check via active_claim_collisions_for_paths returns empty; agent replans (proceeds) rather than escalating; severity ladder via --fail-on-status confirms 'clear'.
failure_mode_prevented: False conflict escalation when a concurrent actor already finished; commit paralysis from stale snapshot treated as authoritative; missing the 'already landed' case where re-running would duplicate commits.
notes: CLAUDE.md names the protocol explicitly. Code enforces it: active_claim_collisions_for_paths calls load_runtime_status fresh on every invocation (not cached). The --fail-on-status flag operationalizes the severity ladder (clear/watch/review/blocked/hard_stop).
one_line: After a concurrent landing, the agent re-runs git status and Work Ledger claim checks against the current state rather than escalating from the stale session-start snapshot — if the path is already clean or the claim already released, the correct action is replan, not conflict escalation.
title: Same-path blocker live re-check: replan vs escalate from session-start snapshot
why_load_bearing: Session-start snapshots are stale by definition in a concurrent multi-agent tree. Escalating from them produces false conflicts: an actor that finished just after the session started looks like a live blocker. The live re-check prevents the false-conflict failure mode and is the only way to confirm a same-path blocker is real vs already resolved.

candidate_fixture: Toy entangled dirty tree that cannot be scoped cleanly; rescue_ref_snapshot writes the full tree as a commit object under refs/aiw/rescue/dirty-tree/<run_id>; main untouched; build_landing_bankruptcy_summary recommends the conservative_rescue_command (not the operator_authorized_broad_checkpoint).
failure_mode_prevented: Loss of in-progress work when dirty tree cannot be cleanly scoped; advancing main with unclassified or entangled dirty paths; using broad-checkpoint lane (which pushes to main) when operator only authorized conservative preservation.
notes: Rescue ref written to refs/aiw/rescue/dirty-tree/<timestamp>-<head_prefix>-<random> via git update-ref. Manifest stored as a tree object (not a tracked file commit). conservative_rescue_command is './checkpoint --rescue-ref --message ...' distinct from operator_authorized_broad_checkpoint './checkpoint --arbiter ...'.
one_line: When a dirty tree cannot be cleanly scoped or committed, the rescue-ref lane preserves the full worktree state as a Git commit object under refs/aiw/rescue/dirty-tree/<run_id> without advancing main or touching the shared index, leaving a recoverable audit trail.
title: Dirty-tree rescue ref — non-advancing git object preservation
why_load_bearing: The checkpoint (broad-save) lane advances main and pushes. The rescue-ref lane is the conservative preservation primitive that keeps the dirty state recoverable as a Git object without disturbing any concurrent actor's HEAD tracking, branch state, or landing in progress. It is the fallback of last resort before the operator-authorized broad checkpoint.

candidate_fixture: Toy session silent for 5h is excluded from contention math (orphan_hide); separate session silent for 25h triggers auto_orphan_sweep which releases its claim, sets ended_at, and appends one event to recent_sweep_events; log capped at 50.
failure_mode_prevented: Ghost claims from crashed sessions permanently blocking unrelated path-scoped commits; contention math treating 4h-silent sessions as live coordination peers; unbounded sweep log growth making sweep pressure invisible.
notes: build_active_claims_snapshot filters effective_active_sessions by _is_orphaned_active_session so orphaned sessions do not contribute. Sweep events carry session_id, claim_id, td_id, leased_until for post-hoc audit.
one_line: Sessions silent for >4h are hidden from live contention math (orphan_hide); after 24h they are auto-finalized via auto_orphan_sweep which releases held claims, sets ended_at, and appends a bounded recent_sweep_events entry — preventing ghost sessions from permanently blocking unrelated actors.
title: Orphaned-session auto-sweep with bounded sweep event log
why_load_bearing: Without auto-sweep, a crashed agent session holds its path claims indefinitely. The 4h/24h two-threshold design separates 'stop counting in contention math' (cheap, reversible) from 'finalize and release claims' (writes the ledger). The bounded sweep log (cap 50) makes sweep pressure observable without full ledger scan.

candidate_fixture: Toy host metrics: clear (low pressure), watch (high memory), blocked (disk-near-full). Pressure status drives worker_budget.max_parallel_local_processes; live probes are suppressed in watch/blocked states; emits pressure.status, pressure.reasons, pressure.strategy.
failure_mode_prevented: OOM from unbounded parallel pytest during high memory pressure; deadlock from broad rebuild during disk-near-full; silent live-probe calls during watch/blocked state consuming rate-limit budget.
notes: Schemas command_budget_guidance_v0 and local_resource_snapshot_v0 already versioned. Closely adjacent to resource_typed_capacity (existing, which covers provider-side capacity); this covers local-machine pressure that gates how many workers can be spawned safely.
one_line: Classifies local system pressure as clear/watch/blocked, derives a bounded worker-count and per-mode command budget, and prevents parallel rebuilds or live probes when the machine is constrained.
title: Resource-pressure command-budget guidance organ
why_load_bearing: Without this organ, worker dispatch ignores disk/memory/CPU saturation and can deadlock or OOM the host. The three-tier pressure ladder translates raw system metrics into typed action constraints that every downstream command mode consults.

candidate_fixture: Toy transform job runs three times: first dispatches and caches output; second returns cache hit (no provider call); third with same fingerprint but a failed prior status produces blocked_duplicate receipt; force=True bypasses both gates.
failure_mode_prevented: Duplicate provider calls draining rate-limit quota; non-deterministic receipt proliferation for identical jobs; race condition between concurrent harness instances on the same transform job.
notes: Two distinct mechanisms share the gate: cache by cache_key returning prior row patch; fingerprint by provider+model+cache_key+prompt_hash blocking re-run of any job that previously failed. FAILED_STATUSES = {empty_response, schema_fail, 429, 5xx, timeout, worker_error, policy_reject}.
one_line: Prevents redundant provider calls by gating each transform job through a content-addressed fingerprint (provider+model+cache_key+prompt_hash) and a separate output cache, blocking re-runs of known-failed jobs and returning cached row patches on hit.
title: Worker run fingerprint dedup and cache-hit gate
why_load_bearing: Without this gate, every metabolism loop iteration re-dispatches identical prompts to rate-limited providers, burning quota and producing non-deterministic duplicate receipts. The fingerprint status machine also detects in-flight races between concurrent harness instances.

candidate_fixture: Toy controller considers three candidates: one skipped (low heat), one no-op (already covered), one vetoed (policy_reject). Three deduped receipts written by SHA-256 dedup key; HarnessResult.row_patch is None for all three; subsequent loop iterations do not duplicate receipts.
failure_mode_prevented: Ghost work (controller believes job never considered when it was skipped); skip-receipt proliferation across loop iterations; audit gap (no evidence for veto reasoning).
notes: Schema std_candidate_skip_receipt_v1. Receipt kinds: skip / no_op / veto. Dedup key is SHA-256 over {schema_version, receipt_kind, candidate_id, source_fingerprint, reason, hard_vetoes}. governor_mode and cpu_gate_state are first-class fields so audit surface knows what state caused the skip.
one_line: Persists a content-addressed skip, no-op, or veto receipt for candidate work the always-on loop deliberately did not run, making low-heat non-decisions replayable and auditable without producing row patches.
title: Worker skip/veto deduped no-op receipt lane
why_load_bearing: Without this organ, controller loops have no evidence that work was considered and deliberately skipped (vs never seen). Deduplication prevents skip-receipt proliferation across loop iterations; the hard_vetoes field is the typed reason set the audit surface can query.

candidate_fixture: Toy provider receipts simulating each of the 7 failure classes; classifier routes each to its review outcome; truth-side leakage scan detects TRUTH_SIDE_FORBIDDEN_MARKERS and routes to SOLUTION_LEAKAGE; axiom classification CLEAN + Lean PASS jointly required for acceptance.
failure_mode_prevented: Solution-leakage promoted as advisory signal; premise-budget violations fed to repair loop instead of re-tried with corrected context; undeclared library priors silently promoted; all Lean failures collapsed to generic retry signal losing failure-class signal.
notes: Seven failure classes; four review outcomes. Truth-side leakage detection scans provider output for forbidden markers. Axiom classification 'CLEAN' is a joint condition with Lean PASS for acceptance.
one_line: Runs a provider proof-body hypothesis through the Lean verifier, classifies the result into one of seven typed failure classes (NONE, PROVIDER_CONTRACT_FAIL, SOLUTION_LEAKAGE, PREMISE_BUDGET_VIOLATION, UNDECLARED_LIBRARY_PRIOR, PREMISE_RETRIEVAL_MISS, PROOF_SYNTHESIS_FAIL), and routes each class to a distinct review outcome (accept_as_advisory / reject / retry / bridge_escalate).
title: Lean verifier feedback loop with failure classification
why_load_bearing: Without typed failure classification, all Lean rejections collapse into a single retry signal and the system cannot distinguish premise-retrieval misses (fixable by context recipe) from solution leakage (reject-and-quarantine) from undeclared library priors (bridge-escalate) from synthesis failures (feed foundry learning). The classification is the prerequisite for every downstream repair, learning, or promotion decision.

candidate_fixture: Toy proof attempts in three lanes: one-shot hammer (LEAN_ACCEPTED_PROOF), tactic-transition search (TACTIC_TRANSITION), oracle-repair (ORACLE_REPAIR); foundry_proof_state_learning_rows.json keyed by status class; blind-policy variant enforces problem-id blindness.
failure_mode_prevented: Oracle-repair and one-shot-hammer success conflated in foundry learning rows; tactic-transition evidence attributed to wrong strategy class; problem-id leakage through status-class collapse in blind-policy lane.
notes: FAILURE_CLASSES_CONSIDERED includes timeout, tactic_mismatch, premise_miss, import_build_missing, unsolved_goals, syntax_or_elaboration_error, oracle_gap_remaining. Taxonomy shared between curriculum and blind-policy runners — version drift would silently misroute learning rows.
one_line: Defines a typed status taxonomy (FORMAL_STATEMENT, GOAL_STATE, TACTIC_ACTION, TACTIC_TRANSITION, LEAN_ACCEPTED_PROOF, ORACLE_REPAIR, PROVIDER_HYPOTHESIS, FOUNDRY_POLICY_LEARNING, SEARCH_EXHAUSTED) that keeps one-shot tactics, proof-state transitions, provider hypotheses, and oracle comparators as distinct lanes even when they all produce Lean-accepted scripts.
title: Proof-state status taxonomy and search-lane separation
why_load_bearing: Conflating status lanes collapses the learning signal: a LEAN_ACCEPTED_PROOF via one-shot hammer has different policy value than one reached via TACTIC_TRANSITION search or ORACLE_REPAIR. Without typed lane separation, foundry learning rows mix signal types and premise-selection evidence cannot be attributed to the correct strategy lane.

candidate_fixture: Toy receipt with answer, ground_truth_proof, prompt, provider_output fields; spine builder copies lineage relationships but strips all FORBIDDEN_BODY_KEYS; CLAIM_BOUNDARY = 'private_lab_oracle_lineage_not_public_benchmark_result' attached; drift-check between spine and repair-lane FORBIDDEN_INLINE_KEYS sets fails closed.
failure_mode_prevented: Ground-truth proof bodies surfaced in public projection via spine; prompt payloads or model-facing content leaked through lineage; control-plane state undecidable without reading private receipts; FORBIDDEN_BODY_KEYS drift silently reopening leakage path.
notes: FORBIDDEN_BODY_KEYS = {answer, candidate_body, decision_point_plan, formal_answer, ground_truth_proof, ideal_body, lean_proof_body, model_facing_payload, prompt, prompt_body, prompt_messages, provider_output, repair_body, retrieval_body, solution, source_proof_body}. Same key set enforced in build_formal_math_proof_repair_lane.py as FORBIDDEN_INLINE_KEYS — drift between these two frozensets would silently open a leakage path.
one_line: Produces a generated Lab-to-Oracle lineage object over private formal-math receipts by enforcing a FORBIDDEN_BODY_KEYS set that prevents proof bodies, ground-truth answers, prompt payloads, and provider outputs from appearing in the spine, making control-plane truth derivable without leaking private benchmark content.
title: Formal-math proofline spine with body-safe lineage contract
why_load_bearing: The spine is the only artifact that makes current control-plane state derivable from a single public-safe JSON contract. Without the body-safe guard, any projection of the spine into a public surface leaks ground-truth proofs or model-facing payloads, violating the benchmark contamination boundary.

candidate_fixture: Toy repo with local ahead commit, blocked direct push gate, detached clean worktree prepare, guarded publish, and remote-ref check; validator rejects claiming pushed until target ref hash equals prepared commit.
failure_mode_prevented: Claiming clean-and-pushed from local status; force-pushing contaminated history; retrying ordinary push after the gate already selected detached clean publication.
notes: This sharpens the git-publication side of dirty_tree_rescue_ref rather than duplicating it: the organ is not rescue refs, it is the claim boundary between local commit state and remote ref state.
one_line: Separates local landing, push-gate admissibility, guarded push execution, and target remote-ref verification into distinct receipts so a clean local commit cannot masquerade as published state.
title: Publication/ref-boundary guarded push receipt
why_load_bearing: The repo is often dirty, ahead, and concurrently mutated. Without a ref-boundary organ, agents either refuse useful scoped commits or overclaim publication after local landing. The guarded lane lets selected clean commits cross the remote boundary only after audit, target-ref confirmation, and force-push refusal.

candidate_fixture: Toy public projection report green at commit A; generated gate/report files change, ledger files churn, and manifest/source files change in separate commits. The checker keeps generated-only and ignored ledger churn fresh, but turns manifest or included-source edits into not_green.
failure_mode_prevented: Using stale portability proof as current release evidence; discarding valid proof because only generated evidence or unrelated ledgers moved; hiding manifest drift under a still-green old report.
notes: The current readiness report exposes the full partition: allowed_generated_evidence_changed_paths, ignored_changed_paths_since_source_revision, portability_relevant_changed_paths_since_source_revision, manifest_hash_matches_current, and source_revision_matches_current_content.
one_line: Classifies a green portability receipt by recorded source_revision, manifest hash, allowed generated-evidence churn, ignored ledger churn, and portability-relevant source changes before it can count as current public-toggle evidence.
title: Portability revision-freshness scope gate
why_load_bearing: A proof can be both real and stale. This organ prevents the system from throwing away dated green evidence due to harmless generated artifacts while also preventing source or manifest drift from being laundered into current-head readiness.

candidate_fixture: Toy release packet with portability green but dependency/security/media blockers present. Readiness builder emits status no_go, public_toggle red, release_action none, forbidden wording list, and blocker ids; clearing all blockers changes release_action to eligible_for_operator_release_decision but still requires explicit operator yes.
failure_mode_prevented: Open-source, source-available, installable, hosted-public, ready-to-publish, public-video, or send-ready wording emitted while the release gate is red or stale.
notes: This supersedes the weak intuition inside go_no_go_publication_readiness that a packet can answer publishability. The sharper organ is the upstream release-action membrane that go/no-go packets consume but cannot override.
one_line: A fail-closed readiness membrane that reduces many green, amber, stale, missing, or blocked inputs into one public_toggle state and release_action enum, with a claim_guard that controls public/source/hosted/send wording while red.
title: Public-toggle release-action membrane
why_load_bearing: Dissemination has many impressive proof and packet artifacts. Without one release-action membrane, portability green, claim-language clear-boundary-only, proof-bundle polish, operator packet readiness, or recipient packet quality can be misread as authorization to publish, open source, host, send, or claim source availability.

candidate_fixture: Toy handle table with R_A5_SNAPSHOT green, R_CLAIM_LANGUAGE clear, and R_READINESS_GATE red. A claim compiler may write review evidence language but must downgrade any public/source/hosted/send verb until the weakest gate turns green.
failure_mode_prevented: A dated green proof, private-stage handle, clear copy scan, or polished packet being upgraded into public release, source availability, hosted availability, send permission, or external validation.
notes: This is the general organ under several dissemination documents: proof handles stay valuable as evidence, but action verbs route to the weakest owner surface. It should not be reduced to release packaging.
one_line: Interprets every proof handle by first citing the strongest evidence at its disclosure class, then inheriting the weakest live action gate before any public, source, hosted, ready, send, or publish verb is allowed.
title: Proof-handle weakest-gate resolver
why_load_bearing: The substrate has many valid proof handles with different scopes: private-stage green, dated A5, claim-language clear, recipient compiler, operator decisions, proof-tail tooling, formal-math and market cells. The resolver is the metabolism that keeps real evidence useful without letting the strongest handle dominate the weakest authority boundary.

candidate_fixture: Toy recipient has one representative-work note and proof grammar. Compiler emits a packet with weak understanding mode, whitelist/blacklist, no-send blockers, one bounded ask, and learning receipt. A simulated reply becomes response-intake evidence, not authority, and cannot approve send or root access.
failure_mode_prevented: Polished packet treated as send-ready; recipient reply treated as validation or release authority; private repo links/root access offered in first contact; prestigious recipients selected before proof fit.
notes: The organ includes the closed-loop handoff fields: packet class, route, inspectable scope, withheld boundary, confidence-changing proof, response class, privacy/access implication, and forbidden inference preservation.
one_line: Compiles a recipient packet by narrowing one inherited system identity through source-backed recipient fit, representative work, proof grammar, understanding mode, allowed/forbidden artifacts, no-send blockers, and expected learning receipt, then routes replies through receiver synthesis and response-intake before any mutation.
title: Recipient-packet closed-loop proof-branch compiler
why_load_bearing: This prevents outreach from becoming mail merge or authority laundering. A packet can be tailored and useful for internal review while still being no-send, no-public, no-root-access, and non-authoritative until gates and explicit decisions clear.

candidate_fixture: Toy release candidate escrow records source_commit, artifact_git_head, and archive_sha256. Closure map marks candidate verified_current, blocks approval for latest HEAD, emits dry_run_only operation, and refuses external mutation until exact tuple approval exists.
failure_mode_prevented: Operator approval for latest HEAD; releasing an archive different from the validated artifact; public switch mutation during review; candidate identity drift hidden behind a green-ish readiness packet.
notes: This is a stronger sibling of public_toggle_release_action_membrane: the toggle says whether a release action is allowed; the escrow switch says exactly which artifact identity an approval can apply to.
one_line: Binds release approval to an exact candidate identity tuple (source commit, artifact git head, archive sha256, validation receipt) and emits a dry-run switch plan with mutated_external_state=false until that exact identity is approved.
title: Exact-candidate identity escrow and dry-run switch plan
why_load_bearing: Publication approval is dangerous when it floats over latest HEAD. This organ makes the release object immutable enough for review: the operator cannot accidentally approve an ambient moving branch, a regenerated archive, or a different proof repo state by saying yes to a vague launch request.

candidate_fixture: Toy 5-problem set with one solution per problem; baseline run records selected_action_kind_counts per problem; ablation renames all ids and reruns; gate asserts equality; one synthetic id-leaking policy fails the gate.
failure_mode_prevented: Silent id-conditioned policy memorization being promoted as goal-conditioned reasoning; benchmark inflation from lookup-by-id behavior.
notes: Renames all problem_ids to ablated_NNN_misleading. Regenerates root-goal action candidates from the same GoalState object. Asserts original_success_count == ablation_success_count AND selected_action_kind_counts match. ablation_passed boolean gates summary validity.
one_line: Proves a proof-search policy is conditioned on goal state rather than memorized problem identity by renaming all problem_ids to ablated tokens, regenerating root-goal action candidates from the same GoalState object, and asserting accepted-action counts match the original run.
title: Blind-policy problem-ID ablation gate
why_load_bearing: Without this gate the policy could silently memorize training problem identifiers and look up solutions instead of reasoning from state. The ablation is the only internal mechanism that distinguishes a goal-conditioned policy from a lookup table — a hidden id-conditioned policy would pass Lean checks but fail the ablation, exposing inflated benchmark numbers.

candidate_fixture: Toy two-problem solved set; evolver synthesizes variants in 10 structural families; full Lean replay on ablated variants; required atomic_success_count match; one variant that names policy-fooling identifiers is caught by the gate.
failure_mode_prevented: Policy credit for solutions that depend on recognizable identifiers rather than proof-state structure; inflation of progress metrics by non-generalizing solutions.
notes: Ten structural family tags (implication_chains, nested_conjunction, disjunction_cases, etc.) cycle over variants. Order-shuffled, all identifiers renamed again on ablation. Foundry learning rows written only from ablation-confirmed accepted set.
one_line: Generates adversarial pressure by systematically renaming theorem names, binder variables, hypothesis names, and custom definitions in solved training rows (10 structural families), then forcing the policy through Lean-checked full replay of all accepted solutions and requiring atomic_success_count to match the original.
title: Adversarial identifier-rename problem evolver
why_load_bearing: Creates structurally distinct but mathematically equivalent problems to stress-test policy generalization. Without it the system only tests the original id-space. The full ablation replay gate certifies that renamed problems produce identical accepted-solution counts — failure invalidates any policy-update credit from the run, and foundry learning rows are written from the ablation-confirmed accepted set only.

candidate_fixture: Toy 3-problem set with ForwardProblem/OracleSidecar split; firewall walks all forward attrs and harness packets checking zero presence of FORBIDDEN_FORWARD_FIELDS; one synthetic typo that leaks the oracle body fails the gate; validate() treats this as a hard gate.
failure_mode_prevented: Oracle proof body appearing in forward search context producing benchmark inflation; truth-side leakage surviving Lean acceptance because the model echoed the oracle solution.
notes: FORBIDDEN_FORWARD_FIELDS = (candidate_body, ideal_body, repair_body, oracle_needed_premise_ids, source proof body, base_problem_index). _firewall_report walks all ForwardProblem attrs and all derived harness packets. Summary boolean forward_problem_has_oracle_fields must be False for a valid run.
one_line: Enforces the oracle/forward boundary at the type level by splitting solved problems into a ForwardProblem dataclass (no ideal_body, no repair_body) and an OracleSidecar dataclass (withheld), then emitting a per-problem firewall report verifying no FORBIDDEN_FORWARD_FIELDS appear in any harness packet.
title: Oracle sidecar forward firewall — typed split of ForwardProblem and OracleSidecar
why_load_bearing: A single Python attribute mistake could cause the oracle body to silently appear in the forward search context. The typed split plus the automated firewall report provide the only programmatic defense against this silent failure mode. Without it, oracle proof body material could be echoed by a contaminated provider and survive Lean acceptance as if it were independent evidence.

candidate_fixture: Toy run with 20 transitions across 4 (feature-set, action-kind) buckets; posterior_score = (accepted+1)/(attempts+2); table sorted descending; skill_policy_update_candidates credits each accepted action_kind with goal_feature_policy_prior.
failure_mode_prevented: Policy that never improves from Lean feedback; tactic selection remaining static after many runs produce clear evidence about which actions work on which goal shapes.
notes: Buckets are (comma-sorted active-feature-names, action_kind). For each bucket: attempts, accepted, failures_by_class, avg_duration_ms, posterior_score. Output schema blind_action_value_table_v0. Bridge from one run to durable policy improvement.
one_line: After each blind-search run, computes a Laplace-smoothed posterior success rate for every (goal-feature-set, action-kind) bucket from the transition manifest, producing a durable action-value table the policy can use to rank future action selection.
title: Goal-feature × action-kind posterior table
why_load_bearing: This is the primary feedback signal connecting Lean outcomes back to tactic priority. Without it, the policy is permanently static; with it, each run deposits evidence about which action kinds succeed or fail on which goal-feature combinations.

candidate_fixture: Toy proof with simp+rw cycle; without hash pruning, search exhausts timeout; with hash, cycle detected after 3 visits; duplicate row emitted per pruned candidate; open_subgoal_count discriminates same-target-different-case states (NOT duplicate).
failure_mode_prevented: Infinite loops on rewrite/simp cycles; exhausting search budget without finding shallow proofs; wasted Lean invocations on structurally identical states.
notes: Normalization strips auto-names (h✝, w✝) with regex; collapses whitespace. Context shape sorted over all hypothesis types (not names) for ordering invariance. Two deque-tracked sets: seen_hashes (committed), enqueued_hashes (queued but not visited).
one_line: Assigns each proof state a SHA256 hash of (normalized target, sorted context-type shapes, open-subgoal count), tracking seen and enqueued hashes separately to prune duplicate states and prevent infinite loops in multi-goal AND/OR search.
title: Canonical goal-state hash for AND/OR search loop pruning
why_load_bearing: Without loop pruning, structural induction with repeated simp/rw attempts generates cyclic states. The canonical hash is the only mechanism preventing the BFS from exhausting the timeout on cycle-inducing tactic chains rather than finding proofs.

candidate_fixture: Toy provider receipt with one clean response and one containing 'oracle_needed_premise_ids' marker; clean one passes Lean compile check; marker-bearing one routed to SOLUTION_LEAKAGE (non-retryable) before Lean check; context_pack.forbidden_material additions merged at runtime.
failure_mode_prevented: Provider model echoing oracle proof body material being promoted to benchmark evidence; inflated solve rates from oracle-contaminated provider calls.
notes: TRUTH_SIDE_FORBIDDEN_MARKERS = (truth_side_proof_bodies, oracle_only_repair_bodies, withheld_until_oracle, oracle_needed_premise_ids, ideal_body, repair_body, retrieval_body). Scan is over flattened string values, lowercased. Leakage check runs BEFORE Lean compile check; SOLUTION_LEAKAGE is non-retryable.
one_line: Before promoting any provider-proposed proof body to forward evidence, scans the full text of the provider response for forbidden marker strings (TRUTH_SIDE_FORBIDDEN_MARKERS) that signal echoed oracle or benchmark truth material, quarantining the receipt on any hit.
title: Provider receipt truth-side leakage detector
why_load_bearing: A provider that has seen oracle proof material and echoes it cannot produce independent evidence. This detector is the only automated gate preventing truth-side contamination from silently inflating benchmark numbers through the provider lane.

candidate_fixture: Toy theorem set: one with visible recursive head (induction proposed), one with hidden recursive head (induction skipped), one without recursive structure (induction never proposed). Counts in induction_scheduler_report: induction_action_attempt_count, forward_success_without_induction_ids, oracle_only_remaining_ids.
failure_mode_prevented: Beam exhaustion from spurious induction on problems without recursive structure; false negatives where induction is skipped because the recursive head is buried.
notes: Definition heads extracted from theorem signature via regex on def declarations. A head is visible if regex finds the symbol in target_text (join of state.target and state.goal_excerpt). Three conditions must all be true.
one_line: Structural induction is proposed for a binder only when (a) the proof is at depth zero, (b) the goal has an equality target, AND (c) a custom definition from the theorem signature appears visibly at the head position of the current goal state — preventing spurious induction on problems where recursive structure is not visible.
title: Induction scheduler with visible-definitional-head gate
why_load_bearing: Unconstrained induction proposals on every Nat or List binder would flood the search beam with expensive dead-end branches. The visible-head gate is the primary signal that induction will likely terminate; without it, action generation degrades to random structural guessing.

candidate_fixture: Toy CP1 with 2 cross-lane bifurcations; bifurcation_resolutions array contains both entries with selected_side, rationale, opposite_side_invalidation; missing resolution fails CP1 validation.
failure_mode_prevented: Silent contradiction absorption: model picks a thesis that implicitly resolves a bifurcation but records no decision trace, so Oracle cannot later determine whether a miss was caused by bad evidence reading or bad contradiction resolution.
notes: The bifurcation_resolutions contract is structurally the most important constraint in CP1: the model cannot produce a valid CP1 without explicitly taking a side on every cross-lane conflict. Instruction in lab_decide.json: address all bifurcations, cite both sides, state what would make the other side correct.
one_line: Lab Decide must explicitly resolve every cross-lane contradiction surfaced by cross_corr_v1 (named selected_side, rationale, opposite_side_invalidation) before the CP1 epicentre thesis can be locked — making the thesis falsifiable at the input-contradiction layer rather than only at the output-direction layer.
title: CP1 bifurcation reconciliation spine
why_load_bearing: Without explicit bifurcation_resolutions, a contradiction between (e.g.) Stockgrid flow and News narrative would silently collapse into whichever evidence the model happened to weight last. The structured array forces named side-selection plus opposite_side_invalidation, making the thesis falsifiable at the input-contradiction layer. This is the mechanism that prevents the thesis from being a winner-of-whatever tournament.

candidate_fixture: Two CP1 runs: one FLOW_LED where Stockgrid Tier 1 prevails without override; one NARRATIVE_LED with explicit defense of the News override; pre_pricing three-tests (Polymarket >75%, >1-sigma price action, flow alignment) executed at the News/Poly boundary.
failure_mode_prevented: Narrative bias in thesis construction: model anchors on News catalysts (most salient, most recent) rather than slower institutional flow signals with greater predictive value.
notes: Codified as [EPISTEMIC HIERARCHY v3.0]. Produces machine-checkable dominant_evidence_track field. Pre_pricing three-tests are the concrete operational form at the News/Poly boundary.
one_line: A fixed four-tier resolution order (Stockgrid/Calc > ETF/Macro > Poly/Stock > News) governs how Lab Decide ranks conflicting cross-lane signals when building the CP1 thesis; any override of Tier 1 by Tier 4 must be explicitly defended in the dominant_evidence_track field.
title: Epistemic hierarchy — 4-tier lane resolution order
why_load_bearing: Without a fixed hierarchy, a model producing a NARRATIVE_LED thesis driven by News could not be distinguished from one that correctly identified a case where News had genuinely new forward-looking information. The hierarchy makes this judgment auditable: any override of Tier 1 by Tier 4 must be defended, and Oracle can later check whether the override was justified.

candidate_fixture: cross_corr_v2 valid_prediction_targets as intersection of target_swarm and solo_target tickers; CP2 with predictions_t target_ids all in that set; one synthetic UNGROUNDED_TARGET attempt rejected by validate_cp2.
failure_mode_prevented: Unbounded target space: model predicts low-liquidity names where grading is noisy, or targets with no equity-lane grounding where direction calls cannot be evaluated by the deterministic diff layer.
notes: Equity-lane confirmation is the key novelty: thesis-level plausibility cannot substitute for structural market evidence. UNGROUNDED_TARGET error code in schema_cp2.json is the machine enforcement.
one_line: cross_corr_v2 produces an authoritative valid_prediction_targets list; lab_director may only predict tickers in this list, and a ticker requires equity-lane confirmation (STOCK, ETF, or STOCKGRID) before entry.
title: CP2 universe constraint — valid_prediction_targets gate
why_load_bearing: Without a universe constraint, lab_director could predict any ticker it finds plausible, producing non-comparable grading results and enabling cherry-picking of highly asymmetric targets with no cross-lane support. The gate enforces multi-lane structural backing AND at least one equity-sensitive supporting lane. Tickers supported only by MACRO, NEWS, POLY, or CALC are explicitly excluded.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic 3-target run with one direction_hit=true, one direction_hit=false, one DEGRADED feed_health (ungraded); realized_target_table per row with snapshot_price/predicted_direction/realized_price/directional_correct/pred_error_pct; feed_health block status per target.
