failure_mode_prevented: Conflation of feed unavailability with prediction failure; Oracle interpretive stages introducing narrative contamination into what should be purely arithmetic grading.
notes: Private state risk high because realized_target_table contains specific ticker prices and prediction miss amounts. Organ (schema + diff mechanism) is not private. Public fixtures must use synthetic or obfuscated prices.
one_line: oracle_truth_diff_equity computes a deterministic row-level grading table comparing Lab CP2 prediction targets against realized prices at T, emitting direction_hit, pred_error_pct, and a feed_health block (READY/DEGRADED/BLOCKED per target) before any interpretive Oracle stage runs.
title: Oracle deterministic equity-diff grading surface
why_load_bearing: This is the ground-truth scoring organ: the only machine-checkable verdict on whether a prediction was directionally correct, and it separates that verdict from any narrative explanation. The feed_health block ensures Oracle interpretive stages do not blame prediction failure for missing price feeds.

candidate_fixture: Toy hindsight brief with one SURPRISE driver, one ALREADY_PRICED driver, one PARTIAL_REPRICING; mutual-exclusion check enforced; prediction_target_outcomes covering all targets from paired prediction_reconciliation.
failure_mode_prevented: Misdiagnosed calibration failure: a run with 0% direction accuracy might look like systematic bias but could be entirely attributable to ALREADY_PRICED events where thesis was correct but market had front-run.
notes: Pre_pricing_assessment field in CP1 is the forward-time claim this artifact validates in hindsight. Pair (forward assessment, backward classification) forms the full calibration loop for the Polymarket/pre-pricing subsystem.
one_line: oracle_truth_map emits a realized_hindsight_brief classifying each major realized driver as SURPRISE / ALREADY_PRICED / PARTIAL_REPRICING (mutually exclusive), providing interpretive context for the deterministic diff before critique or ideal_cp2 reconstruction begins.
title: Oracle hindsight brief — realized driver SURPRISE/ALREADY_PRICED/PARTIAL_REPRICING classification
why_load_bearing: Prediction miss analysis without this classification conflates two distinct failure modes: (A) the model correctly identified the force but the force was already priced, so no directional move occurred; (B) the model identified the wrong force entirely. The SURPRISE/ALREADY_PRICED/PARTIAL_REPRICING classification makes this distinction auditable. The pre_pricing_assessment field in CP1 is the forward-time analog; this is the backward-time validation.

candidate_fixture: Toy ideal_cp2 with evidence_dictionary referencing T-n ledger IDs from paired Lab run; one synthetic post-T id rejected by validate_golden_ids; oracle_meta with truth_influence_refs and explicit_exclusions for what shaped reconstruction but was excluded from evidence.
failure_mode_prevented: Hindsight contamination: Oracle produces an ideal_cp2 that looks well-grounded but whose evidence_dictionary silently contains truth-side signals unavailable at T-n; Evolve learns from contaminated ideal examples and overfits to post-hoc information.
notes: Schema explicitly states 'Oracle does not get a second CP2 schema. It reuses the same CP2-core.' grounding_law array has five normative rules including 'Golden-ID enforcement still applies'. explicit_exclusions field in oracle_meta is the audit surface — each exclusion says what was seen, why it was relevant, and why it was not admissible.
one_line: Oracle's ideal_cp2 must preserve the CP2-core grounding law (only subject-side T-n ledger IDs in evidence_dictionary) while truth-side influence is confined to oracle_meta; the golden_id guard enforces that every evidence ledger_id existed in the original Lab run's ledger set.
title: Oracle CP2 grounding firewall — subject-side ledger ID enforcement
why_load_bearing: Without this firewall, an Oracle that has seen realized truth prices could trivially construct an ideal_cp2 that cherry-picks hindsight-compatible evidence, making the ideal_cp2 useless as a training signal because it would reflect what the truth was rather than what the best subject-side reasoning should have been. The golden_id guard adds the machine-checkable enforcement layer.

candidate_fixture: Toy evolve_delta_report with one UPDATE dossier_delta (rationale citing oracle artifact + field) and one HIGH-severity doctrine_flag requiring 2+ evidence_runs; overnight plan shows budget-aware run selection; codex_review_after_runs:3 escalation policy triggers Type A gate before next wave.
failure_mode_prevented: Single-run overfitting (Evolve updates dossiers after one anomalous run, encoding anomaly as learned pattern); mutation without provenance; writes outside the dossier_target_allowlist.
notes: Overnight planner adds budget-aware scheduling: pairs scored by readiness (READY/DEGRADED/BLOCKED), cost-estimated by target count, selected within abstract bridge budget. codex_review_after_runs:3 means every 3 Evolve outputs triggers a Type A review gate.
one_line: The Evolve delta_analyzer converts Oracle critique artifacts (cp2_critique + prediction_reconciliation + realized_hindsight_brief) into structured dossier CRUD operations and doctrine flags, with evidence gates requiring multiple run-pairs before high-severity mutations are committed.
title: Evolve — Oracle-to-dossier mutation loop with multi-run evidence gates
why_load_bearing: Without this loop, Oracle grading is a dead-end observability exercise — predictions are scored but nothing learns. The evolve_delta_report is the mechanism by which Oracle findings feed back into substrate dossiers that lab_director reads as prior knowledge. Multi-run evidence gates prevent overfitting to single-run noise. The lab_oracle_evolve_overnight planner operationalizes this with budget-aware run pair selection.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic 12-shard pool with 4 sharing intent_tag overlap [trajectory, proposal, threshold]; CLUSTER_TOKEN_THRESHOLD=3, CLUSTER_MIN_SIZE=3 surface them as a cluster with status needs_codex; controller-gated decision: propose_new_concept (confidence 0.78).
failure_mode_prevented: Ideas voiced in N paragraphs sharing a common theme but matching no existing doctrine node silently drift into routing_state=pending forever, with no signal that a new doctrine node is warranted.
notes: Threshold constants (CLUSTER_TOKEN_THRESHOLD=3, CLUSTER_MIN_SIZE=3) are heuristic and operator-interpretable. Boundary between machine-detectable signal and controller-gated action. The triad symmetry score in compute_alchemy_status is a downstream consumer.
one_line: When enough unrouted shards share token overlap above threshold, they surface as an orphan cluster and become a new-doctrine candidate demanding a concept or mechanism node — the machine-checkable signal that a new dimension of the system has appeared in operator voice with no doctrine home yet.
title: Orphan-cluster doctrine emergence organ
why_load_bearing: Without this organ, ideas voiced repeatedly but not matching existing doctrine silently accumulate in pending routing state. The cluster-detection step is the only machine-checkable signal that a genuinely new dimension has appeared and has no doctrine home. The thresholds are operator-interpretable — code surfaces clusters, does not auto-mint doctrine.

candidate_fixture: Toy bundle with signature_tokens [threshold, gate, bounded, discipline, enforce]; principle_slot decision refine_existing(pri_NNN); concept_slot refine_existing(con_NNN); mechanism_slot propose_new(slug threshold-enforcement-gate); triad_symmetry_score computed.
failure_mode_prevented: Shards accumulate in routing-pending state with high candidate-target counts but no governance path to create or refine a doctrine node; operator forced to hand-author without structural-form discipline.
notes: Decision space exactly 5 values: refine_existing, propose_new, merge_existing, split, reject. Triad symmetry score (|principles - 2*concepts| + |principles - 2*mechanisms|) is the quantitative health monitor for doctrine balance. Dense-group recipes are hardcoded structural forms (7 named); orphan clusters go through the generic bundle path. Type A conductor lane.
one_line: The alchemy lane lifts a bounded shard cluster into one controller-gated doctrine triple (principle + concept + mechanism slots) via structural-form naming, signature-token fingerprinting, and one of five decisions (refine_existing / propose_new / merge_existing / split / reject) — the move that turns pattern evidence into doctrine.
title: Raw-seed alchemy doctrine-triple synthesis
why_load_bearing: Distillation produces faithful shards; routing scores shards against existing nodes; but neither step creates new doctrine nodes. Alchemy is the only move that can propose or refine a principle/concept/mechanism triple from shard evidence, using dense-group recipes or generic orphan-cluster bundles. Without it, no new doctrine emerges from operator voice.

candidate_fixture: Toy catalog with 3 named pattern classes (paragraph_opener, mid_sentence_reversal, hedge_marker) each with markers, signal strength, distillation rule; hedge target 1-2 per shard (zero flattens, 5+ fails density reduction).
failure_mode_prevented: Distillers treat hedge-stripping as cleanup and reversal-flattening as normalization, producing shards that sound confident and clean but have removed the epistemic posture from the substrate permanently.
notes: 13 named pattern classes: 12 paragraph openers, 8 mid-sentence reversal markers with signal strength, 11 hedge markers with density, emphatic repetition, ACK-demand, fatigue closes, all-caps register. Hedge target '1-2 per shard; zero is wrong (flattens), 5+ is wrong (failed reduction)'. Empirically derived, not axiom-derived.
one_line: A temporally-sampled, named-recognizer catalog of operator-voice patterns (spiral, reversal, hedge, emphatic-repetition, ACK-demand, fatigue-close, all-caps-rant) compiled from 8 sample points across the raw seed — the empirical ground from which distillation rules derive their specificity.
title: Empirical voice pattern catalog — the Distiller's ground truth
why_load_bearing: Distillation rules are normative; the voice pattern catalog is the observational evidence that makes those rules falsifiable and improvable. Without the empirical catalog, rules are aspirational heuristics; with it, the Distiller can match a specific recognizer to a specific source shape, calibrate confidence, and know which hedge-density range to preserve.

candidate_fixture: Toy 6 shards; signal-token weights route each to the correct plane (paper_module/principle/skill/standard/annex_pattern/raw_evidence); ambiguous shard defaults to raw_evidence safe-harbor; per-plane output contract enforced downstream.
failure_mode_prevented: System-description shards (present-tense facts about what files exist) mistakenly routed as general independent principles, corrupting the principle graph with implementation-description content.
notes: Plane classification is signal-based (token presence and weights) not LLM-based; fast, deterministic, auditable. Output contract per plane is normative. raw_evidence plane is the safe-harbor default when signal is ambiguous.
one_line: Before any shard touches a doctrine node, a six-plane review gate classifies it toward paper_module / principle / skill / standard / annex_pattern / raw_evidence — ensuring the right downstream organ receives it and preventing routing category errors that poison the doctrine graph.
title: Shard review-plane routing gate — six-plane pre-route classification
why_load_bearing: The routing-review step scores shards against existing doctrine nodes but does not classify what kind of artifact the shard wants to become. Without the plane gate, a shard that should become a paper-module fact gets routed as a principle candidate, corrupting the principle graph with system-description content. Six planes encode orthogonal artifact classes, each with a distinct output contract.

candidate_fixture: Toy 5-paragraph corpus; scorer outputs keyword_hints (≤8) per paragraph; salvage_path tokens weighted 4.85; conversational stopwords filtered separately from SMART stopwords; ultra_generic blocklist catches high-DF low-information glue.
failure_mode_prevented: Shard navigation requires full-text scanning of hundreds of paragraph texts; conversational filler tokens dominate keyword extraction making preludes meaningless.
notes: Compound scorer: salvage tokens (path/ID) highest weight, then local-presence-weighted unigrams/bigrams/RAKE phrases scored against corpus-level DF/CF. Conversational stopwords filtered separately (ok, yeah, basically, literally, actually, etc.). RAKE port cites upstream annex with provenance.
one_line: For every raw-seed paragraph, a composite RAKE + DF/CF scoring pass builds bounded keyword_hints (≤8 items) that are topic-weighted navigation preludes — not authoritative semantic truth, but the searchable surface that lets agents find shards without full-text scan.
title: Corpus-keyphrase navigation prelude per shard
why_load_bearing: The shard registry has hundreds of extracted shards; without paragraph-level keyphrase indexing, every navigation move requires reading full paragraph text. The keyphrase organ builds the lightweight lookup surface that makes the registry browsable. The design explicitly inverts the traditional TF-IDF high-DF penalty: topics that recur across paragraphs are corpus signal, not noise.

candidate_fixture: Toy reversal link with drift_status=unresolved; check_command 'grep -rln ... | wc -l' returns 7; reaudit after substrate edit returns 0; drift_status flipped to resolved with evidence; tolerated_legacy variant preserves old instances as read-only.
failure_mode_prevented: A reversal recorded as 'resolved' in the JSONL based on the voiced position, but the substrate still carries the old position in 14 files; every subsequent agent reads both as current.
notes: Sweep module is read-only. Key invariant: declaring drift_status:resolved without a sweep is care-failure. tolerated_legacy vs resolved is operationally critical. Reversal link schema has bitemporal fields (valid_from, valid_until, observed_at, superseded_at) from graphiti EntityEdge model.
one_line: Every reversal link carries a drift_status (unresolved/tolerated_legacy/partial/resolved/n_a) and drift_evidence array with a check_command returning an integer count — so the claim 'the substrate no longer carries the superseded position' is machine-checkable, not cultural, and can be re-audited on any future check.
title: Reversal drift detection and audit sweep
why_load_bearing: Reversal tracking records X-and-not-X pairs, but without drift detection the reversal record is only half the story. The substrate may still carry the old position in many files after the voiced reversal. The drift sweep turns 'both preserved' from an append-only log entry into an auditable substrate state with five drift_status values discriminating between live debt, managed legacy, and fully propagated reversals.

candidate_fixture: Toy archaeological shard with coverage_check.decision=drop_overlaps, overlap_found=[pri_007, con_030]; emit variant with new_dimension named; AI-output file rejected regardless of coverage; archaeological_depth dial modulates gestures_towards density (1-2/2-4/3-6/3-6).
failure_mode_prevented: Mining 571 archaeologically-distant files produces a flood of shards duplicating existing doctrine plus provenance-mixed shards from AI-output files, contaminating extracted_shards backlog with retread + voice-theft.
notes: Three decisions: emit (new dimension), drop_overlaps (partial overlap, still emit but record home), drop_full_coverage (fully covered). Third substrate distinction enforced by gate — ai_output provenance never mined regardless of coverage. archaeological_depth dial (shallow/medium/deep/foundational).
one_line: Before any shard mined from archaeologically-distant voice (obsidian/idea/ corpus) is imported, it must carry a populated coverage_check object (emit / drop_overlaps / drop_full_coverage decision) proving the agent consulted current doctrine — blocking the retread-at-scale failure mode endemic to mining older material.
title: Archaeological voice coverage gate — pre-import doctrine overlap check
why_load_bearing: Archaeological voice overlaps current substrate by construction. Without a hard coverage gate, mining 571 files produces hundreds of shards that duplicate existing principles, plus provenance-mixed AI-output shards mistaken for Will-voice. The gate makes 'dropped shards' a first-class artifact alongside 'emitted shards'.

candidate_fixture: Toy dispatch with mode=paused (zero work); switch to trickle (1 chatgpt allowed, claude/codex blocked); switch to sprint (3 chatgpt); local gate triggers on load_per_cpu_1m above mode threshold; 15-minute red cooldown after saturation event.
failure_mode_prevented: Remote provider stampede and local machine thrashing during interactive operator sessions; accidental paid API spend.
notes: Five modes: paused (no dispatch), trickle (remote_light_local_light only), active, overnight (afk-wider), sprint. Local gates: load_per_cpu_1m vs mode threshold (0.70/0.85/1.25/1.50), swap used >1GB, compressed memory >4GB, WindowServer CPU >35%, 15-minute red cooldown. trickle silently drops heavy local work without error.
one_line: Five named operating modes (paused/trickle/active/overnight/sprint) compose a single runtime gas pedal: every dispatch decision threads governor mode → CPU load gate → memory pressure snapshot → provider spacing history → concurrency cap before any work reaches a queue.
title: Governor mode dispatch state machine — the runtime gas pedal
why_load_bearing: Without the governor, dispatch is uncontrolled: remote providers get saturated, heavy local operations run during interactive sessions, and paid API spend triggers. Mode profiles are the single source of truth for all provider max_concurrent, min_seconds_between_dispatch, and local load thresholds.

candidate_fixture: Toy reactions.yaml with two reactions; signal arrives, six-gate pipeline runs; signal_digest stable → suppressed by recent fire; ledger_fingerprint changes → re-fire allowed; subprocess spawn + barrier install; completed_digests rolling window cap 500.
failure_mode_prevented: Reaction repeat-fire loops on failed but unchanged source material; digest-collision false suppression; concurrent mutation from simultaneous reaction fires.
notes: Signal digest covers materialized signal (ignores volatile timestamps). Ledger fingerprint covers signal minus volatile keys — detects content changes under stable digest. Schema v2 migration added fingerprint path. Source kinds: raw_seed_coverage, operation_event, hologram_build_status, work_ledger_status, orchestration_event, lifecycle_boundary, provider_model_catalog_signal, standard_skill_gap_signal, compliance_coverage_signal.
one_line: The always-on reactions engine evaluates each reaction via a six-gate pipeline (predicate match → armed → cooldown → signal_digest dedupe → terminal-outcome-plus-ledger-fingerprint dedupe → winner selection), then fires the winning reaction's operation as a detached subprocess while installing a wake barrier.
title: Reaction evaluation with dual-fingerprint dedupe pipeline
why_load_bearing: This is the core autonomy execution organ: the only mechanism that converts substrate-observed signals into operations without operator intervention. Dual-fingerprint dedupe prevents both re-fire loops on unchanged signals AND spurious suppression on digest collisions. Barrier prevents concurrent reaction fires from producing conflicting mutations.

candidate_fixture: Toy reaction fires; barrier kind=operation_completion installed with runner PID; new tick blocked by _barrier_is_active; PID exits, barrier cleared, next tick proceeds; separate test: barrier age >2700s triggers SIGKILL + force-clear.
failure_mode_prevented: Concurrent reaction fires producing conflicting substrate mutations; indefinitely stalled runtime from hung bridge subprocess.
notes: Barrier kinds: operation_completion (blocks until PID exits), cooldown (blocks until wake_at). Three failure modes handled: runner PID not found, barrier wake_at expired, barrier age >2700s (45min) → SIGKILL. Barrier state written to reactions_state.json before subprocess spawn.
one_line: After a reaction fires its operation as a detached subprocess, the engine installs a barrier (kind: operation_completion or cooldown) into persisted state that blocks all future reaction evaluations until the subprocess PID exits — with a 45-minute hard-kill guard that SIGKILLs a stuck runner.
title: Reaction wake barrier — detached subprocess suspend-and-resume
why_load_bearing: Session-continuity organ at the reaction level: engine is stateless between ticks but the barrier persists across process restarts. Without it, a new tick could fire a second reaction before the first writes its results, producing concurrent mutations. Hard-kill guard prevents a hung bridge tab from stalling the entire reactions runtime.

candidate_fixture: Toy session ends; hook emits session_lifecycle_boundary with stable_digest; reactions engine loader reads latest matching row from last 200 events; predicate matches agent_surface=claude_code AND boundary=session_stop; operation fires within ~15s.
failure_mode_prevented: Substrate work accumulating across sessions until operator manually re-enters; lost substrate signals between sessions.
notes: Hook emits rows into orchestration_events.jsonl with stable_digest. Reactions loader reads latest matching row from last 200 events, promotes stable_digest as stable_signal_digest, flat-promotes payload.agent_surface + payload.boundary + payload.session_id to top-level signal for predicate matching.
one_line: Agent session stop events emitted by the Claude Code runtime hook are ingested into the orchestration event bus as session_lifecycle_boundary rows; the reactions engine watches these as a named signal source kind (lifecycle_boundary), evaluates predicates against agent_surface and boundary fields, and fires operations immediately on session end — without polling, without operator action.
title: Session lifecycle boundary as typed reaction signal
why_load_bearing: This is the autonomous cross-session continuity organ at the reaction level: closes the gap between a Claude/Codex session ending and the next operation starting. Without it, substrate work triggered by session events requires operator re-entry. The stable_digest from the hook row is promoted to stable_signal_digest, letting dedupe block re-fires of the same boundary.

candidate_fixture: Toy 3-step plan; bridge_snapshot contract requires decision/runtime_items/next_step_updates/provider_limits_observed/baton (exactly 5 sentences)/confidence ≥0.5; step kinds bridge_snapshot/codex_task/meta_mission/runtime_items/manual; sequential vs concurrent annotation respected.
failure_mode_prevented: Multi-step plans losing execution context across session boundaries; concurrent seat executions producing undefined step ordering.
notes: Bridge snapshot contract requires: decision, runtime_items, next_step_updates, provider_limits_observed, baton (exactly 5 sentences), confidence (≥0.5). Plan state persisted under state/autonomy_runtime/<queue_id>/runtime/plans/<plan_id>/.
one_line: Multi-step autonomy plans compile their steps into typed runtime items (type_a_seed/chain/bridge_campaign/operation/nvidia_batch) with per-step baton_pass (exactly 5 sentences), closeout_contract (done/needs_another_go), concurrency annotations, and seat bindings — allowing a plan to describe a multi-agent execution graph that survives across sessions and carries explicit handoff state.
title: Autonomy plan baton pass and closeout contract
why_load_bearing: Without this, multi-step agent work degrades to chat-memory ceremony. The baton pass is validated structurally (exactly 5 sentences, confidence 0-1) and the closeout contract determines whether a step can self-report needs_another_go to trigger re-entry. Bridge_snapshot lets a mid-plan agent read current limits, propose next_step_updates, and hand off to the next seat.

candidate_fixture: Toy 4 candidate row-jobs across 2 providers; score vector populated per row; openrouter paid-spend guard hard-vetoes one row; draft materialization eligibility requires target_row_id + provider in PROVIDER_RUNTIME_BY_ID + target_row_kind=provider_model_row + authority_ceiling in {candidate_triage_only, empty}.
failure_mode_prevented: Dispatching low-value or high-quota-cost work ahead of high-value free-provider work; triggering paid provider spend on low-adherence work.
notes: Existing provider_candidate_selector_snapshot focuses on feed shape; this is the score organ itself. provider_lane_bonus penalizes unmapped providers. source_freshness reward only if source_refs non-empty. staleness_pressure reward only if limit_claims non-empty.
one_line: A 14-dimension score vector (operator_value, source_freshness, workitem_relevance, reversibility, provider_fit, expected_cpu_cost, quota_cost, novelty, duplicate_risk, review_burden, failure_history, staleness_pressure, structured_output_adherence, provider_lane_bonus) determines which provider-model-catalog row jobs are nominated as claimable candidates.
title: Provider row-job score vector selector
why_load_bearing: The priority/heat/readiness organ for the metabolism dispatch path. Without it, the system has no principled basis for choosing which work to run next when multiple row jobs are available. The structured_output_adherence dimension reads the schema_score from the transform_job_seed input packet. Provider_lane_bonus penalizes rows routing to unmapped providers (0.35 vs 0.72).

candidate_fixture: Toy 3-situation graph with evidence/counterevidence edges and reason codes; validate_market_situation_graph enforces no validated_signal without external validation_refs; situation_type enum closed; horizon enum closed; confidence_axes summed.
failure_mode_prevented: Consumers treating feed presence as cross-asset claim authority; undisclosed validation debt; evidence with no counterevidence forcing overconfidence in downstream display.
notes: Situation types closed: flow_price_confirmation, flow_price_divergence, macro_regime_shift, event_market_salience, provider_or_data_drift, coverage_or_missingness_gap, cross_asset_theme. Horizons closed: intraday through structural. Validation states: requires_backtest, requires_event_study, overfit_risk. No validated_signal promotable without external validation_refs.
one_line: Compiles feed-mart observations into typed situation objects, each carrying evidence and counterevidence edges with reason codes, formal shapes, claim levels, validation states, and multi-axis confidence decomposition (data_quality + evidence_strength + counterevidence_penalty + traceability).
title: Market situation evidence graph — typed cross-asset situations with evidence/counterevidence
why_load_bearing: This is the primary reasoning artifact that converts raw feed presence into semantically typed market situations. Without it, consumers get only raw feed rows with no evidence structure, no claim-level gating, no counterevidence, and no validation debt tracking. Every downstream display, dissemination, or reasoning consumer depends on situation objects rather than bare mart rows.

candidate_fixture: Toy 7-lane registry each with one of 4 SafeUseLevel literals; three explicit CandidateSituationGate rows (macro_event_x_equity_response, etf_price_move_x_nav_basket_claim, polymarket_expectation_x_macro_equity_situation) all decision=refuse; preflight_candidate_situation fails closed on unknown claim.
failure_mode_prevented: lab/oracle consumers emitting cross-feed divergence cards or target-swarm claims without measurement conditioning; silent permissive defaults on unknown claim shapes.
notes: preflight_candidate_situation API fails closed on unknown claim shapes — no permissive default. Consumer hook state shows lab_cross_corr_v1 and v2 are listed as not directly consuming the gate yet.
one_line: Projects per-lane trust levels (feed_observation_only → lane_measurement_guarded → refuse_cross_feed_claim) and named candidate situation gates that fail closed on any unknown claim, preventing premature cross-lane interpretation.
title: Cross-feed refusal-first readiness gate
why_load_bearing: Without this gate, any consumer that sees two lanes with data rows is free to assert cross-feed interpretation. The gate encodes which specific cross-feed claims are currently refused and WHY (named gaps), making refusal legible and recoverable rather than silent or ad hoc.

candidate_fixture: Toy 10-market set across all states; classifier deterministic on priority cascade; zombie rail-pinned past-end market classified DE_FACTO_TERMINAL with confidence_score; CLOB enrichment skipped for non-eligible states; components retained (past_end, time_to_end_seconds, railness, entropy, abs_one_day_change).
failure_mode_prevented: CLOB enrichment attempting to fetch order books for resolved markets and treating empty as data failure; calibration inheriting wrong thresholds from implicit rules; lifecycle ambiguity leaking into evidence posture.
notes: Operator reframe 2026-05-12: no_clob_book_published on top-volume past-end rail-pinned market is a terminal-lifecycle signature, not a measurement failure — encoded in priority ordering. Components retained for calibration.
one_line: Classifies each Polymarket identity row into one of 10 typed lifecycle states (DISCOVERY, OPEN_THIN, OPEN_LIQUID, INFORMATION_SHOCK, CLOSING_WINDOW, EVENT_PASSED_UNRESOLVED, DE_FACTO_TERMINAL, FORMALLY_RESOLVED, ARCHIVED_OR_STALE, DATA_CONFLICT) via a deterministic priority-ordered rule cascade, retaining continuous components so calibration can learn thresholds from data.
title: Polymarket lifecycle typed classifier — 10-state priority-ordered cascade
why_load_bearing: Without lifecycle classification, CLOB enrichment, calibration, and divergence cards cannot distinguish an actively priced question from a rail-pinned resolved one. CLOB_ENRICHMENT_ELIGIBLE_STATES frozenset gates which markets expect an order book — absent this, no_clob_book_published is misread as data failure rather than expected terminal-lifecycle observation.

candidate_fixture: Toy 7-lane miner output; v1 produces swarms/bifurcations/orphans with observational language only (any '?' is contract failure); v2 lifts to target_swarms with direction_consensus enum (BULLISH/BEARISH/CONTESTED/N/A); valid_prediction_targets = union of target_swarm and solo_target tickers (deterministic consistency check).
failure_mode_prevented: Hypothesis formation before structural pattern map is complete; mining nodes independently asserting cross-lane conclusions without a dedicated synthesis step; direction labels leaking into structural description prose.
notes: v1 language policy: observational/relational only; any '?' is contract failure. v2 lifts to direction_consensus but still forbids predictive framing. valid_prediction_targets must equal union of target_swarms and solo_targets tickers — deterministic consistency check baked into output contract.
one_line: Two sequential LLM reasoning nodes over 7-lane miner outputs: v1 maps swarms/bifurcations/orphans/confidence-asymmetries at structural co-movement level; v2 lifts to target_swarms, directional_conflicts, solo_targets, thesis_stress, valid_prediction_targets with direction_consensus labels.
title: Cross-lane Lab two-phase pattern mapper — v1 structural map → v2 target-swarm convergence
why_load_bearing: The only organ that explicitly fuses all 7 data lanes (stock, ETF, macro, news, polymarket, calculator, stockgrid) into a single cross-lane pattern map before hypothesis formation. Without v1→v2, downstream Oracle and decide nodes would have to perform cross-lane synthesis themselves, losing the separation between structural observation (v1) and target-surface mapping (v2).

candidate_fixture: Toy 3-group set; metric classification into 4 buckets (directional 1.0/risk 0.75/dispersion 0.65/structural 0.35); shrinkage priors per bucket (directional=6, risk=8, dispersion=9, structural=12); EFFECTIVE_SIZE_PRIOR=8; robust z-score (MAD) for n≥5; market_fusion_readiness marks calculator as derived_observation_only.
failure_mode_prevented: Raw magnitude ranking that treats large structural noise metrics as equally signal-bearing as directional metrics; size bias inflating large-cap clusters; overfitting to single dominant metric.
notes: Metric classification into 4 semantic buckets. Higher shrinkage priors for less actionable metrics. market_fusion_readiness explicitly marks calculator as derived_observation_only with refusal reason calculator_is_not_source_lane_root.
one_line: Fuses stock, macro, and ETF feed artifacts into sector/proxy group clusters, applies lane-specific Bayesian shrinkage priors and MAD-based robust z-scoring, then ranks groups by an Opportunity Score combining Energy, Purity, Cohesion, Participation Rate, Effective Breadth, and Directional Balance.
title: Calculator cross-lane group intelligence — size-aware Bayesian shrinkage + robust z-scoring
why_load_bearing: Converts raw multi-lane numeric feeds into a single ranked group intelligence artifact that downstream Lab miners (miner_calc) cite as a named derived-evidence source. Without it, no composite cross-lane thesis quality score exists — individual lane metrics are not comparable across stock/macro/ETF without the normalization and shrinkage pipeline.

candidate_fixture: Toy markets across all four lens shapes; zombie gate (p≥0.95 AND abs_change≤0.015 → all lenses zero); Newsbreaker further gated by resolved/min_volume/min_uncertainty/outlier velocity; God Mode mid-prob (0.2..0.8) only; Scout thin-market only; thresholds in tuning dict.
failure_mode_prevented: Zombie and resolved markets contaminating newsbreaker salience ranking; single-metric ranking collapsing structurally different attention signals into one ordinal; hardcoded thresholds blocking operator tuning.
notes: Zombie gate: p≥0.95 AND abs_change≤0.015 → all lenses zero. All thresholds in tuning dict keyed by lens name — externally configurable.
one_line: Decomposes prediction market salience into four orthogonal lenses (Hot Seat = log-volume × uncertainty; Newsbreaker = volume × velocity × uncertainty^power, zombie/resolved-gated; God Mode = volume × velocity for mid-probability; Scout = volume × uncertainty for thin markets), each capturing a distinct attention signal.
title: Polymarket four-lens scoring — Hot Seat / Newsbreaker / God Mode / Scout
why_load_bearing: Without the four-lens decomposition, all prediction market rows are ranked by a single metric (volume or change), collapsing four structurally different market situations into one ranking. A zombie market (high probability, near-zero change) would rank high on raw volume but should score zero on Newsbreaker.

candidate_fixture: Toy 8-fire schedule (open=09:30, close=16:00 ET + intraday); grace_window=120min catches delayed execution; stable_digest = SHA-256(kind, fire_point, market_date); same fire point on same date gets same digest, deduplicated; weekday filter suppresses weekend fires.
failure_mode_prevented: Cross-lane features from different wall-clock times being compared as contemporaneous; duplicate fire events on retry or restart; weekends triggering feed refreshes on markets that don't trade.
notes: snapshot_key = 'market_snapshot:{fire_point}:{market_date}' is the canonical cross-lane join key. Grace window 120min default catches delayed executions without double-firing.
one_line: Emits stable-digest-keyed market_fire events at configurable named fire points (open, close, + up to 6 intraday) in the market timezone, using a bounded grace window to catch delayed fires while deduplicating by stable digest so no fire point runs twice per market date.
title: Market clock time-anchor fire-point organ
why_load_bearing: All feed samples, feature rows, situation graphs, and mart artifacts need a common time anchor (market_date × fire_point) for any cross-feed or cross-run comparison to be coherent. Without the clock organ, each feed captures at arbitrary wall-clock times and there is no shared snapshot key binding multiple lanes to the same moment.

candidate_fixture: Toy candidate-axiom catalog with one explicit-id match, one WorkItem evidence match, one query-token-only match, and one autonomy-phrase match; first-contact packet emits the evidence rows, suppresses the query-only row, and validator fails any emitted row missing authority_posture=candidate_not_active_doctrine or non_law_warning.
failure_mode_prevented: Unpromoted axiom candidates being read as binding rules; fuzzy keyword overlap injecting provisional doctrine into cold-start context; candidate clauses losing labels, warnings, or drilldowns during compression.
notes: Distinct from agent_principle_lens: this is the candidate-axiom quarantine membrane, not the active agent-principle selector.
one_line: Entry and context packets may surface axiom candidates only as explicitly labeled provisional pressure: evidence-bearing matches emit candidate rows with non_law_warning and drilldowns, while first-contact query-token-only matches are suppressed into a receipt.
title: Candidate axiom runtime-pressure non-law gate
why_load_bearing: This lets unsettled axiomatic pressure steer Type A behavior without smuggling unpromoted doctrine into active law. Generic words like projection, runtime, or compression can explain a suppressed match, but only explicit ids/slugs, WorkItem evidence, selected artifact evidence, or whitelisted autonomy phrases become operating pressure.

candidate_fixture: Toy oversized entry packet with agent operating packet, principle lens, candidate pressure, closeout git state, route lease, and navigation spine; admission trims text-heavy sections under inline target while preserving the required control fields and recording before/after byte counts plus saved_bytes.
failure_mode_prevented: Cold-start entry packets fitting budget by deleting banned routes, direct-action permission, proof obligations, source-coupling warnings, or drilldowns; agents then over-route, under-prove, or treat omitted authority as absent.
notes: Sharpens omission_receipt_reversible_projection_boundary with the runtime admission floor that chooses what survives first-contact payload pressure.
one_line: The entry packet is budget-trimmed through section-specific compactors, but a protected floor preserves banned routes, route_lease permitted actions, ceremony proof, surface contract, freshness/currentness handles, source-coupling status, and omission receipts before emitting byte-accounting telemetry.
title: Entry-payload admission with non-negotiable control-field floor
why_load_bearing: Entry compression is only safe if it cannot trim away the fields that tell a cold agent what not to do, when to act directly, how to prove the action, and how to recover omitted authority. This organ turns context-budget pressure into accountable admission rather than silent deletion.

candidate_fixture: Toy session traces: one consumes lease via rg, one returns legitimately through coverage-enforcement, one repeats broad context-pack before direct action; reducer emits route_lease_mode_control signals, aggregate counts, and audit findings for the drift cases.
failure_mode_prevented: Agents repeatedly calling kernel after a selected lane; broad routing treated as execution; navigation anti-patterns known only through chat complaints rather than typed aggregate telemetry.
notes: Sharpens cogop_route_lease_executor by adding the observed-session feedback loop that proves whether the lease was actually consumed.
one_line: Agent execution traces are reduced after each --entry handoff to determine whether the emitted route_lease was consumed by direct data-plane action, legitimately returned to kernel, or drifted into bloat/repeated broad routing before action.
title: Route-lease mode-control trace feedback
why_load_bearing: A route lease is otherwise just a suggestion in a packet. This organ makes route consumption observable: full-output kernel bloat, second kernel calls before direct action, repeated broad routes, and known-path kernel calls become typed signals and aggregate findings that can train navigation standards and skills.

candidate_fixture: Toy Type A routing mistake creates one failure-mode CAP with required payload fields; later two sibling CAPs are searched and aggregated; decision ladder patches a skill for procedural failure, refines an existing principle for repeated cross-lane invariant, and rejects one overbroad mint.
failure_mode_prevented: Minting or rewriting active agent principles from one local correction; losing repeated Type A behavior failures in chat; CAPs too thin to support later principle diagnosis.
notes: This is stronger than a generic capture reflex: it names the promotion governance path for Type A behavior doctrine and the required evidence shape for future aggregation.
one_line: When a Type A behavior lesson feels principle-shaped but is still only a local case, the agent captures a rich agent_principle_failure_mode CAP with overgeneralization guards, searches sibling CAPs, and only then chooses narrower owner patch, existing-principle refinement, or new pri_* mint.
title: Agent-principle failure CAP assimilation loop
why_load_bearing: It prevents heated corrections or single-session mistakes from becoming broad active doctrine, while still preserving the failure class in a structured backlog that can aggregate into real agent-principle changes later. The loop is the safety valve between local behavior error and constitutional mutation.

candidate_fixture: Toy prompts containing permission-gated, micro-sliced, largest coherent, and autonomous seed phrases; packet emits operator_autonomy_pressure_phrase candidate rows plus action_autonomy_runtime_pressure diagnostic with active standard ref; sync test fails if matcher and diagnostic phrase lists diverge.
failure_mode_prevented: Type A over-asking permission or slicing work too small after the operator has already named the failure class; active standard hidden behind candidate_not_active_doctrine warnings; matcher and diagnostic phrase lists drifting apart.
notes: This is an agent-behavior control organ, not a generic prompt heuristic: it deliberately joins provisional axiom pressure to a settled entry-surface rule when the operator names autonomy failure.
one_line: Explicit operator phrases about permission-gating, micro-slicing, largest coherent waves, or autonomous seeds trigger both candidate-axiom pressure and an active entry-surface diagnostic row for the settled action-over-pointless-inaction standard, with runtime phrase lists kept in sync by tests.
title: Operator autonomy phrase to active-standard bridge
why_load_bearing: Without the bridge, an operator correction can surface only as provisional candidate pressure, causing Type A to treat an already-active standard as optional or unpromoted. The bridge binds natural-language autonomy complaints to the live governing rule while preserving the candidate/non-law boundary for adjacent axiom pressure.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic operator paragraph packet with one focus row and one context-only neighbor; bridge returns JSON-only source-index rows; importer accepts only emit_shards_for parents and quarantines parent-outside-boundary or untraceable voice-anchor rows.
failure_mode_prevented: Provider output blending neighbor context into provenance, emitting shards for paragraphs outside the packet, claiming global novelty or coverage from a local packet, or smuggling route/doctrine decisions into extracted_shards evidence.
notes: Sharpens the broad raw_seed_metabolism rows: the organ is the authority membrane around provider-visible source indexing, not the whole shard pipeline.
one_line: The bridge distillation lane turns a bounded focus paragraph packet into first-party source-index rows under an explicit visibility boundary, emit_shards_for authority set, context_only disambiguation rows, dynamic fact rows, and an omission receipt; bridge output is evidence only, never doctrine routing or mutation.
title: Raw-seed source-index packet scope membrane
why_load_bearing: This is the membrane that lets external/provider cognition help with raw-seed distillation without giving it global corpus authority, raw-seed write authority, or doctrine authority. It preserves operator voice provenance while preventing packet-local reasoning from masquerading as system-wide novelty or routing truth.

candidate_fixture: Toy paragraph ledger with an expired attempt, a reclaimed higher-epoch attempt, one partial-evidence attempt that forces operator_review_required, one digest-mismatched late import rejected into late_import_rejections.jsonl, and a preview mode proving no writes.
failure_mode_prevented: Expired raw-seed attempt imports obsolete shards; recovery erases in-flight history to make a paragraph look untouched; packet_digest includes itself and becomes unstable; partial imported evidence is auto-abandoned instead of sent to operator review.
notes: This is distinct from generic Work Ledger claiming: it is per-paragraph raw-seed import fencing with canonical packet digests and claim epochs at the importer boundary.
one_line: Every Type A raw-seed subagent attempt carries attempt_id, claim_id, monotonic claim_epoch, canonical packet_digest, and a lease window; stale recovery marks expired attempts, blocks auto-abandon when partial evidence exists, rejects late lower-epoch or digest-mismatched imports, and writes a fixed recovery report plus late-import audit rows.
title: Raw-seed attempt recovery with fencing tokens
why_load_bearing: It closes the overnight-corruption gap: stale provider/subagent work can return after the substrate has moved on. The fencing-token lane lets the system keep parallel raw-seed work without accepting obsolete shards, deleting history, or relying on human memory to notice late packets.

candidate_fixture: Three toy rows: one strong when_to_open match, one anti-trigger match that loses despite lexical overlap, and one absent-passport row demoted; context-pack emits affordance_compatibility labels and the validator reports passport coverage.
failure_mode_prevented: A generic context-pack or semantic search picks a high-overlap row whose not_when should exclude it; future agents rediscover activation conditions from prose; trigger phrase lists proliferate outside the standard-owned row.
notes: Sharpens compression_profile_governed_option_surface: the key organ is not the banded surface itself, but the row-owned passport that governs selection and demotion.
one_line: Rows that should be selected by future agents carry standard-owned affordance passports: cluster_keys, atom, when_to_open, when_not_to_open, safe_drilldown, landmines, and sufficiency_claims. Context-pack projects the passport into selected_rows and lets anti-triggers beat lexical similarity.
title: Affordance passport selection gate
why_load_bearing: It changes routing from bag-of-words retrieval to row-owned operational affordance. A cold agent can see not just that a row is similar, but when it is sufficient, when it is wrong to open, and which drilldown is safe, preventing semantic search from becoming accidental authority.

candidate_fixture: Four toy final responses: permission gate with no blocker, residual future work with no cap, stale dirty snapshot without fresh status, and capture-tripwire prose; each emits one diagnostic, while paired blocker/CAP/status variants pass.
failure_mode_prevented: A final answer contains future-work or side-finding prose with no CAP, asks for permission despite no blast-radius boundary, or refuses scoped commit based on session-start dirt rather than fresh path/claim proof.
notes: Complements, but does not duplicate, cap_reflex_capture_before_prose: this is the exit-side runtime mirror that also handles permission-gating and stale dirty-tree false blockers.
one_line: Before final-response text leaves the agent loop, pure egress detectors scan for permission-gate language without a real blocker, stale dirty-tree blockers without fresh authority, residual deliverables without WorkItem/CAP binding, and capture-reflex tripwires without capture; the Claude stop hook consumes those detectors as behavioral pressure.
title: Egress compliance stop-hook mirror
why_load_bearing: Most agent governance acts at entry, but many failures happen at exit: asking permission after safe work remains, hiding residual deliverables in prose, or citing stale dirty state as a commit blocker. This organ turns final prose itself into an audited surface before chat becomes the only memory.

candidate_fixture: Toy paper module with no-sorry language and only a dotted cell_id; valid cell resolves to formal_evidence_cell_present, unknown cell warns and still overclaims, missing_source cell errors and supplies no anchors.
failure_mode_prevented: A paper module says no-sorry or Lean-proved without a boundary, receipt, or WorkItem; authors hand-compose stale proof metadata; missing-source formal cells accidentally satisfy anchors; metaphorical proof vocabulary triggers false positives.
notes: Sharper than generic formal_math_lean organ: this is the proof-claim governance membrane for prose surfaces that discuss formal evidence.
one_line: Paper modules that use proof-strength vocabulary are audited against formal-evidence cells: high-signal claims require claim_boundary, receipt, and WorkItem anchors, and a resolved dotted cell_id can supply all three via the formal evidence-cell registry while missing, unknown, or incomplete cells emit typed findings.
title: Formal evidence-cell anchor resolver
why_load_bearing: It prevents formal proof language from becoming rhetoric. A module can cite one compact cell id instead of repeating proof metadata, but the validator expands that id into boundary, receipts, WorkItem, oracle split, and non-claims before allowing proof-strength language.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic failed Lean problem with stdout/stderr refs, support-affordance finder receipt, strict JSON canary, one transform job, reducer FAIL receipt, and terminal status proof_repair_lean_rejected_with_receipt; no benchmark success claim is allowed.
failure_mode_prevented: Repeated blind provider retries, provider prose counted as proof success, ground-truth proof leakage into repair context, missing support-affordance search before retry, or public benchmark claims after a private advisory repair attempt.
notes: This should fold into the formal-math cluster, but deserves separate capture because it is a complete provider-to-verifier repair metabolism, not just a Lean proofline state row.
one_line: After a proofline spine reaches proof-level Lean rejection, a narrow repair lane builds one input packet for one failed specimen, runs strict model inventory/canary checks, dispatches exactly one provider repair job when the owner WorkItem is ready, and cashes the result through the candidate-specific ArkLib Lake/Lean reducer; provider text remains advisory until the reducer receipt lands.
title: Formal proof repair one-row reducer gate
why_load_bearing: It makes AI-assisted proof repair honest: the provider may propose a body, but success is defined only by the Lean/Lake environment reducer under a claim boundary that forbids public benchmark claims. The lane binds failed feedback, support-affordance probes, prompt-boundary guard, output schema, and terminal receipt states into one governed attempt.

candidate_fixture: Toy 3-recipe set with minimal_4kb (4 kib, sections [statement, declared_imports]), skill_32kb (32 kib, sections [statement, skill_atlas_card, retrieved_premises]), strategy_classification_4kb (4 kib, deliverable_type=strategy_id_classification); section fill greedy with byte ceiling; omitted_sections emitted when section evicted; deliverable_type routes reducer to correct code path.
failure_mode_prevented: Provider receives ideal_body or oracle_needed_premise_ids (truth-side leakage); provider context exceeds token limits causing truncation artifacts; reducer cannot distinguish proof-synthesis receipt from strategy-advisory receipt.
notes: Budget is kib*1024 bytes hard-capped; approximate_tokens=ceil(bytes/4). strategy_classification_4kb deliverable_type is NOT lean_proof_body — it emits strategy_id_classification, routing reducer to different path. allowed_premise_ids empty for minimal/strategy recipes.
one_line: Six named recipes (minimal_4kb, premise_16kb, skill_32kb, repair_32kb, fewshot_64kb, strategy_classification_4kb) each declare an exact kib ceiling, ordered section list, graph_role, and deliverable_type; a byte-level truncation loop fills sections greedily and emits an omitted_sections manifest.
title: Provider context recipe budget policy
why_load_bearing: The recipe is the sole interface between the deterministic graph and the provider plane: it controls what context is visible and what is permanently withheld, enforces the proof-body / oracle-material leakage boundary at byte level, and sets the graph_role that the reducer uses to classify the receipt. Without this contract, providers receive either too little (missing premises/skill cards) or too much (truth-side leakage).

candidate_fixture: Toy 11-premise index with retrieval_terms; one synthetic train problem retrieves 3 of 4 needed premises (recall=0.75); ring2_list_map_map_index_miss intentionally omits map_map from allowed slice to validate PREMISE_RETRIEVAL_MISS classifier; precision and recall both metered against oracle_needed_premise_ids after the fact.
failure_mode_prevented: oracle_needed_premise not retrieved (proof impossible even with correct strategy); test-split premises leaked into train retrieval via allowed_for_split gap; retrieval miss attributed to proof-synthesis failure.
notes: v0 retrieval is deliberate BM25-lite (term overlap, no embedding). Premise index is hand-authored closed set of 11 Lean Std lemmas (Nat, List, Bool, Iff). PREMISE_RETRIEVAL_MISS is a first-class error class surfaced in oracle attribution.
one_line: Premises retrieved by counting query-term overlaps against a haystack of (premise_id, theorem_or_def_name, namespace, statement_excerpt, retrieval_terms); top-k by score returned as retrieved_premises; only premises whose allowed_for_split list includes the current problem split are eligible.
title: Prover premise retrieval term-scoring engine
why_load_bearing: Term scoring is the sole pre-oracle information channel between the informal problem statement and the Lean library: if the right premise is not retrieved here, neither skill selection nor proof synthesis can cite it. The split-aware filter enforces that holds-out can only receive premises allowed across all splits.

candidate_fixture: Toy 3-problem set: one with iff_split features (selected_strategy=iff_split, retrieval_terms expanded with Iff lemmas), one with recursive_data_induction features, one with no clear match (selected_strategy=unknown, STRATEGY_SELECTION_MISS classifier fires); strategy_classification_4kb recipe routes provider to emit strategy_id from known_strategy_ids enum; reducer rejects unknown id and rejects proof body emitted alongside classification.
failure_mode_prevented: Strategy drift between provider classification output schema and deterministic scorer enum; strategy selection miss misclassified as proof-synthesis fail; proof-body leakage through strategy advisory output.
notes: The strategy enum is locked between _strategy_cards() and _strategy_classification_output_schema() via _known_strategy_ids() — adding a new strategy requires updating both simultaneously or reducer rejects the provider advisory.
one_line: Eight named strategy cards (equality_normal_form, iff_split, constructor_injectivity, recursive_data_induction, membership_decomposition, composition_fusion, symmetry_or_orientation, contradiction_or_false_target) each carry trigger_features, negative_triggers, retrieval_expansion_terms, and likely_required_premises; a hypothesis scorer maps problem features to strategies before retrieval and before any oracle material is visible.
title: Mathematical strategy atlas and pre-oracle hypothesis scorer
why_load_bearing: Strategy selection determines which query terms augment retrieval (biasing premise recall) and which skill cell is overlaid in the 32kb context packs; a wrong strategy selection (STRATEGY_SELECTION_MISS) is a distinct error class that cannot be repaired by premise or proof-synthesis improvements alone.

candidate_fixture: Toy receipt with proof body citing `Nat.add_comm` not in allowed_premise_ids; classifier matches via QUALIFIED_SYMBOL_RE, returns UNDECLARED_LIBRARY_PRIOR (not PREMISE_BUDGET_VIOLATION); review outcome bridge_escalate. Separate receipt with cited_unallowed_premise_ids non-empty: classifier short-circuits and returns []; reducer routes to PREMISE_BUDGET_VIOLATION (retry).
failure_mode_prevented: Lean-accepted out-of-recipe library citations counted as recipe_policy_passed; budget violation and library prior conflated into one error class (losing quarantine path); symbol regex false positive when symbol already in cited_unallowed.
notes: Classifier is explicitly conservative: if cited_unallowed_premise_ids non-empty it short-circuits and defers to PREMISE_BUDGET_VIOLATION. Symbol regex only fires as residual net. Provider self-report via library_priors_used / undeclared_library_prior_symbols trusted first. Review outcome is bridge_escalate (not retry).
one_line: After Lean reduction, a regex `(Nat|List|Bool|Iff|Eq)\.[A-Za-z0-9_.']+` scans the proof body for qualified library symbols; symbols that match a known premise by theorem_or_def_name AND are not in allowed_premise_ids AND are not already caught as cited_unallowed_premise_ids are classified as UNDECLARED_LIBRARY_PRIOR and quarantined separately from PREMISE_BUDGET_VIOLATION, routed to bridge_escalate (not retry).
title: Undeclared library prior symbol classifier
why_load_bearing: Providers can cite library symbols neither in the explicit premise index nor in the recipe-supplied allowed set; without this classifier those uses would pass Lean (the symbol is real) but violate the evaluation contract — counting as recipe_policy_passed=False with a distinct quarantine route (bridge_escalate) from a simple budget violation (retry). The proof may be valuable even if out-of-recipe.

candidate_fixture: Toy support prefix with declaration index; 5 probes run (simp, ring_nf, aesop, first-of-three, simpa[my_lemma]); one probe succeeds (status=direct_local_probe_accepted, provider_retry_allowed=False); one negative case where prefix is broken (status=blocked_by_support_prefix_gap, provider_retry_allowed=False); one normal case (status=support_affordance_packet_ready_for_provider, provider_retry_allowed=True).
failure_mode_prevented: Provider retry dispatched against broken support prefix; provider retry dispatched when tactic surface is empty; direct local proof accepted by Lean counted as provider success; matched-lemma self-reference probed against the target theorem itself.
notes: Seven terminal statuses including direct_local_probe_accepted, blocked_by_support_prefix_gap, insufficient_accessible_premises, support_affordance_packet_ready_for_provider, provider_retry_not_worthwhile_until_new_strategy. prior_failure_class extracted from previous attempt's row_patch and evaluator stdout to avoid re-running same failed approach.
one_line: Before authorising a second provider proof-repair attempt, a deterministic finder runs a fixed tactic probe suite (simp, ring_nf, field_simp, aesop, first|simp|ring_nf|aesop, plus up to 8 matched-lemma simpa probes) against the pre-target support prefix and emits one of 7 terminal statuses that gate or block the retry.
title: Support affordance finder — local tactic probe before provider retry gate
why_load_bearing: Without this gate, provider retries fire even when the support prefix is broken, the tactic surface is empty, or no accessible declarations match the target theorem — all conditions where the provider cannot succeed and the retry burns compute and quota. The gate also catches direct local acceptance (no provider needed) and indexes accessible declarations for the provider packet.

candidate_fixture: Toy 10-problem manifest with provider advisory and deterministic selector both producing strategy_id per problem; comparator emits matches=6, comparable_count=8 (2 rejected for leakage); strategy_match_rate=0.75; provider_results_counted=False so a high match rate doesn't inflate proof success.
failure_mode_prevented: Provider strategy match rate computed against oracle proof success (conflating signals); leakage-rejected advisories counted in numerator; search-efficiency attribution to provider when deterministic selector would have chosen same strategy.
notes: comparable_count is intersection of accepted advisories and problems with deterministic strategy label. Search-efficiency deltas explicitly deferred to separate wave. Function does not call Lean or any provider.
one_line: After reducer acceptance of strategy advisory rows, a separate comparator computes strategy_match_rate = matches / comparable_count by aligning provider strategy_id choices against the deterministic strategy_control selector's choices on the same problem manifest; rejected (leakage, invalid_id, missing) advisories are counted separately and never enter the match numerator.
title: Provider strategy match comparator (advisory vs deterministic)
why_load_bearing: Without this comparator there is no signal for whether provider strategy classification improves over the deterministic baseline — and search-efficiency deltas (fewer transitions, lower depth) cannot be attributed to the provider advisory vs the underlying deterministic selector. The comparator also enforces that provider_results_counted=False: strategy match is never proof success.

candidate_fixture: Toy 9-tier ladder with 3 problems per ring0/ring1/ring2; difficulty_tag on each ProverProblem dataclass; ring2_retrieval_miss problem intentionally omits the needed premise from allowed_for_split; _validate enforces formal_ladder_heldout has ≥20 problems; no problem crosses tiers.
failure_mode_prevented: Treating all benchmark problems as equally hard masks which sub-skill is failing; promoting to external corpora before local ladder is stable; counting retrieval miss as proof synthesis failure.
notes: 9 tiers via difficulty_tag. ring1_repair_probe deliberately uses wrong candidate body to probe oracle repair. ring2_retrieval_miss intentionally withholds needed premise. formal_ladder_heldout requires 20-problem minimum via _validate. external_formal_benchmark_smoke uses MiniF2F Lean4 translation only (no Mathlib).
one_line: A named difficulty-ring ladder (ring0 → ring1_core_lemma → ring1_repair_probe → ring2_premise_retrieval → ring2_retrieval_hit_proof_failure → ring2_retrieval_miss_oracle_repair_probe → strategy_control → formal_ladder_heldout → external_formal_benchmark_smoke) organises every Lean benchmark problem into a tier, gates train/dev/test tuning per tier, and seeds deliberate retrieval-miss rows so the oracle classifies failure class rather than passing silently.
title: Formal problem ladder — difficulty-ring curriculum with split-gated tiers
why_load_bearing: Without the ring curriculum, all benchmark problems look homogeneous and the system cannot distinguish retrieval-miss failures from proof-synthesis failures; the ladder forces calibration evidence to be generated at each ring before promotion to the next. The 20-problem minimum on formal_ladder_heldout prevents premature promotion of unstable lanes.

candidate_fixture: Toy 10-problem set across target_shapes; action gate restricts decide to closed_nat_mod_decision, omega to int_linear_arithmetic, etc.; clean proof selection prefers no sorry + CLEAN axiom audit + shortest body + fastest check; status_transition_audit rejects ADAPTER_HINT→STATEMENT_ONLY_SOLVER_SUCCESS and ORACLE_REPAIR→FORWARD_SOLVER_SUCCESS.
failure_mode_prevented: Counting hint-assisted proofs as solver discoveries; crediting raw proof bodies instead of action policies; running expensive provider calls before a free tactic baseline exists; mixing oracle comparator results into headline solve rate.
notes: Target-shape classifier extracts from theorem_signature text tokens (not oracle labels). Score formula: posterior_score = success_rate - timeout_penalty + target_shape_match_bonus. Clean proof selection: no sorry, CLEAN axiom audit, shortest body, fastest check.
one_line: A deterministic single-tactic search that classifies every problem by target shape (closed_nat_mod_decision / int_linear_arithmetic / nat_arithmetic / conjunction / …), enumerates only shape-allowed actions, checks each in Lean, computes posterior_score = success_rate − timeout_penalty + target_shape_match_bonus per (target_shape × source_family × tactic_id) bucket, and enforces a status-class firewall preventing ADAPTER_HINT or ORACLE_REPAIR outcomes from being counted as forward solver discoveries.
title: Statement-only hammer bandit — target-shape action-value table with status-class firewall
why_load_bearing: Without this bandit, the system has no action-value baseline that is provably free of adapter and oracle contamination; it is the calibration point against which provider-recipe and skill-cell gains are measured. Target-shape routing precedes any expensive search.

candidate_fixture: Toy 10-variant comparison over 5-problem set; aggregate_report has pass_rate, repair counts, failure_taxonomy (14 classes), by_split/by_source dicts, premise_retrieval_metrics, strategy_control_metrics; graph_update_candidates fire only when failure classes exceed zero; run_hash SHA-256 of (problem_results + aggregate + graph_updates).
failure_mode_prevented: Adding new graph nodes without evidence they improve the binding constraint; mistaking retrieval miss for synthesis failure; promoting graph variants based on test-set tuning.
notes: 10 variants in GRAPH_VARIANTS constant. graph_update_candidates trigger thresholds (>=5 pass count triggers mathlib promotion candidate). run_hash provides regression detection.
one_line: Ten named graph variants (baseline → oracle_repair → premise_retrieval → strategy_control → skill_atlas_overlay → skill_foundry_overlay → source_guarded_strategy → source_guarded_foundry → tactic_portfolio → hammer_search) are run over the same problem set and compared on pass_count, fail_count, failure_taxonomy, by_split, by_source, premise_retrieval_recall, and strategy_selection_accuracy in a single aggregate report, with graph_update_candidates generated from the failure distribution.
title: Graph variant multi-arm benchmark comparison — 10-variant pass-rate and failure-taxonomy matrix
why_load_bearing: Without the multi-arm comparison harness the system cannot separate which graph node (premise retrieval, strategy selection, proof synthesis, oracle repair) is the binding constraint; adding a new node is useless without a comparison receipt. run_hash provides regression detection across runs.

candidate_fixture: Toy 5-annex registry (miniF2F=runnable_translation_smoke, PutnamBench=absent, LeanDojo=present_unrunnable_no_mathlib, formal_conjectures=runnable, mathlib=absent); selection_policy picks formal_conjectures as first runnable; no_lean3_mathlib_without_verified_environment rule prevents PutnamBench attempt; readiness_summary persisted as durable run artifact for diff over time.
failure_mode_prevented: Silent zero-result benchmark runs when corpus is absent; attempting Lean3+mathlib problems with Lean4-only toolchain; treating translation smoke as a direct benchmark claim.
notes: Corpora checked: ulamai_annex, ulamai_checkout, frontiermath_solver_annex, frontiermath_solver_checkout, formal_conjectures_checkout, leandojo_checkout, minif2f_checkout, putnambench_checkout, mathlib_checkout. miniF2F smoke translates Lean3 to Lean4+Std (Mathlib absent). Source proof bodies withheld from forward candidates even in smoke lane.
one_line: Before any benchmark dispatch, the harness checks each external theorem corpus annex (miniF2F, PutnamBench, LeanDojo, formal-conjectures, mathlib) for local existence and runnable status, records `exists / runnable_in_this_wave / reason`, selects the first runnable slice, and enforces the rule that Lean3/mathlib corpora are not attempted without verified environment.
title: External corpus readiness gate — annex-by-annex runnable check
why_load_bearing: Without this gate, benchmark dispatch silently fails or produces zero results when an expected corpus annex is absent or needs a different toolchain; the readiness check surfaces the gap as a corpus-readiness artifact rather than a silent empty run. The miniF2F translation-smoke is the only currently-permitted ingestion path because Mathlib is absent.

candidate_fixture: Toy 3-problem manifest serialization: forward_safe row has proof_body_withheld_until_oracle=True, ideal_body and repair_body absent; validate scans for forbidden_keys = {candidate_body, ideal_body, repair_body, retrieval_body}; statement-only bandit additionally records forbidden_forward_material in each row's own metadata; oracle_only block records which fields available after oracle but not before.
failure_mode_prevented: Proof body leaking into forward lab context invalidating pass-rate evidence; test-split problems being used as tuning signal; oracle comparator successes inflating headline solver solve rate.
notes: Three independent enforcement layers: _forward_safe_problem_row strips fields; _validate hard-checks leakage_count==0 + forbidden_keys; statement-only manifest records forbidden_forward_material in each row's metadata. Distinct from oracle_cp2_grounding_firewall (CP2 universe) and provider_receipt_truth_side_leakage_detector (audits provider responses).
one_line: Every benchmark manifest serialization path strips candidate_body, ideal_body, repair_body, oracle_needed_premise_ids, and expected_strategy_ids from forward-visible rows; _validate() hard-checks truth_side_leakage_count == 0 and that no forbidden key appears in serialized manifest rows; test-split tuning is recorded as 'forbidden' in every manifest's leakage_policy block.
title: Benchmark split leakage firewall — manifest serialization strips proof bodies
why_load_bearing: Without enforced serialization stripping the benchmark's train/dev forward-lab context will silently include proof bodies, turning evaluation into a copy task and invalidating all pass-rate evidence. Three independent enforcement layers ensure no single regression can open a leakage path.

candidate_fixture: Toy 5-problem batch run through three lanes (baseline 2 pass, skill_foundry 3 pass, oracle_repair 4 pass); oracle_result_index keyed by lane_id; aggregate report shows skill_foundry gain over baseline (1 problem) without oracle_repair's 4-pass count contaminating forward solve rate.
failure_mode_prevented: Oracle ideal bodies silently inflating forward pass rates if comparator boundary is misconfigured; skill_foundry gains over baseline being undetectable without parallel lane comparison; sequential rather than parallel execution causing batch-to-batch drift contamination.
notes: Three lanes share problem_batch_manifest and premise_index; outputs merged into oracle_result_index keyed by lane_id. lab_oracle_evolve_pipeline paper module formalizes the contract.
one_line: The continuous Lab-Oracle-Evolve runner executes three graph-variant lanes (strategy_control_graph_v0 baseline, skill_foundry_overlay forward Lab, oracle_repair_graph_v0 comparator) over the same problem batch in one run_loop() call; the key invariant is oracle_repair_counts_as_forward_success=False — oracle repair measures what ideal bodies would produce, not a forward success signal.
title: Continuous lab-oracle loop — three-lane parallel execution with oracle repair as comparator
why_load_bearing: Without three-lane parallel execution against a shared problem_batch_manifest, the system cannot measure whether skill_foundry gains over baseline are real or whether oracle ideal bodies would unlock more problems than forward lab actually does. The False invariant on oracle_repair_counts_as_forward_success is the contamination boundary: a single attribute flip would silently let oracle ideal bodies inflate forward pass rates.

candidate_fixture: Toy queue with three 429-failed jobs; rate_limit_state shows window-bounded cooldown; continuous loop tick proceeds with local Lean work; separate provider_trickle_lane resume runs through ladder_eval with resume_limit parameter; next_resume_command pointer surfaced for operator visibility.
failure_mode_prevented: Provider 429s blocking local Lean progress; provider quota exhaustion misclassified as proof failure contaminating math signal; lost retries when provider dispatch is inline in the controller.
notes: provider_trickle_queue.json and rate_limit_state.json are first-class artifacts. Provider resume runs through ladder eval script, not continuous loop. Direct provider dispatch inside loop is explicitly False.
one_line: The continuous runner maintains a rate-limit-aware provider trickle queue (provider_trickle_queue.json, rate_limit_state.json) that holds 429 and missing-status transform jobs from the formal ladder eval plane; the enforced invariant is rate_limit_is_math_failure=False — provider quota exhaustion is a throughput event, never a proof failure signal.
title: Provider trickle queue — 429/missing jobs queued without blocking local Lean
why_load_bearing: Without the trickle queue, provider 429s would either block local Lean progress (latency) or be lost (no resume). Without the False invariant on rate_limit_is_math_failure, the math correctness signal would conflate with provider quota state and contaminate oracle grading. Provider resume runs through the ladder eval script, not the continuous loop controller — separating throughput control from math control.

candidate_fixture: Toy 3-artifact ingestion: one verisoftbench (target_theorem normalized to remove := or := by), one constructivebench (header imports extracted, theorem_part_without_answer split off), one synthetic regression containing 'lean_proof_body' key (resolution receipt status=FAIL, no transform job built); candidate_priority sorts verisoftbench rows first.
failure_mode_prevented: Oracle ground-truth proofs leaking into lab transform jobs through an ingestion regression; benchmark-shape coupling causing constructivebench-style fields to appear in verisoftbench rows; downstream proof hypothesis jobs missing decision_point_plan context because priority order is wrong.
notes: FORBIDDEN_RESOLUTION_KEYS = 14 truth-side keys (answer, lean_proof_body, ground_truth_proof, decision_point_plan resolved fields, etc.). Two constructors: verisoftbench (normalize := and := by), constructivebench (extract header imports, split theorem_part_without_answer). candidate_priority sorts oracle-ingress VeriSoftBench rows first.
one_line: The oracle ingress adapter resolves decision-point strategy artifacts into formal_problem rows via two benchmark-specific constructors (verisoftbench: target_theorem with :=/:= by normalization; constructivebench: theorem_part_without_answer with header import extraction); before any transform job is built, a FORBIDDEN_RESOLUTION_KEYS set is checked across all resolved row keys — if answer, lean_proof_body, ground_truth_proof, or 11 other truth-side keys appear, the leakage guard status flips to FAIL.
title: Oracle ingress adapter — two-format resolution fan-out with forbidden-key leakage gate
why_load_bearing: This is the boundary between oracle truth artifacts and forward-Lab transform jobs. Without the typed resolution receipt + forbidden-key gate, an oracle ingestion regression could silently leak ground-truth proofs into the lab's job manifests. candidate_priority sorts oracle-ingress VeriSoftBench rows first so the first downstream proof hypothesis job is maximally likely to have decision_point_plan context.

candidate_fixture: Toy 5-problem batch: 2 foundry-FAIL + oracle-repair-pass → mine_oracle_repair_into_skill_candidate; 1 foundry-FAIL + oracle-repair-FAIL → quarantine_until_better_oracle_signal; 2 foundry-pass (no candidate generated); 1 candidate retested on perturbation family, only then promoted; context_recipe_update_candidates similarly flags 429-rate-limit patterns without touching recipes.
failure_mode_prevented: Skill_foundry overfitting to oracle-repair successes without heldout retest; oracle-repair-FAIL signals being silently discarded instead of routed to quarantine for better-oracle-signal pickup; in-loop mutation contaminating downstream lane comparisons.
notes: Two candidate streams: skill_update_candidates.json (from foundry-FAIL vs oracle-repair diff), context_recipe_update_candidates.json (premise-budget violations + 429 patterns). Promotion policy is advisory_only_until_retested_on_heldout_or_perturbation_family.
one_line: After each loop pass, the continuous runner builds skill_update_candidates.json from the diff of foundry failures against oracle-repair successes; for every foundry-FAIL problem, if oracle-repair succeeded the recommendation is mine_oracle_repair_into_skill_candidate, else quarantine_until_better_oracle_signal; promotion policy is advisory_only_until_retested_on_heldout_or_perturbation_family — candidates are not applied in-loop.
title: Skill update candidate promotion — advisory-only with oracle-repair-backed routing
why_load_bearing: Skill_foundry without an Evolve gate would either accept every oracle-repair success as a learned skill (overfitting) or never learn (no gate). The advisory-only quarantine lane is the Evolve node specific to formal math: outcomes are candidates, not mutations, and quarantine requires retesting on heldout or perturbation families before promotion.

candidate_fixture: Toy lane attempts to claim gate status without rollback artifact: promotion-rule check fails; lane with all six met: gate claim accepted. Amber retry: 2 max, second retry forced to stronger independent verifier; exhaustion downgrades to red with operator exception packet. Operator receives periodic digest with aggregate counts, never per-row routine diffs.
failure_mode_prevented: Lane self-declaring 'autonomous' without rollback/probe/fingerprint scaffolding; amber retry loop with same verifier (no escalation discipline); routine green work routed to operator as approvals (human_as_validator_bottleneck).
notes: Six promotion-rule conditions: explicit path scope, deterministic verifier set, independent adjudicator option, rollback artifact, route probe, fingerprint contract. Amber max_retries=2 with second retry mandatory verifier escalation. Operator periodic digest only.
one_line: A lane may claim autonomous proof gate status only after satisfying six promotion-rule conditions (explicit path scope, deterministic verifier set, independent adjudicator option, rollback artifact, route probe, fingerprint contract); three gate classes — green (auto-apply, receipt), amber (max 2 retries with second retry forced to stronger independent verifier, exhaustion downgrades to red), red (operator exception packet with decision_needed + why_machine_gate_failed + safe_fallback) — and the operator sees only a periodic digest.
