title: Autonomous proof gate — promotion rule and amber bounded retry with mandatory verifier escalation
why_load_bearing: The wave-2 `autonomous_proof_gate_three_color_default` row named the three colors; this row names the promotion contract that gates which lanes may even claim gate status, and the bounded amber-retry policy with mandatory verifier escalation on second pass. Routing routine green work to the operator is named anti-pattern human_as_validator_bottleneck. Without the promotion rule, any lane could self-declare 'autonomous' and bypass the operator without proper rollback/probe/fingerprint scaffolding.

candidate_fixture: Toy overnight_ledger.jsonl with three planned dry-runs; cap_quick capture surfaces 'executor gap: no bridge worker consumes ledger'; frontend /station/lab-oracle-evolve shows selected_count=3, estimated_spend=$0.12, budget=$5; running overnight_ledger.py appends one more row without any model call; no log of dry-runs actually executing.
failure_mode_prevented: Treating the overnight ledger as a learning surface when it is only a plan surface; Evolve dry-runs accumulating as plans without ever executing; budget estimation being conflated with budget spending.
notes: Frontend surface /station/lab-oracle-evolve shows selected/eligible count, estimated spend/budget, selected preview. The pipeline paper module names the gap explicitly: 'the ledger is a plan, not an executor'.
one_line: The overnight Evolve ledger (state/lab_oracle_evolve/overnight_ledger.jsonl) is a JSONL planning artifact populated by overnight_ledger.py: resolves READY/DEGRADED run pairs, estimates abstract bridge capacity per pair, selects Evolve dry-run commands up to a budget, and appends a ledger row without spending bridge or model budget; the gap is that no bridge worker consumes the ledger — dispatching the selected dry-runs in order remains unwired.
title: Lab-Oracle-Evolve overnight ledger — budgeted plan surface with no active executor
why_load_bearing: This row names the execution-closure gap explicitly: there is a plan surface (the ledger), there is a frontend surface (/station/lab-oracle-evolve), there is even a bridge-budget estimator — but no executor consumes the ledger to actually run the selected dry-runs. The gap is load-bearing because it explains why Evolve mutations accumulate as plans rather than as receipts. Recording the gap as a typed organ makes it actionable.

candidate_fixture: Toy repair lane: attempt 1 with strategy=induction_then_simp → terminal status lean_rejected; attempt 2 with same strategy → blocked by gate; attempt 3 with strategy=cases_then_omega → allowed (new strategy); attempt_count field always =1 per request.
failure_mode_prevented: Infinite retries on the same failing tactic; blocking genuinely-novel repair attempts on attempt count; loose coupling between strategy selection and retry policy.
notes: PROOF_REPAIR_TERMINAL_STATUSES set lists terminal statuses (lean_rejected, provider_unavailable, timeout, schema_failed, prompt_boundary). dispatch_policy.reason logic in build_repair_lane gates re-entry. attempt_count always =1 in provider_repair_request_contract.
one_line: The repair lane allows exactly one provider attempt per strategy; a terminal attempt status (lean_rejected, provider_unavailable, timeout, schema_failed, prompt_boundary) locks the lane and re-entry requires the caller to demonstrate a new strategy — the gate is `provider_retry_requires_new_strategy_after_terminal_attempt: true` and budget is a strategy-novelty predicate, not a counter.
title: Proof repair budget enforced as strategy-novelty gate, not attempt count
why_load_bearing: Separates retry policy from exploration policy: any new repair attempt must carry a structurally different strategy, preventing infinite retries on the same failing tactic. Without strategy-novelty enforcement, repair lanes either retry the same approach indefinitely (waste) or block retry on count (missing the case where a genuinely new approach is available).

candidate_fixture: Toy 3-failure batch: one proof-level rejection (rejected tactic chain, `unknown_module_prefix=false`) → repair lane; one unknown-module-prefix failure → environment-fix lane; one compile-schema failure → schema-fix lane. Each lane has separate budget and policy.
failure_mode_prevented: All Lean failures collapsed into one queue; repair budget being consumed by environment/schema failures that need a different lane; synthesis being attempted on rejected proof bodies (wrong lane).
notes: failed_judgment.proof_level field in proof_repair_input_packet. lean_rejection_is_proof_level from env_receipt. unknown_module_prefix as distinct judgment field.
one_line: A failed specimen enters the repair lane only when the Lean verifier's rejection is `proof_level=true` (field `lean_rejection_is_proof_level` in the environment gate receipt); unknown-module-prefix failures, compile-schema failures, and import-resolution failures route to different lanes; the repair lane's input packet carries `failed_judgment.proof_level` as the classification key.
title: Repair candidate selected by Lean rejection level (proof-level vs other)
why_load_bearing: Defines the precise boundary between synthesis (generate new proof body) and repair (fix existing rejected proof body). The routing key is not a score or error string — it is a boolean derived from the Lean verifier output level. Without this boundary, all Lean failures collapse into one queue and repair-vs-synthesis budget is unbounded.

candidate_fixture: Toy 3-failure batch: one with target theorem name in proof text → target_theorem_self_reference; one with 'unknown identifier' in stdout → unknown_identifier_or_support_gap; one with generic 'tactic failed' → proof_search_fail. Each lane's repair_contract carries different `forbidden_self_reference` / `prior_unknown_identifier_feedback` fields.
failure_mode_prevented: All failed repairs receiving the same retry prompt regardless of Lean error class; missing the routing power of stdout text to shape the next attempt; conflating self-reference, support gap, and synthesis failure into one generic 'try again'.
notes: _prior_failure_class function. prior_failure_class field in receipt. forbidden_self_reference and prior_unknown_identifier_feedback fields in repair_contract section of context pack.
one_line: The affordance finder reads the prior repair attempt's row patch and Lean stdout to route into one of three failure classes: target_theorem_self_reference_or_invalid_premise (target name in proof text OR 'type mismatch' error), unknown_identifier_or_support_gap ('unknown identifier' OR 'unknown constant' in stdout), proof_search_fail (residual); each class carries a different strategy instruction to the provider in the repair context pack.
title: Prior failure class triage — three repair sub-lanes from one Lean signal
why_load_bearing: One Lean error signal fans out into three structurally different provider prompts. The triage is deterministic and grounded in Lean output text — no model scoring. Without it, every failed repair gets the same retry prompt, losing the routing power of the Lean error class to shape the next attempt.

candidate_fixture: Toy 5-experiment queue: one with `obligation_node_refs=['obl_42']` and obl_42 exists → explicit_node_ref; one with `obligation_node_refs=[]` and `obligation_node_refs_declared=True` → explicit_no_obligation_ref; one with refs=['obl_missing'] → explicit_refs_unresolved_fallback; one with `obligation_node_refs_declared=False` and namespace match → namespace_loose_fallback; one with no DAG link at all → no_dag_link.
failure_mode_prevented: Weak namespace-level associations silently inheriting credibility of explicit node refs; flat priority queues hiding the difference between authored and inferred DAG linkage; downstream consumers misreading namespace fallback as a strong claim.
notes: obligation_join_quality field, _build_dag_link_index, refs_declared logic, explicit_refs_unresolved_fallback_namespace_loose branch.
one_line: Every queue item carries both a `classification` (ready/blocked/boundary) and an `obligation_join_quality` from a five-tier ladder: `explicit_node_ref` > `explicit_no_obligation_ref` > `explicit_refs_unresolved_fallback_namespace_loose` > `candidate_refs_present_namespace_loose_fallback` > `namespace_loose_fallback` > `no_dag_link`; the `obligation_node_refs_declared` bool controls whether namespace-loose fallback is allowed.
title: Decisive-experiment queue obligation join quality ladder
why_load_bearing: Separates the 'is there an experiment?' question (classification) from 'how confident is the DAG linkage?' question (join quality). Without the ladder, weak namespace-level associations would silently inherit the credibility of explicit node refs. The ladder makes the falloff in join confidence explicit and queryable.

candidate_fixture: Toy 3-experiment queue: one with dag_link.hinge=True → hinge_open_dag_node (priority 1); one with cell.has_action_edge=True → manifest_action_edge (priority 2); one with neither → registry_inventory_only (priority 3); no posterior or expected-information weights involved.
failure_mode_prevented: Learned scoring function overfitting to past selections; flat queue treating all experiments equally; loss of pilot author's structural-importance judgment under model-driven priority.
notes: priority_basis field construction. dag_link.get('hinge') check. has_action_edge from cell. hinge_open_dag_node value observed in live queue data.
one_line: Queue items carry a `priority_basis` with three values: hinge_open_dag_node (DAG link exists AND `hinge=true`) > manifest_action_edge (cell has `has_action_edge=true` in registry) > registry_inventory_only; there is no Bayesian scoring, expected-information calculation, or coverage metric — priority is a deterministic function of authorial assertion (`hinge=true`) plus registry action-edge presence.
title: Decisive experiment priority — hinge-node first from obligation DAG, not score-based
why_load_bearing: Shows a deliberate design choice: decisive selection is author-declared structural importance, not learned or computed. Hinge nodes represent the pilot author's judgment about which nodes collapse the proof if proven. Without authorial priority, the queue would either need a learned scoring function (overfitting to past selections) or treat all experiments equally (no triage).

candidate_fixture: Toy 11-entry index covering Nat (add_comm, add_assoc, mul_comm), Bool (and_comm, or_comm), List (length_append, map_append, append_assoc), Iff (intro, mp, mpr); each entry has retrieval_terms, namespace, source_ref to Init/, allowed_for_split=[train, dev, test]; Mathlib corpus marked absent.
failure_mode_prevented: Entire Std namespace becoming implicit allowlist; undeclared library priors slipping through because no closed set exists; retrieval over an unbounded library surface.
notes: 11 hand-curated Init/Std lemmas. Each entry: theorem_or_def_name, namespace, source_ref, retrieval_terms, statement_excerpt, allowed_for_split. Mathlib marked absent. Leakage policy: proof bodies excluded, oracle_needed_premise_ids hidden from lab.
one_line: Curated registry of 11 Init/Std lemmas (Nat, Bool, List, Iff namespaces) serving as the sanctioned library prior; each entry carries retrieval_terms, namespace, source_ref to the toolchain Init path, theorem_or_def_name, statement_excerpt, and allowed_for_split eligibility; Mathlib and LeanDojo are tracked as absent corpus entries.
title: Lean/Std toolchain premise index
why_load_bearing: This is the authoritative library-prior boundary. Without an explicit closed-set premise index, the system cannot distinguish 'sanctioned library lemma' from 'undeclared prior'. The hand-authored curation forces conscious admission of each lemma rather than letting the entire Std namespace become an implicit allowlist.

candidate_fixture: Toy proof state with 5 local hypotheses (h1, h2, hxy, hyz, hcontra); selector lexical-matches against goal text mentioning x and y → selected_facts=[hxy, hyz]; dependency-family filter excludes hcontra (unrelated dependency family); trace row records selector_id, selected_facts, target_shape, selection_reason.
failure_mode_prevented: Proof-state search with no local-fact policy; all hypotheses treated equally in tactic substitution; inability to attribute failed transitions to selection misses vs synthesis failures.
notes: Named selector v0 is BM25-style lexical. Dependency family filter excludes hypotheses outside the current goal's dependency closure. Embedding-based selection is a future variant (not implemented).
one_line: Named selector (selector_id: lexical_plus_dependency_family_v0) picks which local hypothesis names to surface as selected_facts for each proof-state transition; selection is purely syntactic: lexical match against hypothesis names in the current proof-state context, filtered by proof-state dependency family; no embedding or semantic retrieval; each trace row records selector_id, policy_id, selected_facts, target_shape, premise_miss flag, selection_reason.
title: Lexical-plus-dependency-family premise selector
why_load_bearing: Distinct from global premise-index retrieval: this is the LOCAL hypothesis selector that picks which goal-context facts to surface per transition. Without it, the proof-state search has no policy for which local facts to make salient; without trace-row recording, learning from selection mistakes is impossible.

candidate_fixture: Toy probe across 8 tactics: rfl/decide/omega/simp/simp_all/grind pass; aesop fails with TACTIC_SEARCH_FAIL (Mathlib import missing); native_decide passes; tactic_portfolio_availability artifact keyed by portfolio_id with compile_status per tactic.
failure_mode_prevented: Search crashes mid-execution from calling unavailable tactic; stale static allow-list disconnected from actual environment; Mathlib gaps assumed-away rather than detected.
notes: Probe compiles minimal test file per tactic. aesop fails with TACTIC_SEARCH_FAIL (requires Mathlib). Typed availability record per portfolio_id.
one_line: Per-run live probe that compiles a minimal test file for each candidate tactic (rfl, decide, native_decide, omega, simp, simp_all, grind, aesop) under the declared import set (Std only in current runs) and records compile_status, duration_ms, error_class; aesop fails with TACTIC_SEARCH_FAIL because it requires Mathlib; grind passes; result is a typed tactic_portfolio_availability record keyed by portfolio_id.
title: Tactic portfolio availability probe
why_load_bearing: This probe is what determines which tactics are CALLABLE before any proof search begins — the Mathlib availability gap is detected here, not assumed. Without per-run probing, the system would either crash mid-search when calling an unavailable tactic or maintain a stale static list of 'allowed' tactics that doesn't reflect the actual environment.

candidate_fixture: Toy 4-goal batch: `(2+3) % 5 = 0` (closed_nat_mod_decision → decide only); `x + 2 ≤ y` (int_linear_arithmetic → omega only); `A ∧ B` (conjunction → simp_all or constructor); `True` (true_intro → exact True.intro). Each tactic outside the admissible set is rejected at the routing gate before any Lean call.
failure_mode_prevented: Search trying every tactic on every goal; wasted Lean compile cycles on shape-inadmissible tactics; loss of target-shape signal as routing input.
notes: Closed enum of target shapes. Each shape has admissible tactic list. Gate precedes hammer bandit and proof-state search. simp/simp_all/rfl always allowed regardless of shape.
one_line: Gate organ that classifies each proof goal into a target_shape (closed_nat_mod_decision, int_linear_arithmetic, conjunction, disjunction, nat_arithmetic, rat_normalization, true_intro, equality, existential, false_elim) and admits only the tactic or body template admissible for that shape before any search; examples: closed_nat_mod_decision admits decide; int_linear_arithmetic admits omega; conjunction admits simp_all or and_comm_intro_constructor_template; true_intro admits exact True.intro template.
title: Target-shape tactic routing gate
why_load_bearing: This gate precedes the hammer bandit loop and the proof-state search loop and is what determines which library entry points are even attempted. Without target-shape routing, the search would try every tactic on every goal, wasting Lean compile cycles on tactics with no chance of succeeding on the current shape.

candidate_fixture: Toy corpus_readiness_v0 artifact: mathlib_available=False (probe returns ENVIRONMENT_FAIL with 'unknown module prefix Mathlib'); miniF2F-lean4-mathlib=translation_smoke_only_lean3_mathlib_source; LeanDojo=absent; Pantograph=absent; consumers query mathlib_lake_project_import_available before attempting any Mathlib-dependent proof.
failure_mode_prevented: Mathlib-dependent proof attempts crashing mid-search; consumers downstream of run unable to query Mathlib availability; corpus state assumed rather than probed per-run.
notes: Per-run artifact distinct from dispatch-side annex ladder. Mathlib probe returns ENVIRONMENT_FAIL with 'unknown module prefix Mathlib'. Typed flags mathlib_available / mathlib_direct_import_available / mathlib_lake_project_import_available.
one_line: Per-run artifact (corpus_readiness_v0) that checks availability of each external corpus (mathlib, miniF2F-lean4-mathlib, PutnamBench, ProofNet, LeanDojo, Pantograph) and records readiness_status (present / translation_smoke_only_lean3_mathlib_source / absent) plus mathlib_available, mathlib_direct_import_available, mathlib_lake_project_import_available flags.
title: Corpus readiness / Mathlib absence gate
why_load_bearing: Authoritative gate marking the boundary between lean_std-reachable and Mathlib-reachable proof space. Distinct from `external_corpus_readiness_gate_annex_ladder` which is dispatch-side selection; this is the per-run availability artifact that consumers downstream of the run can query.

candidate_fixture: Toy 10-problem RING2 run: precision=0.36, recall=0.90; 1 retrieval_miss (correct premise not in retrieved set), 4 proof_failure_despite_hit (premise retrieved but proof fails for other reason); adversarial ring2_list_map_map_index_miss intentionally decoys allowed_premise_ids; leakage_policy: proof bodies withheld until oracle, test_split_tuning forbidden.
failure_mode_prevented: Retrieval improvements not measurable; proof-synthesis improvements attributed to retrieval (or vice versa); retrieval miss vs synthesis failure conflation.
notes: Live run shows precision 0.36, recall 0.90. Adversarial ring2_list_length_append_symmetry_hit_fail (correct premise wrong orientation), ring2_list_map_map_index_miss (decoy allowed_premise_ids).
one_line: Dedicated run harness (PROVER_BENCHMARK_RING2) evaluates premise retrieval quality over the lean_std toolchain premise index; each problem carries retrieval_query_terms and oracle-gated needed_premise_ids; aggregate report tracks premise_precision, premise_recall, retrieval_miss_count, proof_failure_despite_hit; adversarial cases include ring2_list_length_append_symmetry_hit_fail (correct premise retrieved but wrong orientation) and ring2_list_map_map_index_miss (decoy allowed_premise_ids supplied).
title: RING2 premise retrieval precision/recall harness
why_load_bearing: This is the precision/recall measurement organ that lets the system know if its premise retrieval is improving. Without per-run precision/recall and per-failure-mode subdivision (retrieval miss vs proof failure despite hit), retrieval-side and synthesis-side improvements cannot be attributed independently.

candidate_fixture: Toy 3-statement check: one statement references 'minpoly' but support prefix has no Mathlib import (MATCH_TERMS hit, status STATUS_INSUFFICIENT_PREMISES, provider short-circuited); one statement references no MATCH_TERMS (status STATUS_PROVIDER_READY); one statement references 'aeval' AND support prefix imports Mathlib.LinearAlgebra (MATCH_TERMS hit + import present, status STATUS_PROVIDER_READY).
failure_mode_prevented: Provider dispatch on Mathlib-dependent statement when support prefix has no Mathlib imports; wasted provider call on guaranteed failure; loss of lexical signal that statement requires unavailable library.
notes: MATCH_TERMS tuple of 16 Mathlib-specific declaration tokens. TACTIC_PROBES tuple of Mathlib-requiring tactics (simp_concrete_z, simp_powerbasis, simpa_powerbasis_gen, ring_nf, field_simp). Short-circuit statuses: STATUS_INSUFFICIENT_PREMISES, STATUS_BLOCKED_SUPPORT_GAP.
one_line: Deterministic step (build_formal_math_support_affordance_finder) between a statement-scope packet and a provider proof-repair attempt; indexes declarations in the legal pre-target support prefix and runs a small tactic/probe suite; contains a hard-coded MATCH_TERMS tuple (minpoly, minPoly, powerBasisSucc, PowerBasis, ConcreteBTField, definingPoly, aeval, Polynomial, monic, degree, irreducible, basis, gen, Tower, Binary, generator) used to detect when the statement or support prefix references Mathlib-specific declarations; TACTIC_PROBES list includes simp_concrete_z, simp_powerbasis, simpa_powerbasis_gen, ring_nf, field_simp — tactics that require Mathlib imports.
title: Support affordance finder — Mathlib declaration match-terms index
why_load_bearing: This is the local Mathlib-detection index. Without it, the system would dispatch a provider attempt on a Mathlib-dependent statement even when the support prefix has no Mathlib imports, guaranteeing failure. The MATCH_TERMS index is the lexical signal that triggers STATUS_INSUFFICIENT_PREMISES or STATUS_BLOCKED_SUPPORT_GAP short-circuit.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic cluster with five atom rows and one alchemy plan: concept/principle slots refine_existing, mechanism slot proposed_new only after hidden-mechanism heuristic passes; dry-run shows alchemy_lifted shard refs plus bidirectional doctrine edge; single-atom proposed_new rows are rejected as fragment noise.
failure_mode_prevented: Every session re-invents or skips assimilation; proposed_new atom noise becomes new doctrine; refine_existing recommendations get ignored; shard provenance and doctrine edges land separately; raw seed appears processed but never crosses into the graph.
notes: Sharpens but does not duplicate raw_seed_alchemy_doctrine_triple_synthesis: this row is the controller-owned judgment walk and slot discipline around alchemy, not the alchemy compiler itself.
one_line: Between atomization and apply, the controller binds a dense idea_group_id or orphan cluster, reads the alchemy plan, verifies each slot as refine_existing or proposed_new, and only then runs apply-alchemy so shard provenance and doctrine edges land together.
title: Raw-seed shard-assimilation controller walk
why_load_bearing: The extraction machine can faithfully produce shards forever without changing doctrine. This organ is the cognitive crossing that prevents atom-level fragment noise from becoming parallel principles and prevents alchemy output from being rubber-stamped without proving the existing node is wrong.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic campaign directory with ledger/log/coverage evidence for parser_import and reaction_barrier failures; miner emits stable finding_id/signature rows, bins at max eight findings and five target paths, campaign summary requires documentation_refresh_paths, replay defaults dry-run through apply-session-loop.
failure_mode_prevented: Repeated agent rediscovery becomes operator toil; stale or duplicate raw-seed machinery keeps being re-explained in chat; maintenance authoring mutates doctrine directly; a single broad maintenance packet overwhelms bridge workers.
notes: Sharpens the generic runtime_maintenance_finding row into the actual self-pruning campaign organ: evidence miner, bounded bins, bridge authoring, and gate-preserving replay are the load-bearing metabolism.
one_line: A separate raw-seed maintenance lane mines durable runtime evidence into typed runtime_maintenance_finding rows, collapses recurring incidents into stable hashes, bins them by mutation class, and optionally drives bridge authoring plus apply-session replay without hand-editing doctrine.
title: Raw-seed maintenance deterministic self-pruning campaign
why_load_bearing: Raw-seed distillation can keep extracting while stale, duplicate, superseded, or repeatedly failing runtime evidence accumulates. This organ makes hygiene AI-owned and deterministic: the same disk evidence produces the same findings and bounded bins, while mutation remains preview-first and gate-preserving.

candidate_fixture: Tiny std_standard_type_plane with one new artifact kind and no explicit renderer profile; builder auto-derives renderer passport, option-surface density bands, allowed read/validate actions, mutation guard, source authority, and stale-projection check; deleting stored passports makes --check fail with required_next_action.
failure_mode_prevented: A new artifact kind is visible in one wiki/front-end/page surface but has no source authority, no mutation lane, no option surface, stale renderer coverage, or projection freshness check.
notes: Supersedes the weak portion of standards_registry_executable_grammar_index and complements renderer_passport_type_plane_projection: the missing organ is the single-drop registration invariant that fans out to render, browse, validate, and mutate surfaces.
one_line: One type_plane_rows entry declares an artifact kind's governing standards, source authority, option surface, validation probe, mutation lane, projection refs, and renderer contract, then downstream builders derive kind atlas, option surfaces, System Atlas projection, and renderer passports from that single drop point.
title: Standard type-plane single-drop artifact contract
why_load_bearing: Without this organ, adding an artifact kind requires parallel registrations in standards, browse surfaces, renderers, validation, and UI mutation gates. The type-plane contract prevents projection surfaces from becoming authority and makes missing downstream coverage detectable instead of silently rendering generic pages.

candidate_fixture: Doctrine node with reference_groups pointing to a concept, mechanism, principle, extracted shard, raw_seed_paragraph, standard, repo_file, docs_route, and raw_seed_line_range. Resolver returns resolved capsules for supported kinds, supported_by_route_registry or deferred_owned receipts for route/deferred kinds, no unresolved supported kinds, and source_digest_sha256 for direct sources.
failure_mode_prevented: Reference shards look present but cannot be opened by a cold agent; supported target kinds fail silently; docs-route or line-range handles are mistaken for resolved source; provider context loses provenance or over-reads whole files.
notes: Distinct from source_capsule_provenance: this is the internal point-of-use resolver and addressability policy for doctrine references, not a public/private clip capsule.
one_line: Doctrine reference groups carry typed reference shards, but point-of-use compilers attach live reference_projection_capsule receipts that resolve principles, concepts, mechanisms, standards, raw-seed anchors, extracted shards, and repo files into bounded projection text, source digests, standards, drilldown routes, and explicit deferred states.
title: Doctrine reference point-of-use capsule resolver
why_load_bearing: Edges and citations are not enough for cold agents or provider packets. This organ turns references into auditable, budgeted, source-bound capsules at the moment of use, while preserving unresolved/deferred target kinds as first-class receipts instead of silently dropping provenance.

candidate_fixture: Registered toy lane with one row_job. Packet builder resolves lane_row, attaches standard_capsule and skill_capsule, includes closed_sets with allowed_patch_targets and valid_navigation_groups, adds failure_feedback retry memory, and provider input test asserts capsules are present while missing capsule registration degrades to unregistered_* flags.
failure_mode_prevented: Every lane hand-assembles worker prompts; providers invent navigation groups or patch targets; skill deltas never affect future packets; standard clauses are lost during compression; retry attempts repeat the same failure class.
notes: Sharpens population_lane_generic: the ledger already knows generic lanes exist, but not the load-bearing worker-packet organ that carries standard law, skill memory, closed sets, and failure feedback into provider execution.
one_line: Population lanes build provider row-job packets from the lane registry by attaching a standard_capsule, skill_capsule, target_context, closed sets, output schema, proof-gate summary, examples, anti-patterns, and retry memory, so workers inherit lane law and recent failure feedback instead of receiving bespoke prompts.
title: Population worker-context packet with standard and skill capsules
why_load_bearing: The always-on population system depends on provider workers making small source edits without knowing the whole repo. This organ gives each worker a bounded grammar and behavioral memory: what the standard requires, what the skill's gold examples look like, which closed sets are legal, and which failures the lane has recently learned from.

candidate_fixture: Temp HUD log with historical rows containing body fields, a fresh live row after cursor seed, and a forced projector exception. First sampler poll writes initialized cursor and emits no operator_bridge events; second poll emits exactly one sanitized operator_bridge AgentEvent with conversation trace_id; exception produces one stream.gap event without breaking the sampler.
failure_mode_prevented: Operator HUD actions remain invisible to mission-control surfaces; historical private bodies are slowly replayed into AgentTraceStore; repeated sampler polls duplicate action events; a projector bug breaks the whole observability poll instead of becoming a typed stream gap.
notes: Distinct from prompt_shelf_metadata_privacy_index and operator_trace_structurer_manual_intake: those govern HUD receipt metadata and manual trace intake. This organ is the live tail projector that joins operator HUD receipts into the canonical agent trace without backfilling private bodies.
one_line: The operator bridge does not leave HUD action receipts in a side ledger: a resident sampler seeds a cursor on first activation, projects only new hud_actions.jsonl rows into AgentTraceStore as canonical operator_bridge events, strips body-bearing fields defensively, dedupes by action_id, and emits stream.gap on projector failure.
title: Operator-bridge tail projector with privacy-preserving activation cursor
why_load_bearing: Operator-facing ChatGPT work is a live cognitive lane, but historical HUD logs may contain private bodies and should not be backfilled into the canonical trace. This organ makes operator actions visible to the same observability stack as Claude/Codex/metabolism while preventing privacy leakage, duplicate projection, and sampler crashes from becoming silent trace corruption.

candidate_fixture: Fake AgentTraceStore containing healthy Claude/Codex sessions, infrastructure heartbeat, and multiple auth-failure observer sessions. Reducer returns healthy missions, demoted_missions with demote_reason=auth_failure_loop and per-session event drilldowns, telemetry_quality noise_classes/stale_sources/projection_warnings, and stable activity/source-runtime constants for the frontend.
failure_mode_prevented: An auth failure loop or idle infrastructure stream looks like active work; frontend code invents its own activity taxonomy; raw trace events are suppressed instead of classified; concurrent-session warnings cannot be attributed to the right actor/work-lane.
notes: Sharpens agent_self_observability_plane: the broader plane exists in the ledger, but this captures the specific reducer organ that turns raw event firehose plus Work Ledger state into a mission-control projection with conservative noise demotion.
one_line: The mission-status surface composes AgentTraceStore status/replay, Work Ledger runtime status, session attribution, and telemetry-quality classifiers into a lossy operator projection that keeps meaningful live missions visible while demoting noisy auth-failure loops and stale infrastructure into explicit diagnostic lanes with raw drilldown refs.
title: Agent mission-status reducer with telemetry-quality demotion
why_load_bearing: Agent behavior management cannot depend on self-report or raw event volume. This organ lets the cockpit answer who is doing real work, which sessions are live/recent/stale, which streams are noisy, and where the raw proof lives, without deleting canonical events or letting infrastructure loops crowd out actual missions.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic world-model inputs for host_agent_dotfiles, frontend_navigation counts/drift_preview, and paper_modules freshness/status_counts. Aggregator returns drift_aggregate_v1 sorted by count, severity_counts with per-plane previews capped to five, and /api/drift returns the same slice from the world snapshot even when a source degrades to an empty shape.
failure_mode_prevented: Hook-role drift, navigation drift, and paper-module drift are each visible only inside their own lens; Station headers disagree about what needs attention; agents trust stale projected surfaces because no shared drift counter names the owning plane and preview evidence.
notes: Not a dissemination/status surface: this is the actual Station/world-model attention reducer that prevents drift from being recomputed inconsistently across cockpit lenses.
one_line: The world model rolls host-agent dotfile drift, frontend navigation drift, and paper-module freshness drift into one drift_aggregate_v1 attention surface with per-source counts, severity rollups, and bounded previews, exposed both inside WorldModelSnapshot and through /api/drift.
title: World-model cross-plane drift aggregate
why_load_bearing: A living substrate has many drift producers, and each cockpit lens re-summing its own drift silently creates inconsistent attention state. This organ makes drift a shared runtime projection: dotfile hook/role wiring, navigation graph captures, and paper-module freshness all report into one operator-visible counter without becoming source authority.

candidate_fixture: Temp family with extracted_shards, raw_seed index, concept, mechanism, phase, and cycle fixtures. Calls resolve_authority_chain for shard/raw_seed/concept/mechanism/phase/cycle and /api/authority/shard/<id>; asserts source rungs include shard, raw_seed_paragraph, concept, mechanism, relevant docs, and that unsupported/empty handles return None or 404.
failure_mode_prevented: Station nodes, UI receipt diffs, or cold-agent cards point at generated projections with no recoverable source ladder; shard/raw-seed/provenance clicks become dead ends; unsupported handles silently imply authority; UI semantic diffs compare rendered text without checking authority-chain fidelity.
notes: Distinct from doctrine_reference_point_of_use_capsule_resolver: that resolves doctrine reference bundles into budgeted capsules for packet/context use. This organ resolves runtime UI handles into authority chains for interactive drilldown and receipt comparison.
one_line: World-model UI handles resolve through a typed authority_chain_v1 router that maps principles, concepts, mechanisms, shards, phases, cycles, and raw-seed anchors to rungs carrying kind, id, label, summary, source_path, ontology links, and generated_at; unsupported or empty handles fail closed instead of letting projections masquerade as authority.
title: World-model authority-chain resolver for UI handles
why_load_bearing: The frontend and cold agents constantly see compressed handles. Without an authority-chain resolver, a click on a shard, mechanism, phase, or raw-seed anchor can only open a projection or rely on name memory. This organ turns compressed UI nodes back into source/provenance ladders and preserves the projection-not-authority boundary at the interaction point.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic ChatGPT snapshots with private sentinel text: create a provisional thread, promote it to conversation identity, update a streaming assistant tail, observe a visible tail window without shrinking history, handle blank vanish/reappear, rebuild projections from events.jsonl, and assert index/progress/candidate payloads omit private sentinel fields.
failure_mode_prevented: ChatGPT thread work disappears when the tab changes; agents paste entire private transcripts into public/tracked contexts; streaming partial responses overwrite complete memory; visible-window snapshots truncate prior history; stale tab targets lose conversation identity after URL promotion or blank-tab rollover.
notes: This is not a dissemination or release surface. It is the private runtime memory substrate that makes operator ChatGPT work queryable and recoverable without raw transcript leakage.
one_line: Operator ChatGPT tab state is recorded as a private append-only event log whose replay materializes per-thread JSON records, metadata indexes, prompt catalogs, semantic turn stacks, and privacy checks while raw bodies stay confined to ignored state or explicit operator-requested output.
title: Operator thread memory private event projection
why_load_bearing: Operator-mediated Type B work otherwise lives as volatile browser state or unsafe pasted transcript blobs. This organ gives the system durable per-thread memory, stable hashes, lifecycle recovery, streaming-tail merge behavior, and metadata-only browse surfaces without turning private raw text into tracked substrate.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic thread containing a continuation contract, WorkItem/CAP ids, validation and commit receipts, and private sentinel text. Continuation card exposes receipt types, response anatomy, safe ids, block counts, card fingerprint, and raw-drilldown handle; Type B markdown packet is copyable, privacy_scan clean, public_release_safe false, and contains no sentinel/private_preview/raw_turn_text markers.
failure_mode_prevented: External Type B sessions receive either too little context to reason or too much private transcript; copy actions drift from fresh thread state; Type B treats metadata as public-safe or authoritative over repo state; Type A loses the exact receipt/CAP/commit handles needed to verify continuation.
notes: This sharpens the Type B-to-Type A loop with an actual runtime shuttle organ: continuation is grounded by metadata, receipts, anatomy, and handles, not by transcript dumping or static prompt prose.
one_line: Thread memory compiles a metadata-only continuation card, operator/assistant progress lens, and copyable Type B handoff packet carrying hashes, receipt classes, safe ids, response anatomy, freshness receipts, omission receipts, export policy, privacy scan, and ASK_TYPE_A handles instead of raw transcript bodies.
title: Operator thread continuation and Type B handoff shuttle
why_load_bearing: High-class Type B can help reason about continuation only if it receives enough structure to understand the episode without being handed private state or asked to hallucinate repo facts. This organ turns a private thread into a purpose-carrying shuttle artifact that Type B can reason over while Type A remains responsible for live substrate verification.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic prompt-shelf run, soft capture_diagnostic snapshots, Claude and Codex rollout JSONL fixtures, repo and non-repo cwd sessions, near-tie candidate sessions, partial soft source with operator suffix, and stale/missing projection status. Expected output includes strong/tentative/ambiguous bands, current_bindings only for primary bands, none-band dropped by default, diagnostics for unmatched captures, and HUD overlay fields that never replace response-state color authority.
failure_mode_prevented: Type B advice is consumed by Claude/Codex with no recoverable provenance; unrelated sessions are linked because cwd filtering is absent; partial soft captures are treated as complete operator deltas; near ties become false certainty; HUD surfaces render ambiguous links as confirmed; stale/missing projections require raw-text scans to inspect status.
notes: This is the actual provenance join between operator-mediated Type B reasoning and Type A execution, not a prompt-shelf reporting surface. It proves where handoffs likely landed and preserves uncertainty instead of forcing a single lineage.
one_line: A bounded joiner links observed Type B assistant turns to Type A Claude/Codex user inputs by merging curated prompt-shelf runs with soft capture diagnostics, filtering rollouts to the repo cwd, scoring pairs with containment, anchor, token overlap, and time coupling, then publishing confidence-banded typeb_to_typea edges, current bindings, diagnostics, status, and additive HUD linkage visuals.
title: Operator handoff linkage confidence-edge projector
why_load_bearing: The operator frequently pastes Type B reasoning into Type A execution surfaces. Without this organ, that handoff becomes invisible chat archaeology or a false-certainty manual claim. The projector creates auditable provenance edges while keeping raw assistant/user bodies behind private input readers and exposing only metadata/status to routine consumers.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic thread with repeated structural prompts and private sentinel text. Candidate packet classifies prompt labels/repeat levels, emits private_thread_prompt_ref with sha16 hashes and field_path, suggests Prompt Ledger/WorkItem/standard/skill routes, defaults to metadata_only with no sentinel/evidence_private_excerpt, includes explicit opt-in private excerpt mode, and check_privacy_boundary stays ok.
failure_mode_prevented: Repeated operator/Type B lessons vanish in browser history; private snippets leak into tracked learning surfaces; one-off Type B advice is promoted directly into standards or skills; prompt-shelf learning bypasses Prompt Ledger adoption state; agents cannot cite the private evidence handle later.
notes: Complements prompt_trace_adoption_state_machine: that row owns prompt-ledger adoption posture, while this organ is the private thread-memory miner that supplies candidate evidence without granting mutation authority.
one_line: Private operator thread memory mines repeated or structured prompt turns into metadata-only lesson candidate packets and an index that carry route contracts, evidence refs, hashes, owner-surface candidates, confidence, do-not-assimilate risks, and required Type A verification while mutation_allowed remains false and Prompt Ledger adoption is the first point-of-use surface.
title: Operator thread lesson-candidate advisory gate
why_load_bearing: Operator/browser threads are rich learning evidence, but direct assimilation would leak private text or let a single Type B interaction mutate doctrine. This organ converts thread-local signal into advisory, route-bound candidates that can enter Prompt Ledger and Task Ledger workflows only after evidence-gated Type A verification.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic CDP event stream: requestWillBeSent to /backend-api/conversation starts candidate only; responseReceived or dataReceived confirms open generation and mints pending gold; loadingFinished turns the same attempt completed and blue; sentinel/ping and /prepare requests are ignored; a flight started before the latest copy ack cannot remint unseen state.
failure_mode_prevented: A hidden stale stop button or unrelated network call makes the system think a new answer is pending; completed old responses become fresh unseen work after the operator has copied/acknowledged them; background telemetry endpoints pollute response state; response attempts disappear across watch ticks.
notes: Distinct from the response-attention lease: this row is the attempt-evidence collector. The lease consumes it but also consumes DOM, action, and runtime-favicon evidence.
one_line: The ChatGPT HUD observer listens to CDP network events and records generation-attempt rows keyed by target/request/conversation, separating candidate request starts from confirmed generation streams and completed/failed attempts before those signals are allowed to mint response-attention obligations.
title: Operator response flight attempt ledger
why_load_bearing: DOM stop buttons and hidden-tab status are noisy. This organ gives the operator bridge a second, lower-level evidence channel for 'a response attempt actually exists' and prevents background pings, prepare calls, stale completed requests, or pre-ack flights from reminting pending state. It turns volatile browser network events into a bounded ledger that downstream attention logic can cite by attempt id.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic multi-tab sequence: composer send creates pending lease and gold outline; hidden weak idle scan cannot clear it; confirmed flight completion turns it completed_unseen with blue outline; copy_all_responses or mark_response_seen clears to seen; old copy receipts cannot clear newer pending; retired seen leases block stale remint but allow a true new send or high-confidence active visible birth.
failure_mode_prevented: A response completes in a background tab and is never noticed; a stale hidden stop button creates false pending work; a weak scan clears pending before the operator sees/copies it; a prior copy receipt clears a newer response; watcher restarts lose obligation state.
notes: The lease is the behavioral organ above response flight: it stores obligation truth and action/visual consequences, not just browser observations.
one_line: The observer persists per-conversation response-attention leases with pending, completed_unseen, and seen states, transition and obligation logs, evidence-strength classes, ack/copy clear authority, pending-lineage preservation, and a final visual veto that keeps gold/blue attention visible until a real acknowledgment or completion boundary exists.
title: Operator response-attention obligation lease
why_load_bearing: The operator is managing many ChatGPT tabs; losing which tab has a pending or unseen answer is a direct control failure. The lease makes attention state durable across watcher restarts, hidden tabs, weak idle scans, stale DOM controls, copy receipts, active-tab dwell, and network flight completion. It prevents weak observations from clearing obligations while still allowing authoritative ack/copy or new-send evidence to move the state forward.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic HUD queue with copy_all_responses, copy_type_b_handoff_packet, copy_constitution_packet, mark_response_seen, mark_response_pending, and duplicate actions. The watcher overwrites browser clipboard success with authoritative pbcopy where appropriate, requires extraction/proof fields for response copies, writes metadata-only jsonl rows without clipboard_text/private body, reports response_copy_proof_missing when required fields are absent, and projects each action id once into AgentTrace.
failure_mode_prevented: A HUD click changes cognitive state without a disk receipt; browser-stale Type B handoff or primer packets remain authoritative after code changes; response-copy claims lack extraction proof; private clipboard text persists in hud_actions.jsonl or AgentTrace; duplicate action ids produce duplicate canonical events.
notes: Distinct from operator_bridge_tail_projector_privacy_cursor: that row is the consumer/tail projector. This organ is the producer-side receipt/proof lane inside the HUD observer.
one_line: HUD actions drain through a fast watcher lane that dedupes action ids, rewrites privileged copy artifacts through pbcopy when needed, annotates proof-governed actions with required evidence fields, strips body-bearing content from durable hud_actions.jsonl, and exposes health checks for unknown or proof-missing current actions.
title: Operator HUD action receipt proof lane
why_load_bearing: Operator clicks are state transitions in the cognitive substrate. Without durable, sanitized, proof-checked receipts, copy/ack/sidecar actions are invisible, stale client bundles can masquerade as authoritative, and private response bodies can leak into tracked telemetry. The lane converts UI clicks into auditable metadata-only events while preserving raw bodies only in explicit artifacts or volatile in-memory HUD state.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic trace-structurer capture containing a sidecar output packet: HUD shows a sidecar continuation candidate with authority limits and deep-attachment lineage; copy_sidecar_continuation_text records copied with proof_status complete; mark applied/rejected/stale/superseded records lifecycle receipts; the candidate projector replays hud_actions.jsonl and updates application_state only when hashes, turn index, source window, transport channel, and content-visibility strength match.
failure_mode_prevented: A copied sidecar candidate is assumed applied; stale or rejected continuation text remains visually ready; candidate state is updated by a receipt from a different response; raw trace/provider/live repo authority is implied by a sidecar text blob; agents lose why a continuation candidate was superseded.
notes: This is actual runtime continuity machinery, not prompt packaging: it controls how sidecar-generated continuation text becomes, or fails to become, a main Type A continuation.
one_line: Trace-structurer sidecar output can surface as a Type A continuation candidate, but it stays in a lifecycle-gated state machine: ready_to_apply -> copied/applied/rejected/superseded/stale, with lineage-matched HUD receipts, application targets, authority-limit fields, and proof-required sidecar hashes before any candidate is treated as applied.
title: Sidecar continuation candidate lifecycle gate
why_load_bearing: Sidecar continuation text is tempting to paste as the next Type A move. This organ prevents copy from being confused with application, records why a candidate was rejected/stale/superseded, preserves hashes and transport channel evidence, and keeps raw trace replay/live repo/provider JSONL authority outside the candidate's authority. It makes continuation handoff auditable instead of clipboard folklore.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic queued prompt launches a seed pass; the fixture opens a saved seed JSON, validates heartbeat, writes one proof receipt, rewrites reentry_prompt, and rejects an attempted cron/heartbeat interpretation unless the prompt explicitly requests scheduling.
failure_mode_prevented: Queued prompt text is treated as a scheduled automation; Task Ledger is treated as the prompt conveyor; a stale queued prompt stops the run before live seed authority is opened; mission proof stays in chat instead of disk.
notes: Mined from std_autonomous_seed_prompt.codex_app_mission_queue_boundary and the Type A receiver skill. This is the mission-conveyor membrane, not the generic autonomous-seed concept.
one_line: Codex app queued prompts are a host conveyor for seed-backed Type A mission passes, while mission truth lives in the saved seed bundle, Task Ledger residuals, Work Ledger coordination, proof artifacts, and seed rewrite fields.
title: Autonomous seed mission conveyor boundary
why_load_bearing: Without this boundary, agents misroute repeated queued prompts into cron, heartbeat, watcher, or detached automation surfaces and lose the disk-first seed authority that makes each pass recoverable. The boundary keeps host delivery, mission state, backlog, and live coordination as separate authorities.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic seed run with three cases: one valid durable owner-tool mutation, one invalid validation-only closeout, and one blocked closeout with explicit unsafe/claimed/source-write-blocked stop condition plus Task Ledger or Work Ledger receipt.
failure_mode_prevented: A repeated autonomous seed returns no-op, verification-only, generated-refresh-only, CAP-capture-only, or settlement-only closeouts and calls them success; the next pass repeats the same idle behavior.
notes: This row captures the hard no-null edit ban now carried by the execution skill and standard validation rules.
one_line: Every autonomous-seed pass must land at least one durable file edit or owner-tool mutation, record the owned path in changed_this_turn or proof_loop_report.owned_paths_mutated, and validate it; typed no-edit receipts are blocked/non-success unless every safe lane is genuinely unavailable.
title: Autonomous seed hard no-null edit contract
why_load_bearing: This turns autonomous seeds from repeated classification loops into substrate-changing missions. Without it, agents can spend queued passes proving green checks, settling ledgers, capturing CAPs, or writing prose while the seed teleology never advances.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic stale prompt names wave 001 while the live seed names wave 002; the receiver classifies prompt as transport evidence, opens the live seed, writes proof_loop_report.selected_seed_id, detection_evidence, live_authority_opened, mutation_landed_or_blocked, owned_paths_mutated, validation_refs, continuation_written, and next_run_first_decision.
failure_mode_prevented: A queued stale prompt causes a no-op; replayed prompt text overwrites live seed authority; a continuation receipt is emitted without mutation, validation, or next-run decision; context-pack misses the seed owner card.
notes: Mined from the current system_atlas_crystal_architecture_comprehension proof_loop_report and the replay/stale prompt standard contract.
one_line: A stale or replayed autonomous-seed prompt is treated as transport evidence, not mission authority; the receiver opens the live seed bundle, validates heartbeat, executes from current_focus and next_wave, and writes an autonomous_seed_replay_receipt_v0 proof_loop_report with owned paths, validation refs, continuation, and next first decision.
title: Autonomous seed live replay receipt
why_load_bearing: Repeated prompt queues and copy-pasted continuation text are useful only if they cannot freeze the mission at an old wave. The replay receipt proves that the receiver consumed live disk state, landed or blocked one mutation, and left the next run recoverable without chat memory.

candidate_fixture: Toy ledger with five rows, row-to-organ router, route card, fixture spec, decision matrix, DAG, and readiness audit; validator rejects individual_row_selection, missing fixture specs, unknown pattern refs, stale source hash, and hard_no_standalone rows marked selectable.
failure_mode_prevented: A future agent picks one impressive pattern_id as a leaf without opening route cards, fixture specs, readiness audit, dependency DAG, or anti-claims; source ledger hash drifts from the selector; public reconstruction treats macro rows as authority.
notes: This row captures the current pattern-ledger selection mechanism itself as a mined organ, making the ledger's anti-row-selection contract visible to future reconstruction passes.
one_line: Macro-side mined pattern rows are not selectable public leaves until a route-readiness audit proves organ membership, prerequisites, fixture contract, proof or validator boundary, anti-claim, and hard no-standalone exclusions across companion overlays.
title: Extracted-pattern organ readiness selector
why_load_bearing: The ledger is intentionally a candidate pool, so route existence can look like leaf readiness. This selector prevents isolated rows, private fixtures, release wrappers, or child suborgans from becoming public microcosms before an organ bundle and synthetic proof contract are present.

candidate_fixture: Toy ledger with strong, doctrine-only, code-without-standard, weak-ref, and private-fixture rows; validator classifies evidence classes, reports missing refs, refreshes binding and weak-binding sidecars, and emits a markdown coverage report with anti-claim that validation is not pattern authority.
failure_mode_prevented: Rows with impressive titles but missing concrete refs enter public selection; doctrine-only rows are mistaken for built organs; private fixture rows ship with live evidence; source-ref breakage is found only during release.
notes: This row captures the source-binding sidecar lane as an organ so future microcosm reconstruction can show how candidate pattern evidence is checked before projection.
one_line: The pattern ledger has a macro-side validator that reads ledger rows plus substrate-binding sidecars, recomputes evidence classes, validates concrete refs, emits weak-binding classes, and writes coverage reports without rebuilding or exposing the public microcosm.
title: Extracted-pattern substrate binding validation
why_load_bearing: Pattern rows are hypotheses until their source refs, standards, paper modules, validators, generated artifacts, and private-fixture posture are checked. This binding validator gives future reconstruction a trustable preselection substrate instead of relying on prose summaries or row titles.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic project with a recording_run_map, pattern narration, short montage sequence, one take with transcript/view_timeline/intent_events, and one take with a 0.2 second flash. The gate emits shoot_script.json, story_coverage_audit.json, intro_montage_plan.json, chapter_plan.json, rough_cut_plan.json, and youtube_package.json; it marks the flash unusable, excludes mark_private and mark_retake spans, computes coverage_percent, and refuses a passing story_package_status when coverage fails.
failure_mode_prevented: A demo recording is treated as publishable because it contains impressive concept mentions; micro-flashes count as coverage; private or retake spans enter the rough cut; YouTube chapter constraints fail only after render; the next recording gap is left in chat instead of a typed artifact.
notes: This is the corrected product gate above scene planning: generated animations are optional support, while frontend footage, coverage, chapters, and public-safe delivery determine whether the recording is usable.
one_line: The demo-take story package converts planned recording intent plus recorded take evidence into a YouTube-oriented product gate: shoot script, coverage audit, intro montage plan, chapter map, rough cut plan, and upload package, with coverage truthfulness and public-safe constraints before render.
title: Demo-take story package product gate
why_load_bearing: The public dissemination path can fail by optimizing for concept mentions or nice footage while missing the actual video structure. This pattern makes the product question explicit: did the recording cover the planned beats, which clips are usable, what should be recorded next, and can the result become a watchable cut without leaking private or retake spans.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic take with transcript, view_timeline, intent_events, extracted pattern ledger, paper module index, and storyboard output. The planner matches route-plane and projection phrases, selects terminal_proof for kernel/preflight language, inserts chapter cards, excludes mark_private and mark_retake spans, writes storyboard.html without absolute host paths, and emits an audit receipt with all concept refs resolved.
failure_mode_prevented: Renderer code silently invents scenes; generated visuals are selected without resolvable substrate refs; private/retake narration is rendered; storyboard leaks absolute host paths; kernel/terminal proof segments are flattened into generic screen footage.
notes: This row captures the typed edit-decision layer, not the renderer. It is the membrane that turns narration plus screen context into reviewable scene intent.
one_line: A demo-take scene plan binds transcript spans, active view context, matched substrate concepts, operator intent events, and visual_lane choices into an audited editorial plan before any video renderer is allowed to invent or render scenes.
title: Demo-take scene-plan visual lane gate
why_load_bearing: The recording path needs an edit-decision layer between raw take packages and video output. This gate lets Type A and future editor surfaces inspect why a segment is screen footage, terminal proof, split screen, generated animation, evidence card, chapter card, operator physical, or excluded, while preserving public-safe and no-host-path constraints.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic take with two usable view episodes, one 0.2 second flash, one retake span, one private span, markers, intent events, transcript words, visual frames, and EDL. The index emits view_episodes.json, ui_delta_index.json, candidate_clips.json, and multimodal_index.json; rejects retake/private clips as public_safe=false; classifies the flash as montage_flash; records route_change, marker, intent_event, and frame_keyframe events; and references sibling sidecars rather than embedding copies.
failure_mode_prevented: 0.2 second route flashes count as usable coverage; private/retake spans disappear instead of becoming explicit reject clips; story audits duplicate raw-span scoring; every frame/word is persisted into an oversized index; downstream assemblers embed sidecars instead of referencing them.
notes: This is the edit graph between raw demo-take package state and story-package assembly. It should stay rebuildable from the take package, not become hand-edited editorial truth.
one_line: A loose recording take is indexed into a queryable edit graph of view episodes, sparse UI delta events, candidate clips, and a multimodal rollup that downstream story-package assembly prefers over ad hoc raw-span scoring.
title: Demo-take multimodal edit graph
why_load_bearing: Raw recordings combine screen tracks, transcript words, route changes, markers, intent commands, sampled frames, and EDL rows. Without a typed edit graph, coverage audits repeatedly rescore raw spans and confuse flashes, retakes, private sections, hooks, outros, and usable explanations. The graph promotes evidence into stable sidecars while keeping them rebuildable projections.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic scene plan with chapter card, screen footage, generated animation, and projection compression lanes plus tiny placeholder mic/screen tracks. The adapter mirrors assets under remotion/public/take-assets, emits composition.json with ScreenClip, PaperModuleCard, ProjectionCompression, and ChapterCard components, writes a render receipt with mixed_lane_status, audio/video-bound scene counts, render_status, and story_package_status, and fails story_package_status independently of MP4 encoding when coverage/youtube package gates fail.
failure_mode_prevented: A renderer invents or reorders scenes; take media leaks through absolute paths instead of static refs; a successful mp4 encode is treated as story approval; generated visuals ship without audio binding; mixed screen/generated/audio composition is untested until the final render.
notes: This row is the adapter and receipt boundary. It is not a claim that the Remotion final render is public-ready without the story-package and release gates.
one_line: The demo-take composer translates audited scene plans or story-package rough cuts into Remotion composition props, mirrors take media into the renderer's static asset boundary, writes render receipts, and separates structural render_status from story_package_status.
title: Demo-take Remotion composition adapter
why_load_bearing: Rendering is a dangerous authority boundary: a valid MP4 can still be an invalid story package, and generated visuals can hide missing screen/audio bindings. This adapter makes render inputs inspectable and receipt-backed while preserving the rule that planners decide scenes and renderers only consume declared scenes.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic Codex turn with repeated pytest command failing then passing, plus py_compile passing; the capsule text reports observed_checks pass=2 fail=1 total=3, terminal_checks pass=2 fail=0 total=2, final_validation=passed, and metadata terminal_validation_count=2. A second fixture makes the terminal row fail and leaves final_validation=needs_review.
failure_mode_prevented: A final passing validation is reported as needs_review because an earlier retry failed; a terminal failing retry is hidden by an earlier pass; private prompt/tool output is copied wholesale into a capsule; trace review requires expanding live TUI command cards.
notes: This row captures a narrow but important receipt semantics upgrade: terminal validation identity, not raw failure volume, governs closeout validation while keeping failed attempts visible.
one_line: The CLI prompt trace capsule renders a provider-agnostic per-prompt tool trace with redacted/truncated command evidence, deduplicated terminal validation rows, and final_validation derived from the last result for each validation command identity rather than every historical failed attempt.
title: CLI prompt-trace terminal validation capsule
why_load_bearing: Agents often rerun the same test until it passes. A trace capsule that counts every failed retry as final truth misreports the closeout, while one that ignores failures hides the debugging path. Terminal validation rows preserve the observed attempts and compute final_validation from the terminal row per check, giving reviewers a compact but faithful command-level receipt.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic Task Ledger with a terminal high-pressure row, a high-rank low-pressure schedulable row, a lower-rank high-downstream-pressure schedulable row, a globally hidden pressure row, and a dependency-blocked row. The priority constellation emits top_schedulable_workitem, top_schedulable_unlock_pressure_workitems, top_global_unlock_pressure_workitems, top_dependency_blocked_workitems, dynamic_focus, and selector_explanation; entry admission preserves the four lanes under budget and terminal rows remain evidence only.
failure_mode_prevented: A terminal row wins startup pressure because it has downstream edges; a hidden global unlock row is mistaken for schedulable next action; dependency-blocked work is presented as immediately executable; dormant phase state hides real WorkItem pressure.
notes: Mined from the scheduler-grade startup pressure wave. It is a public-safe scheduling pattern when replayed against synthetic WorkItem rows, but live Task Ledger row contents stay private authority.
one_line: Startup, pulse, preflight, and entry surfaces preserve four WorkItem pressure lanes instead of one fake top task: schedulable_now, schedulable_unlock_pressure, global_unlock_pressure, and dependency_blocked.
title: Task Ledger startup pressure lane split
why_load_bearing: A dormant runtime can still have schedulable work, hidden unlock pressure, and blocked dependency pressure. Collapsing those into one top row lets terminal rows or globally important but unschedulable rows distort the next action. The lane split keeps readiness, pressure, and blockage separate while still giving cold agents a ranked path.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic station census with a passing Station monitor row, rootNavigator and graph geometry capture gaps, one partial unmeasured row, and one requested view missing from the census. The action map contains exactly one action row per census or missing-view row; missing rows route to census_binding_gap/add_to_census_or_bind_capture, partial rows route to contract markers, calibrated-watch rows route to resolution, calibrated-pass rows route to regression guards, and hot_action_rollup includes only score>=80 non-monitor resolution rows.
failure_mode_prevented: Only hot views appear in preflight; missing requested views have no action; calibrated-pass rows create false pressure; monitor or accepted-difference rows crowd out resolution work; partial measurement debt remains prose instead of a governed next action.
notes: Mined from the all-view quality action-map wave. It should be replayed with synthetic view rows and screenshots, not with private live operator view state.
one_line: The frontend-wide view quality census emits a governed action row for every census or missing-view row, then derives hot-action preflight as a ranked projection over that full map rather than as the universe of quality work.
title: View Quality all-view action map
why_load_bearing: Frontend quality loops often overfocus on the hottest broken views and lose the long tail of unmeasured, partial, calibrated-watch, and calibrated-pass rows. This pattern turns every observed or expected view into an explicit next action while keeping the hot rollup as a projection with exclusion rules, not the authority surface.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic Task Ledger with one schedulable WorkItem, one Work Ledger claim packet, and stale phase freshness. A selection-only entrypoint returns task_ledger_source=materialized_projection, live_work_runtime_attention.authority_order=[Task Ledger WorkItems, Work Ledger claims, phase runtime context], phase_context_role=context_only_not_liveness_authority, and recommended_workitem_ids. A target-aware dirty-path call switches to authority replay and keeps the same order.
failure_mode_prevented: A stale phase or subphase pointer is treated as the next-action authority; selection-only entrypoint work needlessly replays the full Task Ledger; a target-aware mutation reads only a materialized projection; agents inspect phase files before live WorkItem and Work Ledger pressure.
notes: Mined from the WorkItem entrypoint live-work-first wave. This is an entry read-model contract, not permission to expose live Task Ledger row bodies or skip authority replay for mutation. Source commits reviewed: 67b09aa94b15.
one_line: The WorkItem runtime entrypoint defaults selection-only calls to materialized Task Ledger projections and emits live_work_runtime_attention with authority order Task Ledger WorkItems, Work Ledger claims, then phase runtime context; target-aware mutation replays authority events instead.
title: WorkItem entrypoint live-work authority order
why_load_bearing: Cold agents need the current work menu without treating phase or wave files as liveness authority, and mutation lanes still need source-authority replay when exact paths or exclusive claims are involved. This pattern makes the authority order explicit and prevents stale phase context from overriding live WorkItem and claim state.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic recommended_next_workitems with long source_event_ids, dependency edge lists, downstream unlock ids, and pressure data. The entrypoint emits compact_live_workitem_card rows, truncates why_this_next to four reasons, converts dependency lists to counts, includes recommended_workitems_projection.omitted, and points full_detail_sources back to synthetic execution_menu_schedulable and schedulable_by_rank fixtures.
failure_mode_prevented: A first-contact entry packet embeds full source event ids or dependency edge bodies; agents lose why-this-next actionability after aggressive trimming; edge lists leak private backlog topology; context budget is spent on raw WorkItem rows instead of a claimable work menu.
notes: Mined from the compact live-work WorkItem entry-card wave. It is a first-contact payload membrane over WorkItem projections, not a new Task Ledger authority store. Source commits reviewed: d555435c2b1f.
one_line: Live WorkItem entry cards preserve action fields and bounded pressure/dependency counts while explicitly omitting source event ids, dependency edge lists, and downstream-unlock detail from first-contact entry payloads.
title: Live WorkItem card compaction gate
why_load_bearing: Entry surfaces need enough WorkItem detail to choose and claim work, but full Task Ledger rows can leak private backlog structure and blow context budgets. The compact card gate preserves id, title, state, rank, work type, required next event, bounded why-this-next, dependency counts, and pressure scalars while recording the omitted detail sources.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic kernel phase packet with active wave, focus conflict count, one execution_menu_id, and Work Ledger claim counts. The WorkItem entrypoint emits phase_runtime_context.view_profile=phase_runtime_context_v0, legacy_projection_id=subphase_runtime_attention, liveness_authority=false, preferred_authority_order=[Task Ledger WorkItems, Work Ledger claims, phase runtime context], plus a subphase_runtime_attention payload marked successor_projection=phase_runtime_context and a projection affordance marked legacy_alias_of_phase_runtime_context.
failure_mode_prevented: A legacy subphase projection keeps accidental authority after a successor exists; phase freshness warnings are conflated with Work Ledger blockers; agents choose work from phase/wave files before live Task Ledger and Work Ledger projections; old consumers silently receive a renamed payload without compatibility semantics.
notes: Mined from the phase-runtime-context preference wave. It should be projected with synthetic phase/wave packets only; real live phase runtime state remains private runtime context. Source commits reviewed: 0f2206c647a3.
one_line: The WorkItem entrypoint emits phase_runtime_context as the preferred phase/wave context projection and keeps subphase_runtime_attention as a typed legacy alias with liveness_authority=false and compatibility_status=legacy_alias_of_phase_runtime_context.
title: Phase runtime context legacy-alias boundary
why_load_bearing: Renaming a projection is not cosmetic here: older subphase language made phase/wave context look like current-work authority. The successor projection preserves compatibility while marking context_role, preferred_authority_order, legacy_projection_id, and no-liveness authority so new routing reads phase context after live WorkItem selection.

candidate_fixture: Second-wave compound fixture over the accepted public-root trio: formal_math_readiness_gate, formal_math_premise_retrieval, and formal_math_lean_proof_witness. It should stitch one solved toy Lean theorem, one missing-corpus prerequisite, one premise-retrieval miss, one tactic-affordance miss, one verifier rejection, and the Lean-accepted/advisory provider-hypothesis review boundary into a single receipt that emits corpus_readiness, allowed_premise_index, provider_context_recipe, tactic_portfolio_probe, target_shape_route, seven-class lean_verifier_feedback, proofline evidence cells, and public_safe_readiness without exporting proof bodies or private source text.
failure_mode_prevented: A public demo claims theorem-proving capability from a passing proof body while hiding corpus absence, unsupported tactic availability, premise retrieval failure, provider leakage, or untyped verifier feedback.
notes: Demand rank 1 after the 2026-05-21 compound-priority pass. Partial means the public root now has three present spine pieces (readiness gate, premise-retrieval gate, bounded Lean/Lake witness), not that the full compound fixture has shipped. Lean-accepted provider proof hypotheses remain reviewed_advisory_not_promoted evidence; that membrane is part of the flagship claim, not a gap to paper over.
one_line: A public-safe formal math tranche combines corpus readiness, Lean/Std premise indexing, provider context budgeting, tactic availability, target-shape routing, verifier failure taxonomy, body-safe proofline evidence, and the now-present bounded Lean/Lake witness into one replayable research-engine organ.
title: Formal math readiness proof-spine compound
why_load_bearing: This is still the highest-signal public microcosm candidate because it turns a frontier AI theme into verifiable execution: readiness gates, sanctioned premise retrieval, and a local Lean/Lake witness are already present, while the integrated compound fixture still withholds private proof bodies, provider payloads, Mathlib authority, and benchmark claims. The impressive part is the honesty of the proof spine: every failure class has to be typed before the public claim can advance.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic Codex session with one correct kernel route, one skipped route, one late grep, one repeated validation command, one harness mismatch, one claim-before-mutation event, and one capture-before-prose event. The organ emits redacted trace capsule rows, agent_route_compliance_audit findings, session attribution, harness configuration deltas, Work Ledger claim receipts, Task Ledger capture receipts, route_intervention hint coverage, navigation_training_emissions, and a debt-retirement receipt showing which future bootstrap or skill surface consumed the lesson.
failure_mode_prevented: Agent trace review stays as a passive log; route hints are generated but never attached to the next entry surface; harness mismatch is misread as model weakness; private raw session bodies are exported as proof.
notes: Demand rank 2. Current AI infrastructure signal favors traces, spans, evals, durable execution, and production debugging; this row is the system-local version because it closes the loop from observed agent behavior into route repair. It remains absent until a synthetic trace-to-repair fixture proves consumption by a future entry surface.
one_line: A synthetic agent-session tranche links prompt/tool traces, route-compliance audit, session attribution, harness configuration, route intervention hooks, and navigation-debt retirement into a closed trace-to-repair loop.
title: Agent trace-to-route repair observability compound
why_load_bearing: Agent observability is impressive only when it changes future behavior. This compound turns traces into route audits, audits into typed repair hints, hints into route intervention coverage, and repeated anti-patterns into governed debt retirement. The demand-weighted version adds claim/capture evidence: the public fixture should prove that route compliance, Work Ledger claims, and Task Ledger capture-before-prose become future-entry control signals without exporting private session bodies.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic WorkItem with two owned paths, one unrelated dirty path, one interrupted bridge handoff, one validation command, one protected git-metadata commit blocker, and one append-exempt Work Ledger finalization. The organ emits compact_live_workitem_card, work_landing_attempt_binding, mission_transaction_preflight, scoped_commit decision table, continuation_packet_resume, validation receipt, commit_blocker_status, append_exempt_closeout, and work_landing_reconcile_finalizer_plan while proving unrelated dirt is neither staged nor reverted.
failure_mode_prevented: A demo agent edits without a claim, freezes because the tree is dirty, loses progress across a handoff, stages unrelated paths, or reports completion despite an unbound commit blocker.
notes: Demand rank 3. Long-horizon agent credibility is in demand, especially around durable execution and sandboxed work, but this row is less immediately attention-grabbing than Lean verification or trace-to-repair. Keep it as a synthetic replay of transaction discipline, scoped claims, and honest commit/blocker binding.
one_line: A long-horizon agent-work tranche combines live WorkItem authority order, compact entry cards, Work Landing transactions, scoped commit preflight, continuation resume, and finalizer reconciliation into one replayable completion system.
title: Durable agent work-landing replay compound
why_load_bearing: The most credible agent demo is not another task list; it is a durable work loop that can start from live authority, claim scoped paths, survive interruption, validate output, handle commit status honestly, and land or reconcile residuals without losing private backlog detail. Demand-weighted, this is the substrate counterpart to durable agent execution: progress must be replayable from receipts, not from chat memory.
