candidate_fixture: Synthetic lab-oracle packet with two CP1 branches, one CP2 invalid universe target, one equity-lane confirmation requirement, one deterministic oracle diff, one low-authority evidence decoy, and one evolve mutation that rewrites a dossier claim only after hierarchy resolution. The receipt emits reconciliation rows, invalid_target_reason, equity_confirmation_status, oracle_diff_grade, mutation_delta, authority_resolution_order, and public_safe_claim_boundary.
failure_mode_prevented: A public prediction demo scores out-of-universe targets, mutates dossiers from low-authority evidence, treats equity-lane confirmation as optional, or leaks private market reasoning while claiming scientific rigor.
notes: Demand rank 4. AI-for-science and forecasting are attention-grabbing, but this ledger row is boundary-heavy by design: schema-only, synthetic-only, no live market claims, no private forecast state, and no public prediction authority until a constrained science fixture exists.
one_line: A public-safe prediction tranche combines CP1 bifurcation reconciliation, CP2 universe constraints, four-tier epistemic resolution, deterministic equity diff grading, and evolve-to-dossier mutation into a synthetic scientific-forecasting loop.
title: Synthetic science oracle-dossier reconciliation compound
why_load_bearing: Scientific reasoning and forecasting are attention-grabbing when the system can show how hypotheses, constraints, oracle evidence, and dossier mutation reconcile under deterministic grading. Demand-weighted, this remains a powerful fourth-place lane because the public claim is fragile: it must demonstrate constrained reconciliation on synthetic data and must not present private market state or live forecasts as public truth.

candidate_fixture: First-wave fixture with projection_protocol, cleaning_policy, import_plan, five negative cases, and exported runtime bundle. The accepted receipt records projection_cell_count=3, source_ref_count=3, public_replacement_ref_count=4, validation_ref_count=5, all expected negative cases observed, body_redacted=true, private_state_scan.status=pass, and next_best_lane=real_substrate_import_tranche.
failure_mode_prevented: A future public Microcosm tranche copies raw seed, provider payloads, operator context, or private source bodies; treats path refs as source authority; omits private material without receipts; imports impressive macro rows without validation refs; or claims release/private-root equivalence from a projection packet.
notes: Added from newly present public-root substrate. This is not a future-menu absence row; it is a current accepted organ that makes future high-novelty imports safer, especially formal_math and route-observability tranches.
one_line: The public Microcosm root now has an accepted macro-projection import organ that turns private-root intelligence into path-level source refs, public replacement refs, omission receipts, validation refs, authority ceilings, and negative-case receipts instead of copying private bodies or upgrading authority.
title: Macro projection import protocol public membrane
why_load_bearing: The public microcosm needs a way to import macro substrate without becoming a private-root dump or a release claim. This organ is the membrane: it preserves lineage and public replacement shape, rejects private-body import, rejects missing omission receipts, rejects validation-free cells, and blocks release/private-equivalence overclaims. It is the prerequisite for growing impressive public cells from macro patterns safely.

candidate_fixture: First-wave reveal fixture with reveal_walkthrough, substrate_evidence_map, audience_claim_floor, four negative cases, and exported runtime bundle. The accepted receipt records time_budget_minutes=10, step_count=5, command_count=7, evidence_ref_count=10, substrate_ref_count=12, all expected negative cases observed, body_redacted=true, private_state_scan.status=pass, and public_claim='Microcosm turns a repo into a local operating substrate: patterns, routes, work transactions, events, evidence, and explanations.'
failure_mode_prevented: A public reveal reads like marketing instead of a runnable local loop; a reader must know private Atlas context before inspecting patterns/routes/evidence; release or hosting readiness is implied by local receipts; public material claims private-root equivalence; or evidence is hidden behind raw JSON drilldown without a board-level route.
notes: Added from newly present public-root substrate. This row is a current accepted organ, not a release authorization. It turns the most attention-grabbing cells into a bounded public walkthrough only after command/evidence backing.
one_line: The public Microcosm root now has an accepted reveal-walkthrough organ that validates a ten-minute cold-reader path through commands, substrate refs, evidence refs, public claim floor, authority ceiling, and negative cases before any public reveal can sound like marketing.
title: Public reveal walkthrough command/evidence gate
why_load_bearing: An impressive public demo has to be executable and bounded. This organ ties the claim to commands and receipts, proves the route is inspectable without private knowledge, and rejects release/hosting overclaim, private-equivalence overclaim, missing evidence routes, and marketing-only material. It is the product-facing counterpart to the macro import membrane.

candidate_fixture: First-wave fixture with public corpus_readiness, tactic_portfolio_availability, premise_index, target_shape_tactic_routing, provider_context_recipes, and five negative cases. The accepted receipt records observed errors for Mathlib overclaim, unprobed tactic availability, proof-body premise leakage, unavailable tactic routing, and provider recipe overclaim; body_redacted=true, private_state_scan.status=pass, and authority_ceiling blocks Lean/Lake execution, proof authority, provider calls, and Mathlib presence claims.
failure_mode_prevented: A public formal-math demo claims Lean/Mathlib/provider proof capability without proving corpus readiness, tactic availability, premise hygiene, target-shape route constraints, provider context budget, or authority ceiling first.
notes: Added from accepted public-root substrate after external demand review: formal proof automation is attention-grabbing, but the public-safe move is a readiness/authority boundary, not a theorem-proving overclaim. This row is current organ evidence, not release authorization or Lean witness authority.
one_line: The public Microcosm root has an accepted formal-math readiness organ that validates corpus availability, tactic probes, premise-index hygiene, target-shape routing, and provider context budgets as metadata before any Lean or proof claim is allowed.
title: Formal math readiness gate public boundary
why_load_bearing: This is the practical front door for the flagship formal-math tranche: it makes the impressive Lean story credible by proving what the environment may and may not claim first. It rejects Mathlib overclaims, unprobed tactics, proof-bearing premise rows, unavailable tactic routes, and provider recipes that smuggle proof bodies or exceed public budgets.

candidate_fixture: First-wave fixture with projection_protocol, synthetic Lean/Std premise_index, retrieval_queries, context_recipes, strategy_cases, and five negative cases. The accepted receipt records errors for proof-body premise leakage, oracle-id query leakage, test split tuning, context-budget overflow, and unknown strategy id; body_redacted=true, private_state_scan.status=pass, and authority_ceiling blocks Lean/Lake execution, proof bodies, oracle ids in public inputs, provider calls, release, and theorem-correctness claims.
failure_mode_prevented: A public theorem-proving demo presents premise retrieval as successful without showing split discipline, oracle-id hiding, budget enforcement, strategy-id validation, or proof-body redaction.
notes: Added from accepted public-root substrate after external demand review. Formal-math attention is strongest when the system exposes a reproducible retrieval/evaluation mechanism, not a raw proof claim. This row remains synthetic, redacted, and below proof authority.
one_line: The public Microcosm root has an accepted premise-retrieval organ that turns Lean/Std premise metadata, query term scoring, split eligibility, context budgets, and public strategy ids into a redacted synthetic evaluation loop below proof authority.
title: Formal math premise retrieval public eval gate
why_load_bearing: This is the first formal-math row that looks like a real research engine rather than readiness paperwork: it measures retrieval behavior, strategy gating, split discipline, and budget control while keeping oracle-needed premise ids and proof bodies out of public inputs. It complements the readiness gate with an evaluable mechanism.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic multi-tab fixture: a composer send records a response flight candidate; confirmed CDP generation mints a pending gold attention lease; completion turns it completed_unseen blue; metadata-only HUD copy or mark-seen action clears it with proof fields; a sidecar continuation candidate can be copied without being marked applied; an approval decision resumes the run. Negative cases reject stale pre-ack flights, weak hidden-tab clears, missing copy proof, duplicate action ids, private body leakage, and sidecar text treated as applied without lineage match.
failure_mode_prevented: A long-running agent waits on a human without a durable approval record; a completed response in another tab is missed; a stale hidden stop button remints pending state; copied sidecar text is mistaken for applied work; private response bodies leak through telemetry; duplicate HUD action ids corrupt trace replay.
notes: This row is intentionally a folded compound, not a standalone browser/HUD leaf. It should project only as synthetic receipt fixtures under agent_route_observability_runtime, external_boundary_anti_corruption_runtime, and bridge_phase_continuity_runtime.
one_line: A synthetic operator-response loop combines CDP flight attempts, durable response-attention leases, metadata-only HUD action receipts, sidecar continuation lifecycle, and typed approval decisions into one auditable human-in-loop control organ.
title: Operator response-attention human-loop compound
why_load_bearing: This is the control-plane version of durable human attention: an agent run can pause for a person, prove what needs attention, resume from typed receipts, and avoid leaking response bodies. Without the compound, the component rows remain scattered across browser telemetry, HUD clicks, sidecar packets, and trace projection, so the public microcosm cannot show the nontrivial part: attention state as replayable substrate rather than chat folklore.

candidate_fixture: Synthetic three-attempt formal-math loop: attempt A misses a premise, attempt B selects an unavailable tactic, attempt C emits an invalid proof body. The fixture records redacted input hashes, public premise-query terms, tactic availability probe, Lean/verifier failure class, trace grade, repair action, failure-mode ledger append, curriculum/DAG delta, and cold rerun receipt. Negative cases reject proof-body leakage, oracle-needed premise ids in public inputs, trace grade without a trace, repair without verifier class, promotion without cold rerun, provider payload leakage, and human approval treated as proof correctness.
